abstract: Multiple electron and proton transfers in nanomaterials pose significant demands and challenges across various fields such as renewable energy, chemical processes, biological applications, and photophysics.

title: Biomaterial-based combinatorial approach of aescin-comprised zein-coated gelatin nanoparticles alleviates synovial inflammation in experimental inflammatory arthritis
abstract: A schematic representation of collagen-induced rheumatoiad arthritis (RA) before and after treatment.

title: A hidden phase uncovered by ultrafast carrier dynamics in thin Bi 2 O 2 Se
abstract: A systematic study of thickness- and fluence-dependent carrier dynamics reveals the hidden ferroelectric transition in CVD-grown Bi 2 O 2 Se thin films, offering potential applications in optoelectronic devices that benefit from this transition.

title: Organic iontronic memristors for artificial synapses and bionic neuromorphic computing
abstract: Organic iontronic memristors are promising for high-density data storage, artificial synapses, and neuromorphic computing. This review provides a comprehensive summary of their concept, classification, preparation, mechanism, and application.

title: Atomically thin iridium nanosheets for oxygen evolution electrocatalysis
abstract: A one-pot solvothermal synthesis route to prepare freestanding ultrathin Ir nanosheets with atomic thickness for oxygen evolution electrocatalysis was developed.

title: Metal-assisted core–shell plasmonic nanoparticles for small molecule biothiol analysis and enantioselective recognition
abstract: We present a metal ion-assisted surface-enhanced Raman scattering biosensor based on an amphiphilic polymer matrix (APM) for rapid and accurate identification of cysteine enantiomers ( l / d -Cys) in various food samples.

title: The controllable and efficient synthesis of two-dimensional metal–organic framework nanosheets for heterogeneous catalysis
abstract: 2D MOF nanosheets prepared using a high-gravity reactive precipitation method exhibit high catalytic activities toward Knoevenagel condensation and the glycolysis of poly(ethylene terephthalate).

title: A handheld fluorescent platform integrated with a Sm( iii )-CdTe quantum dot-based ratiometric nanoprobe for point-of-use determination of phosphate
abstract: Phosphate (Pi) is crucial for various physiological processes and aquatic environments, which emphasizes the need for a simple, on-site sensor to promptly detect Pi for human health and environmental conservation.

title: Enhanced thermoelectric performance of a wide-bandgap twisted heterostructure of graphene and boron nitride
abstract: The twisted structure of two-dimensional heterostructure improved thermoelectric properties by enhancing electrical properties and reducing thermal conductivity.

title: Chemical passivation of 2D transition metal dichalcogenides: strategies, mechanisms, and prospects for optoelectronic applications
abstract: An overview of the strategies, progress, mechanisms, and prospects of chemical treatments to passivate the defects and improve the optical and electronic properties of 2D transition metal dichalcogenides.

title: Time-gated multi-dimensional luminescence thermometry via carbon dots for precise temperature mobile sensing
abstract: A new luminescence thermometry method uses surface- engineered carbon dots with temperature-responsive phosphorescence. Activated by low-power LED and measured via smartphone, it shows 7.9% °C −1 sensitivity, surpassing existing CD-based thermometers.

title: Visualizing and quantifying dynamic cellular forces with photonic crystal hydrogels
abstract: A Photonic Crystal Force Microscopy (PCCFM) system, comprising a photonic crystal substrate (PCS) and a common microscope, enables real-time, high-throughput visualization and quantification of cellular forces.

title: Unravelling the nanoscale mechanism of polarization reversal in a Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 -based ferroelectric capacitor by vector piezoresponse force microscopy
abstract: The nanoscopic nature of polarization reversal in hafnium oxide thin films consists in a combination of 180°-flipping of the polarization vector and ferroelastic switching. The shear piezoelectric coefficient is twice the longitudinal coefficient.

title: Design of terahertz metamaterial absorbers with switchable absorption functions utilizing thermal and electrical dual-modulation strategies
abstract: An integrated terahertz metamaterial absorber, based on thermal and electrical dual-modulation strategies, with two different and tunable absorption functions is demonstrated.

title: Design of “green” plasmonic nanocomposites with multi-band blue emission for ultrafast laser hyperthermia
abstract: Plasmonic-fluorescent IV group semiconductor (Si, SiC, Ge) nanocomposites exhibit strong multi-band blue emission, accompanied with a hyperthermia modality. Laser ablation approaches control their performance and size-dependent chemical composition.

title: A novel GO hoisted SnO 2 –BiOBr bifunctional catalyst for the remediation of organic dyes under illumination by visible light and electrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: A novel GO-hoisted SnO 2 –BiOBr bifunctional catalyst for the remediation of organic dyes under illumination by visible light and electrocatalytic water splitting.

title: Thermal behavior of the dielectric response of composites based on poly(vinylidene fluoride) filled with two-dimensional V 2 CT x MXenes
abstract: In this study, two-dimensional V 2 CT x MXenes were prepared by an accessible and rapid method, which involved aluminothermic combustion synthesis of the V 2 AlC MAX phase and its further processing in an HCl/LiF mixture under hydrothermal conditions.

title: High-resolution imaging of 3D stray-field components with a Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticle sensor
abstract: An approach to image 3D stray-field components by using a Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticle sensor is provided. This technique offers a way to observe the out-of-plane and in-plane stray-field components, and paves a way for the development of spintronic devices.

title: Skyrmion lattice formation and destruction mechanisms probed with TR-SANS
abstract: Ordering kinetics of magnetic skyrmion lattices are investigated using time resolved small angle neutron scattering. Measurements reveal ordering occurs over 10–100 ms, which is unusually long for magnetic behavior.

title: A wafer scale thin film of ultra-small Sc 2 O 3 nanocrystals on a 2D COF with high rigidity
abstract: A method combining surfactant-assisted synthesis and laminar assembly polymerization is employed to fabricate a 2D Sc 2 O 3 /COF composite film at ambient temperature and pressure, achieving lateral infinitude, uniformity, and high mechanical strength.

title: Continuous production of bimetallic nanoparticles on carbon nanotubes based on 3D-printed microfluidics
abstract: We propose a novel method for high-yield and continuous production of Pt-Pd NPs on MWCNTs based on 3D-printed microfluidics. Well-controlled nanocomposites show excellent electrocatalytic activity in H 2 O 2 , superior to those prepared in batch methods.

title: Unlocking the full potential of citric acid-synthesized carbon dots as a supercapacitor electrode material via surface functionalization
abstract: Functionalization of green-emitting carbon dots with hyperbranched bis(methylol)propionic acid (bis-MPA) polyester hydroxyl polymers resulted in the successful passivation of surface defects and hence better performance in supercapacitors.

title: Recent progress of high-performance in-plane zinc ion hybrid micro-supercapacitors: design, achievements, and challenges
abstract: New concepts, strategies, and latest developments of in-plane Zn ion hybrid MSCs as well as key challenges and future directions have been highlighted, which provides new insights into several kinds of emerging miniaturized energy storage devices.

title: Nitric oxide releasing novel amino acid-derived polymeric nanotherapeutic with anti-inflammatory properties for rapid wound tissue regeneration
abstract: The prepared PNAG NPs loaded with SNPs induce cell migration, proliferation, and angiogenesis, thus reducing inflammation and accelerating in vivo wound healing.

title: Proceeding of catalytic water splitting on Cu/Ce@g-C 3 N 4 photocatalysts: an exceptional approach for sunlight-driven hydrogen generation
abstract: This study investigates the photocatalytic hydrogen evolution on Cu/Ce supported g-C 3 N 4 catalyst.

title: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) for photoelectrocatalytic (PEC) reducing carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) to hydrocarbon fuels
abstract: In this review, the recent progress on PEC reduction of CO 2 based on MOF materials is summarized. In addition, the challenges and future wave surrounding the development of robust PEC CO 2 systems based on MOF materials are discussed.

title: Magnetic-ferroelectric synergic control of multilevel conducting states in van der Waals multiferroic tunnel junctions towards in-memory computing
abstract: Multilevel nonvolatile storage and in-memory computing can be achieved simultaneously in one unit of the TiTe 2 /Mn 2 Se 3 /bilayer-In 2 S 3 /Mn 2 Se 3 /TiTe 2 multiferroic tunnel junction.

title: Immobilizing nanozymes on 3D-printed metal substrates for enhanced peroxidase-like activity and trace-level glucose detection
abstract: Development and synthesis of thermally treated metal organic framework-based nanozyme immobilised on 3D-printed metal substrate, which exhibited extraordinary peroxidase-like enzymatic activity and trace-level glucose detection in real samples.

title: Impact of catalyst support on water-assisted CO oxidation over PdO/MO 2 (M = Sn, Ti, and Si) catalysts: experimental and theoretical investigation
abstract: This study investigates the influence of different supports on PdO-based catalysts during CO oxidation, particularly under humid conditions.

title: An updated review of few-layer black phosphorus serving as a promising photocatalyst: synthesis, modification and applications
abstract: This study summarizes recent progress on FL-BP, focusing on strategies for improving stability and activity. The preparation, structure, and photocatalytic applications are also highlighted. Finally, prospects for future research are proposed.

title: Donor–acceptor type triphenylamine-based porous aromatic frameworks (TPA-PAFs) for photosynthesis of benzimidazoles
abstract: Two donor–acceptor type triphenylamine-based porous aromatic frameworks (TPA-PAFs) were constructed, which achieved the photocatalytic synthesis of benzimidazoles and their derivatives in high yields under mild conditions.

title: Ultrafast light-driven metasurfaces with an ultra-broadband frequency agile channel for sensing
abstract: In this paper, a novel compact biosensor is proposed with an ultrawide resonance frequency agile channel shifted from 0.82 to 1.85 THz, with a tuning functionality up to 55.7%, and sensitivity of 118 and 247 GHz/RIU at 0.82 and 1.85 THz.

title: Temperature-driven evolution of ceria–zirconia-supported AuPd and AuRu bimetallic catalysts under different atmospheres: insights from IL-STEM studies
abstract: An illustration of the impact of oxidizing atmospheres at high temperature and at room temperature on the structure of bimetallic AuPd-supported nanoparticles.

title: Manipulating ultrafast magnetization dynamics of ferromagnets using the odd–even layer dependence of two-dimensional transition metal di-chalcogenides
abstract: Odd–even WS 2 layer number dependent ultrafast demagnetization and damping are studied by varying the pump fluence.

title: An artificial aluminum–tin alloy layer on aluminum metal anodes for ultra-stable rechargeable aluminum-ion batteries
abstract: A novel alloy electrodeposition method in low-temperature molten salt is proposed to fabricate AlSn alloy layer on copper as anode for RAIBs, which successfully addresses the issues of dendrite formation and corrosion on anode/electrolyte interface.

title: Mono-, bi- and tri-metallic Fe-based platinum group metal-free electrocatalysts derived from phthalocyanine for oxygen reduction reaction in alkaline media
abstract: A detailed investigation into Fe-based catalysts derived from phthalocyanine with mono, bi, and tri-metallic compositions is presented, focusing on how their processing and structure affect their oxygen reduction reaction activity in alkaline media.

title: Bayesian optimization of glycopolymer structures for the interaction with cholera toxin B subunit
abstract: The optimal structure of synthetic glycopolymers for GM1 mimetics was determined through Bayesian optimization.

title: Construction of Z-scheme heterojunction interfacial charge transfer pathways in ZnIn 2 S 4 @NENU-5 for photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: In this study, a straightforward one-step hydrothermal method was employed to embed NENU-5 nano-octahedra (NOs) within ZnIn 2 S 4 nanoflowers (NFs), forming ZnIn 2 S 4 @NENU-5 heterostructures.

title: G 4 -Hemin-loaded 2D nanosheets for combined and targeted chemo-photodynamic cancer therapy
abstract: A supramolecular strategy for the fabrication of a PDT-active 2D nanosheet loaded with a POD-mimicking DNAzyme for the synergetic combination of PDT and CDT for targeted cancer therapy is reported.

title: An artificial layer enables in situ generation of a homogeneous inorganic/organic composite solid electrolyte interphase for stable lithium metal batteries
abstract: This study provides new insights into designing homogeneous solid electrolyte interphase layers for high-voltage Li metal batteries by adopting LiNO 3 and LiDFOB as additives within a porous PVDF–HFP polymer skeleton.

title: Broadband photodetectors from silane-passivated CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals by ultrasound-mediated synthesis
abstract: Large-scale sonochemical synthesis of passivated perovskite nanocrystals with high stability and outstanding optical properties enables the fabrication of efficient broadband photodetectors.

title: Flexible wearable piezoresistive physical sensors with photothermal conversion and self-cleaning functions for human motion monitoring
abstract: Superhydrophobic cotton fabrics were assembled into piezoresistive physical sensors with good photo-thermal properties, self-cleaning properties, and rapid response/recovery time for dynamic monitoring of human motion.

title: Simultaneous derivatization and exfoliation of a multilayered Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene into amorphous TiO 2 nanosheets for stable K-ion storage
abstract: A facile one-step route is proposed to synthesize a-2D-TiO 2 @C by simultaneous derivatization and exfoliation of multilayered Ti 3 C 2 T x . Due to structural and compositional synergies, a-2D-TiO 2 @C delivers good cycling stability in K-ion storage.

title: Immunomagnetic particles exhibiting programmable hierarchical flower-like nanostructures for enhanced separation of tumor cells
abstract: The synergetic effect of nano-sized petals and micro-sized particles in the hierarchical nanostructure of FIMPs enhance interaction between the cells and the materials, thus enabling CTC separation with high selectivity and high efficiency.

title: Nanocarbon-based sensors for the structural health monitoring of smart biocomposites
abstract: Smart biocomposites with integrated nano-carbons based sensors for structural health monitoring advances the real-world applications.

title: Multifunctional two-dimensional perovskite based solar cells for photodetectors and resistive switching
abstract: This investigation showcases the realization of multifunctional two-dimensional perovskite solar cells, incorporating transient light detection and resistive switching functions within a single device, achievable by facile external bias adjustments.

title: Clicking beyond suspensions: understanding thiol–ene chemistry on solid-supported MoS 2
abstract: The on-surface reaction of maleimides, which was previously reported to be effective on MoS 2 suspensions, is now revealed at the molecular level on bulk and molecular beam epitaxy-grown single-layer MoS 2 .

title: Solid-waste-recycled CuO/C 3 N 4 S-scheme heterojunctions for efficient photocatalytic antibiotic degradation
abstract: The S-scheme heterojunction formed by C 3 N 4 and CuO effectively improves the degradation and mineralization efficiency of tetracycline.

title: Biomass-derived substrate hydrogenation over rhodium nanoparticles supported on functionalized mesoporous silica
abstract: RhNPs on N-functionalized SBA-15 enable biomass transformation. An imidazolium-based ionic liquid improves catalytic performance by limiting Rh–silanol interactions, underscoring the importance of stabilizers in optimizing catalyst activity.

title: UV-modification of Ag nanoparticles on α-MoC x for interface polarization engineering in electromagnetic wave absorption
abstract: Based on bimetallic HZIF-ZnMo, a high-performance electromagnetic wave absorbing material with comprehensive conductivity loss, polarization loss and dielectric loss was obtained by graphiting and UV-modified silver nanoparticles.

title: Rapid microwave-assisted synthesis and characterization of a novel CuCoTe nanocomposite material for optoelectronic and dielectric applications
abstract: A ternary CuCoTe-based nanocomposite was synthesised using the rapid microwave technique by varying power and irradiation time. The obtained nanoparticles demonstrate better optical and dielectric properties suitable for optoelectronic applications.

title: MOF-derived scaffolds as electrode materials: a mini-review
abstract: Herein, the authors have focused on the recent development of MOF-derived scaffold electrodes using transition metal oxides, sulfides, selenides, tellurides, and their composites for supercapacitors.

title: Rebamipide nanocrystal with improved physicomechanical properties and its assessment through bio-mimicking 3D intestinal permeability model
abstract: This study investigated the development and evaluation of rebamipide nanocrystals (REB-NCs) a BCS class IV drug for enhancing solubility and permeability, and used as anti-ulcer medication.

title: Unveiling the potential of a BCN-biphenylene monolayer as a high-performance anode material for alkali metal ion batteries: a first-principles study
abstract: Schematic diagram of a battery using bpn-BCN monolayer as an anode material. The bpn-BCN monolayer exhibits high specific storage capacities for alkali metals, with values of 1057.33 mA h g −1 for Li, 647.27 mA h g −1 for Na, and 465.98 mA h g −1 for K.

title: Rapid continuous aqueous production of copper indium sulfide quantum dots via a microwave-assisted microfluidic technique
abstract: An easy-to-perform and organic-solvent-free reaction platform based on a microwave-assisted microfluidic technique is developed to fabricate copper indium sulfide (CIS) quantum dots (QDs) and CIS@ZnS QDs fast and continuously.

title: Blue ZnSeTe quantum dot light-emitting diodes with low efficiency roll-off enabled by an in situ hybridization of ZnMgO nanoparticles and amino alcohol molecules
abstract: In situ amino alcohol hybrid ZMO NPs have been developed, which not only reduces exciton quenching at the QDs/ETL interface, but also enhances electron injection. In the end, a peak EQE of 8.6% and an extremely low efficiency roll-off were achieved.

title: Freeze-crosslinking approach for preparing carboxymethyl cellulose nanofiber/zirconium hydrogels as fluoride adsorbents
abstract: An extremely simple and practical method for synthesizing tough carboxymethyl cellulose nonfiber/Zr hydrogels was developed.

title: Charge transfer in TiO 2 -based photocatalysis: fundamental mechanisms to material strategies
abstract: The review highlights charge transfer, improved photocatalytic performance, and possible photocatalysis schemes in TiO 2 -based composites. It also addresses perspectives and challenges in charge transfer mechanisms for TiO 2 -based photocatalysis.

title: Charge carrier recombination processes, intragap defect states, and photoluminescence mechanisms in stoichiometric and reduced TiO 2 brookite nanorods: an interpretation scheme through in situ photoluminescence excitation spectroscopy in controlled environment
abstract: A scheme for the main photophysical processes in brookite TiO 2 nanorods is derived by excitation-resolved PL spectroscopy in controlled gas environment, shedding light on their efficient hydrogen generation via alcohol photo-oxidation.

title: Giant intrinsic piezoelectricity in 2D hybrid organic–inorganic perovskites [C 6 H 11 NH 3 ] 2 MX 4 (M = Ge, Sn, Pb; X = Cl, Br, I)
abstract: 2D-piezoelectric materials are attractive for micro-electromechanical systems (MEMS), medical implants and wearable devices because of their numerous exceptional properties.

title: Electrochemical deposition of gold nanoparticles on carbon ultramicroelectrode arrays
abstract: Electrochemical deposition of gold nanoparticles on carbon ultramicroelectrode arrays by varying deposition potential, deposition time, and gold ion concentration to obtain controlled particle size, dispersion, and count.

title: Reformation of La 0.7 Sr 0.3 MnO 3 properties by using ZnO in La 0.7 Sr 0.3 MnO 3 -ZnO heterostructures grown on (001) oriented Si
abstract: In (001)Si/La 0.7 Sr 0.3 MnO 3 (LSMO)/ZnO, the ZnO changes linear d I /d V vs. V of LSMO to parabolic, modifies different interactions and conduction electron concentration of t 2g -state of Mn d-orbitals, and induces Fowler–Nordheim tunnelling around the T C .

title: Asymmetric porous hydrogel encapsulating vulcanized molecular brushes with anti-bacterial adhesion, anti-infection, and pro-healing properties towards infected wound treatment
abstract: VMB@APH has been successfully developed with the effective combination of anti-bacterial adhesion, anti-infection, and pro-healing properties, which is of great significance for promoting the healing of infected wounds.

title: Nickel as a modifier of calcium oxalate: an in situ liquid cell TEM investigation of nucleation and growth
abstract: The mechanism of nickel as an inhibitor of calcium oxalate crystallization was elucidated via real-time nanoscale liquid cell TEM imaging and confirmed via molecular dynamics simulations. These findings are of significance to kidney stone formation.

title: Photoelectrochemical transistors based on semiconducting polymers: an emerging technology for future bioelectronics
abstract: This mini-review focuses on organic photoelectrochemical transistors and summarizes their recent advances in operation mechanisms, device architectures, and applications in biomedical sciences, optoelectronics and sensor technologies.

title: Stabilizing tetragonal ZrO 2 nanocrystallites in solvothermal synthesis
abstract: Phase-pure tetragonal ZrO 2 nanoparticles have been prepared under simple solvothermal synthesis conditions using different types of alcohols as solvents and studied using in situ X-ray scattering.

title: Alternating vs. random amphiphilic polydisulfides: aggregation, enzyme activity inhibition and redox-responsive guest release
abstract: This paper reports an alternating copolymer (ACP) with a bio-reducible amphiphilic polydisulfide backbone and highlight the impact of the alternating monomer connectivity on the self-assembly and biological activities.

title: Characterisation of the morphology of surface-assembled Au nanoclusters on amorphous carbon
abstract: Au nanoparticles over 100 atoms in size formed via surface-growth of sputtered atoms on amorphous carbon are approximately spherical in morphology. The distribution of particle structural isomers was found to match those formed via gas-condensation.

title: Structural distortion-induced monoclinic sodium iron hexacyanoferrate as a high-performance electrode for rocking-chair desalination batteries
abstract: This study reveals that structure distortion induced monoclinic NaFeHCF with minimized defects enables stable, rapid and low energy consumption desalination in rocking-chair desalination battery.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic performance of SnS 2 under visible light irradiation: strategies and future perspectives
abstract: The improvement of SnS 2 photocatalyst can be conducted through structural modification, morphological manipulation, and heterojunction formation.

title: An enhanced SPR optical fiber biosensor using Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene/AuNPs for label-free and sensitive detection of human IgG
abstract: A high-performance label-free optical fiber biosensor developed for highly sensitive human IgG detection.

title: Sliding ferroelectricity and the moiré effect in Janus bilayer MoSSe
abstract: Simultaneous switches of out-of-plane and in-plane ferroelectric polarizations by sliding, as well as the moiré effect, are observed in Janus bilayer MoSSe.

title: A chromium cobaltite based ternary composite as an efficient electrode material for hybrid supercapacitors with theoretical investigation
abstract: The synergistic integration of PPY and AC with chromium cobaltite leads to the development of advanced electrode materials for hybrid supercapacitors.

title: Magnetic nanoparticles for magnetic particle imaging (MPI): design and applications
abstract: Magnetic particle imaging (MPI) is an advancing technique with significant potential for medical diagnostics and therapeutics. It detects magnetic signals from tracers with superior sensitivity and quantifiability than traditional methods.

title: Exploring the cellular antioxidant mechanism against cytotoxic silver nanoparticles: a Raman spectroscopic analysis
abstract: In-time Raman microspectroscopy investigation of intracellular molecular variations revealed the activation of antioxidant mechanisms during the first 24 h of exposure to Silver Nanoparticles (AgNPs).

title: Silver-coated hollow fiber surface plasmon resonance sensor for glucose detection with enhanced limit of detection
abstract: In this study, we proposed and demonstrated a surface plasmon resonance (SPR) biosensor based on a silver coated hollow fiber (HF) structure for sensitive and stable low-concentration glucose detection.

title: An in situ exsolved Cu-based electrocatalyst from an intermetallic Cu 5 Si compound for efficient CH 4 electrosynthesis
abstract: An in situ exsolved Cu-based electrocatalyst is developed and exhibits high activity and selectivity towards CH 4 production from the reduction of CO 2 in a flow cell.

title: Large polarons in two-dimensional fullerene networks: the crucial role of anisotropy in charge transport
abstract: The recent synthesis of a two-dimensional quasi-hexagonal-phase monolayer network of C 60 molecules, known as qHPC 60 , holds significant promise for future semiconductor applications.

title: Recent advances in biomaterial designs for assisting CAR-T cell therapy towards potential solid tumor treatment
abstract: Advances in functional biomaterial designs for assisting CAR-T therapy against solid tumors.

title: Nanostructured electrocatalysts for organic synthetic transformations
abstract: This minireview aims at highlighting the most recent advances in the production of electrocatalytically active nanomaterials and their applications in organic catalysis.

title: Room temperature ferroelectricity and an electrically tunable Berry curvature dipole in III–V monolayers
abstract: Group III–V monolayers: Potential room temperature 2D ferroelectric for next generation device applications.

title: Multiple-unit interlocking enhances the single-stranded tiles assembly of DNA nanostructures
abstract: Multiple-unit interlocking assembly strategy has provided a method for accelerating the constructing of stable DNA nanostructures with the potential for numerous applications.

title: Assessing inorganic nanoparticle toxicity through omics approaches
abstract: This review aims to provide a deeper understanding and new perspectives of omics approaches to characterize the toxicity and biological interactions of inorganic nanoparticles, and improve the safety of nanoparticle applications.

title: Hydrogen production catalysed by atomically precise metal clusters
abstract: Atomically precise metal clusters for electrocatalytic and photocatalytic H 2 production are systematically summarized, which is expected to provide new insights into the design of high-performance H 2 production catalysts.

title: Research on the preparation and performance of Ni 2 P@MOF composite nanomaterials
abstract: The minimum RL value for the Ni 2 P@MOF in the graph is −27 dB, with an effective absorption rate of 90% and an effective bandwidth of 6 GHz. Thus, it emerges as a high-performance absorbing material with significant potential applications.

title: Cells have the ability to break and chemically modify GaP(As) nanowires
abstract: Cells can break and chemically modify nanowires allowing imaging, transfection and assay development.

title: Recognizing the reactive sites of SnFe 2 O 4 for the oxygen evolution reaction: the synergistic effect of Sn II and Fe III in stabilizing reaction intermediates
abstract: A combination of electro-kinetics and computational study establishes the synergetic effect of Sn( ii ) and Fe( iii ) sites on the SnFe 2 O 4 surface to promote hydroperoxo (*OOH) formation as the rate-limiting step of the oxygen evolution reaction.

title: Generation of skyrmions by combining thermal and spin–orbit torque: breaking half skyrmions into skyrmions
abstract: The study establishes that Joule heating combined with Spin–Orbit Torque (SOT) effectively induces the stripe domain – skyrmion transition in magnetic materials.

title: A double-crack structure for bionic wearable strain sensors with ultra-high sensitivity and a wide sensing range
abstract: Benefiting from the double-crack structure, sensors show ultra-high sensitivity, a wide working range and great value in the human–machine interaction.

title: New paradigms of 2D layered material self-driven photodetectors
abstract: The burgeoning initiatives implementing self-driven 2D layered material photodetectors have been presented, heralding new avenues for the next-generation integrated and miniaturized optoelectronic industry.

title: Quantitative photocurrent scanning probe microscopy on PbS quantum dot monolayers
abstract: Photoconductive atomic force microscopy quantitatively probes monolayers of PbS/perovskite quantum dots with a contact area of 1–3 dots in stable and reproducible acquisition conditions for I / V curves and photocurrent maps.

title: Large-scale semi-embedded AgNW-based stretchable transparent electrodes via superwettability-induced transfer of AgNWs/ionic liquid onto gradient PDMS
abstract: Stretchable transparent electrodes (STEs) composed of highly conductive silver nanowires (AgNWs) and mechanically stretchable polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) are compelling materials for the development of flexible electronic devices.

title: Unraveling the ammonia sensing behavior and degradation pathways of a novel lead-free MA 2 CuBr 4 based ammonia sensor
abstract: We report a lead-free copper-based halide perovskite gas sensor to detect ammonia gas at ambient temperature.

title: A NAD(P)H oxidase mimic for catalytic tumor therapy via a deacetylase SIRT7-mediated AKT/GSK3β pathway
abstract: The C@Co nanozyme with NOX-like activity can efficiently consume NAD(P)H within cancer cells and the generation of NAD(P) + promoted the expression of deacetylase SIRT7, whic inhibited the AKT/GSK3 β signaling pathway, ultimately promoting apoptosis.

title: A twist for tunable electronic and thermal transport properties of nanodevices
abstract: A twist-driven switching phenomenon in low-twist nanodevices based on zigzag graphene. Nanoribbons with [6,3]-flake.

title: Dual-site OER mechanism exploration through regulating asymmetric multi-site NiOOH
abstract: Asymmetric nickel oxyhydroxide (NiOOH) possesses multi-OH and O active sites on different surfaces, (001) and (001̄), which possibly causes a complicated catalytic process.

title: Clustering triggered emissive liquid crystalline template for dual mode upconverted and downconverted circularly polarized luminescence
abstract: A liquid crystalline system exhibiting clustering triggered emission shows intrinsic downconversion chiral luminescence and acts as a template for the generation of upconversion circularly polarized luminescence leading to dual mode chiral emission.

title: A gel polymer electrolyte functionalized separator for high-performance lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: Application of TPFPB/Al 2 O 3 /PE separators in lithium–sulfur batteries.

title: Dehydrogenative oxidation of hydrosilanes using gold nanoparticle deposited on citric acid-modified fibrillated cellulose: unveiling the role of molecular oxygen
abstract: We developed an efficient and environmentally friendly catalyst, Au:F-CAC, for the dehydrogenative oxidation of hydrosilanes. Detailed analyses revealed that the cationic Au sites generated via the adsorption of oxygen play an important role.

title: Enhanced antibacterial efficacy: rapid analysis of silver-decorated azithromycin-infused Soluplus® nanoparticles against E. coli and S. epidermidis biofilms
abstract: The threat of antibiotic-resistent biofilms demands solutions. Silver-coated azithromycin-Soluplus nanoparticles release silver ions for 6+ hours, disrupt biofilms, and enable rapid, sensitive bacterial detection with laser-induced graphene sensors.

title: Unravelling the atomistic mechanisms underpinning the morphological evolution of Al-alloyed hematite
abstract: Hydrothermal synthesis based upon the use of Al 3+ as the dopant and/or ethanol as the solvent is effective in promoting the growth of hematite into nanoplates rich in the (001) surface, which is highly active for a broad range of catalytic applications.

title: Electron-beam writing of a relaxor ferroelectric polymer for multiplexing information storage and encryption
abstract: A template-free electron beam lithography (EBL) technique was developed to arbitrarily write ferroelectric nanopatterns of P(VDF-TrFE-CTFE) and applied them for high-security-level multiplexing memory.

title: α-Graphyne with ultra-low diffusion barriers as a promising sodium-ion battery anode and a computational scheme for accurate estimation of theoretical specific capacity
authors: Sharma S. R. K. C. Yamijala
abstract: We propose α-graphyne, with a low diffusion barrier and excellent thermodynamical stability, as a promising anode host for Na-ion batteries. We developed a new computational scheme to accurately calculate theoretical specific capacity (TSC).

title: Interface-resolved photovoltage generation dynamics and band structure evolution in a PbS quantum dot solar cell
abstract: The charge separation and recombination dynamics at different interfaces in a quantum dot solar cell are investigated by time-resolved photoelectron spectroscopy.

title: Correction: Elaborating the interplay between the detecting unit and emitting unit in infrared quantum dot up-conversion photodetectors
abstract: Correction for ‘Elaborating the interplay between the detecting unit and emitting unit in infrared quantum dot up-conversion photodetectors’ by Qiulei Xu et al. , Nanoscale , 2023, 15 , 8197–8203, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3NR01237A.

title: Retraction: Enhanced activity of vancomycin by encapsulation in hybrid magnetic nanoparticles conjugated to a cell-penetrating peptide
abstract: Retraction of ‘Enhanced activity of vancomycin by encapsulation in hybrid magnetic nanoparticles conjugated to a cell-penetrating peptide’ by Wenjie Zhang et al. , Nanoscale , 2020, 12 , 3855–3870, https://doi.org/10.1039/C9NR09687F.

title: Size-dependent catalytic activity for CO oxidation over sub-nano-Au clusters
abstract: Gold (Au) nanocatalysts present outstanding activity for many reactions and have long attracted much attention, but the size effect of sub-nano-clusters on catalytic activity lacks systematic research.

title: p-Block metal atom-induced spin state transition of Fe–N–C catalysts for efficient oxygen reduction
abstract: Herein, we successfully implanted p-block metal single sites (SnN 4 , SbN 4 ) into the Fe–N–C system to vary the spin states of Fe species and investigated the ORR performance of active metal centers with varying effective magnetic moments.

title: Bi-cation incorporated Ni 3 N nanosheets boost water dissociation kinetics for enhanced alkaline hydrogen evolution activity
abstract: Both experimental and theoretical analyses were used to show that simultaneous doping of V and Fe cations could accurately and completely enhance the intrinsic catalytic activity of Ni 3 N nanosheets as hydrogen evolution reaction electrocatalyst.

title: Elucidating the assembly of nanoparticle organic hybrid materials (NOHMs) near an electrode interface with varying potential using neutron reflectivity
abstract: Schematic showing the ordering of free HPE polymer in D 2 O (left), static NOHM-I-HPE in D 2 O (middle), and NOHM-I-HPE in a negative potential in D 2 O (right) near a gold electrode.

title: From sensing interactions to controlling the interactions: a novel approach to obtain biological transistors for specific and label-free immunosensing
abstract: The MNC biosensor is employed for specific and label-free sensing of AFP from 0.5 μL drops of diluted serum with a limit-of-detection of 10.5 aM and a dynamic range extending over 6 orders of AFP concentration.

title: Metamagnetic transition and meta-stable magnetic state in Co-doped Fe 3 GaTe 2
abstract: The phase diagram and M – H curves of the metamagnetic regime of (Fe 1− x Co x ) 3 GaTe 2 with x = 0.19–0.26 at various temperatures were studied.

title: The synthesis and supercapacitor application of flexible free-standing Ti 3 C 2 T x , Mo 2 TiC 2 T x , and V 4 C 3 T x MXene films
abstract: MXenes represent a fascinating category of two-dimensional materials made up of transition metal carbides and nitrides, the three types (Ti 3 C 2 T x , Mo 2 TiC 2 T x , and V 4 C 3 T x ) of flexible free-standing MXene films were prepared successfully.

title: Macrophage membrane-modified targeted phase-change nanoparticles for multimodal imaging of experimental autoimmune myocarditis
abstract: Myocarditis is an important public health issue due to the high prevalence of sudden death in adolescents and young adults.

title: Multifunctional tunable Cu 2 O and CuInS 2 quantum dots on TiO 2 nanotubes for efficient chemical oxidation of cholesterol and ibuprofen
abstract: (a) TEM image of TNTs decorated with Cu 2 O and CuInS 2 QDs. (b) Amperometric response of different configurations of biosensing electrodes. (c) Cholesterol oxidation mechanism on the surface of the CuInS 2 /Cu 2 O/TNT electrode.

title: Optical single molecule characterisation of natural and synthetic polymers through nanopores
abstract: We developed a method based on image analysis to optically determine the molecular weight of both artificial and natural polymers translocating through nanopores.

title: N-doped carbon layer cladded carbon sphere for broadband and boosted microwave attenuation capacity
abstract: The C@NC/PVDF composites obtain excellent wave attenuation capacity by regulating the NC layer thickness and filler loading.

title: DON encapsulated carbon dot–vesicle conjugate in therapeutic intervention of lung adenocarcinoma by dual targeting of CD44 and SLC1A5
abstract: HACD–TMAV, a carbon dot–vesicle conjugate, selectively delivered glutamine inhibitor DON to CD44-enriched cancer cells, inducing ROS triggered apoptosis by dual targeting CD44 and SLC1A5 through downregulation of PI3K/AKT/mTOR signalling cascade.

title: An elliptical nanoantenna array plasmonic metasurface for efficient solar energy harvesting
abstract: A novel ultrathin plasmonic metasurface absorber, composed of TiN/GaN/TiN elliptical nanoantenna arrays, is designed to excite localized surface plasmon resonances for near-perfect broadband optical absorption in both air and water environments.

title: Clonogenic assay and computational modeling using real cell images to study physical enhancement and cellular sensitization induced by metal nanoparticles under MV and kV X-ray irradiation
abstract: A study on the radiophysical dose enhancement and the intrinsic biological sensitization by gold and iron nanoparticles in A549 cancer cells.

title: Imaging of intracellular protein aggregates through plasmon-assisted clusteroluminescence
abstract: The formation of clusters in non-aromatic molecules can give rise to unconventional luminescence or clusteroluminescence.

title: Progress in nanoparticle-based electrochemical biosensors for hormone detection
abstract: A critical comparison between different types of nanoparticle-based electrochemical biosensors for the detection of various hormones.

title: Exploring the degradation of silver nanowire networks under thermal stress by coupling in situ X-ray diffraction and electrical resistance measurements
abstract: During the thermal ramp of a AgNW network, the XRD measurement is sensitive to the bulk of AgNW, whereas the electrical resistance is mainly influenced by AgNW junctions. This enables the observation of differences in thermal transition values.

title: Building a depletion-region width modulation model and realizing memory characteristics in PN heterostructure devices
abstract: A new depletion-region width modulation model is proposed to realize memory characteristics, and the coexistence of memristive and memcapacitive behaviors is demonstrated in p-CuAlO2/n-ZnO, p + -Si/n-ZnO and p-NiO/n-ZnO heterostructure devices.

title: Machine learning in electron beam lithography to boost photoresist formulation design for high-resolution patterning
abstract: A high-precision photoresist imaging model and formulation optimizer for electron beam lithography are developed. The optimized photoresist formulation meets the preset imaging performance requirement, boosting photoresist material design.

title: Enabling white color tunability in complex 3D-printed composites by using lead-free self-trapped exciton 2D perovskite/carbon quantum dot inks
abstract: This work reports a facile preparation of lead-free self-trapped exciton 2D A 2 SnX 4 perovskite/carbon quantum dot inks with tunable white-light emission for luminescent 3D printed composites, and potential application in optoelectronic devices.

title: Impact of supporting nanometric membranes on the thermo-optical dynamics of individual plasmonic nanodisks
abstract: The cooling dynamics of single gold nanodisks supported on nanometric membranes and their optical detection are investigated using time-resolved optical spectroscopy and numerical simulations.

title: Morphological and structural design through hard-templating of PGM-free electrocatalysts for AEMFC applications
authors: 'Hilah C. Honig', 'Silvia Mostoni', 'Yan Presman', 'Rifael Z. Snitkoff-Sol', 'Paolo Valagussa', 'Massimiliano D'Arienzo', 'Roberto Scotti', 'Carlo Santoro', 'Mohsin Muhyuddin', 'Lior Elbaz'
abstract: This study delves into the critical role of customized materials design and synthesis methods in influencing the performance of electrocatalysts for the oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) in anion exchange membrane fuel cells (AEMFCs).

title: Recent advances in photoelectrochemical hydrogen production using I–III–VI quantum dots
abstract: This minireview covers recent advances in photoelectrochemical hydrogen production using I–III–VI QDs, detailing the material design strategies.

title: Barium molybdate up-conversion nanoscale particles with IR-LED chip, temperature sensing, and anti-counterfeiting applications
abstract: Barium molybdate nanoparticles exhibiting up-conversion luminescence were synthesized via the solvothermal method.

title: Polyoxometalate-mediated syntheses of three structurally new silver clusters
abstract: Three structurally new polyoxometalate-templated silver clusters have been synthesized using a facile solvothermal approach, the resulting three clusters exhibit distinct temperature-dependent photoluminescence and photothermal conversion properties.

title: Lanthanide-based nanomaterials for temperature sensing in the near-infrared spectral region: illuminating progress and challenges
abstract: This review highlights recent advancements in the development of lanthanide (Ln 3+ )-based nanothermometers, focusing on those operating in the near-infrared (NIR) spectral region and outlining key findings as well as remaining challenges.

title: Multifunctional water-soluble binders for Li–S batteries
abstract: In order to suppress the shuttle effect of a sulfur material during the charging and discharging processes, this work provides a new strategy for designing advanced water-soluble multifunctional binders for improving the cycle life of Li–S batteries.

title: New insights into red blood cells in tumor precision diagnosis and treatment
abstract: Red blood cells (RBCs), provide a new research perspective for the development of tumor liquid biopsies, molecular imaging, drug delivery, and other tumor precision diagnosis and treatment technologies.

title: A-site-deficiency range identified for in situ exsolution from (La 0.4 Sr 0.6 ) 1− α Ti 0.95 Ni 0.05 O 3± δ electrodes for SOFC and SOEC
abstract: A wide range of A-site-deficient samples of (La 0.4 Sr 0.6 ) 1− α Ti 0.95 Ni 0.05 O 3− δ ( α = 0.00, 0.13, 0.15, and 0.18) was designed to investigate their properties, exsolution process, and electrochemical performance.

title: Shape-tunable two-dimensional assemblies from chromophore-conjugated crystallizable poly( l -lactides) with chain-length-dependent photophysical properties
abstract: This study unveils shape-tunable precision two-dimensional structures from chromophore-conjugated poly( l -lactides) by crystallization-driven self-assembly and the polymer chain length-dependent optical properties of the surface-occupied π-systems.

title: Harnessing a WO x -based flexible transparent memristor synapse with a hafnium oxide layer for neuromorphic computing
abstract: Transparent memristor-based neuromorphic synapses are expected to be specialised devices for high-speed information transmission and processing.

title: Recent progress in biosensor regeneration techniques
abstract: Biosensors are widely applied in biomarker detection. Their widespread use necessitates regeneration methods to ensure cost-effectiveness and sustainability. This mini-review systematically summarizes recently reported regeneration techniques.

title: Celebrating the 150th anniversary of Vanderbilt University
abstract: An introduction to the Nanoscale Horizons , Nanoscale and Nanoscale Advances themed collection celebrating the 150th anniversary of Vanderbilt University.

title: Polymer functionalized antimony sulfide quantum dots for broadband optical limiting
abstract: Highly soluble SQDs-PVK was synthesized in situ . The annealed SQDs-PVK/PMMA film showed outstanding nonlinear optical performance, with large nonlinear absorption coefficients of 713.71 cm GW −1 at 532 nm and 913.60 cm GW −1 at 1064 nm.

title: Tuning the dimensional order in self-assembled magnetic nanostructures: theory, simulations, and experiments
abstract: Our synthesis method yields FeCo nanostructures of varied dimensions, validated by simulations. In addition, our findings highlight enhanced coercivity in these structures.

title: Modulating the electronic structure of VS 2 via Ru decoration for an efficient pH-universal electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Electron redistribution occurs at the interface of Ru and VS 2 , optimizing the electronic structure and strengthening the intrinsic activity of VS 2 .

title: First-principles theory of the nitrogen interstitial in hBN: a plausible model for the blue emitter
abstract: We elaborate on the methodology of computing color centers in periodic and cluster models of hBN. Using first-principles methods, we determined various optical properties of the nitrogen interstitial defect and compared them to experimental results.

title: Photocatalytic applications of covalent organic frameworks: synthesis, characterization, and utility
abstract: This review highlights the recent advances in utilizing covalent organic frameworks (COFs) for photocatalytic applications.

title: A switchable green emitting dye and its phenomenal properties: implications for the photoluminescence features of carbon dots
abstract: The presence of green PL emitting anionic HPPT-like species within CA–urea CDs is corroborated by examination of a reversible specific pyrrole ring opening hydrolysis, which may be used for fine tuning of the PL emission colour from CA–urea CDs.

title: A first-principles study of multilayer Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene model
abstract: A more realistic multilayer Ti 3 C 2 T x model is proposed, and the structural and electronic properties are presented in this study.

title: Changes of phonon modes and electron transfer induced by interface interactions of Pd/MoS 2 heterostructures
abstract: Effect of Pd nanoparticles on the phonon modes of monolayer MoS 2 . Compared with pristine spectrum, a new A′ 1g vibration peak appears after Pd deposition. Its Raman intensity mapping shows the detectable signal on the whole Pd–MoS 2 area.

title: The dual-functional role of carboxylate in a nickel–iron catalyst towards efficient oxygen evolution
abstract: Carboxylate ligand, as a proton acceptor, activates O–H bonds and boosts OER kinetics, while strengthening Fe–O bonds and inhibiting Fe dissolution. This dual-functional role significantly enhances both activity and stability of NiFe LDH.

title: Potassium hydroxide treatment of layered WSe 2 with enhanced electronic performances
abstract: In this report, WSe 2 -based field effect transistors with enhanced electron and hole mobilities of 40 and 85 cm 2 V −1 s −1 were prepared by the layer-by-layer etching technique.

title: Supramolecular design as a route to high-performing organic electrodes
abstract: Supramolecular design principles are emerging as a powerful tool to manipulate organic cathode performance. In this review, we discuss how supramolecular interactions influence electrode behavior.

title: Recent advances in the synthesis of transition metal hydroxyl oxide catalysts and their application in electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reactions
abstract: The oxyhydroxides (MOOH) for oxygen evolution reaction (OER) in recent years is reviewed. The control mechanism of improving OER catalytic performance of MOOH is discussed. Finally, the challenges and future developments of MOOH in OER are analyzed.

title: Enhancement of the photoacoustic effect during the light–particle interaction
abstract: For a short pulse duration, increasing the interfacial thermal conductivity can enhance the heat transfer effectively. The enhancement effect of photoacoustic signal by increasing the interfacial thermal conductivity is affected by pulse duration.

title: A high-speed infrared tellurium photodetector on a silicon nitride platform
abstract: We demonstrate a high-speed waveguide-integrated tellurium photodetector on a silicon nitride platform. At a wavelength of 1570 nm, the photodetector has a measured responsivity of 0.5 A W −1 at 1 V bias voltage and a bandwidth of 12 GHz.

title: Bifunctional electrocatalytic hybrid heterostructures for polysulfide anchoring/conversion for a stable lithium–sulfur battery
abstract: In situ phase engineering of transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) based hybrid heterostructures with controlled sulfur vacancies offers a promising strategy for superior-performance lithium–sulfur (Li–S) batteries.

title: Correction: Towards control of excitonic coupling in DNA-templated Cy5 aggregates: the principal role of chemical substituent hydrophobicity and steric interactions
abstract: Correction for ‘Towards control of excitonic coupling in DNA-templated Cy5 aggregates: the principal role of chemical substituent hydrophobicity and steric interactions’ by Sebastián A. Díaz et al. , Nanoscale , 2023, 15 , 3284–3299. https://doi.org/10.1039/D2NR05544A.

title: Evaluation of polymer–preservative interactions for preservation efficacy: molecular dynamics simulation and QSAR approaches
abstract: A computational approach for guiding appropriate selection of polymers and preservatives, and ultimately for accelerating the rational design of high efficacy preservative systems.

title: Optical dark-field spectroscopy of single plasmonic nanoparticles for molecular biosciences
abstract: This review examines the use of gold nanoparticles as sensors in biomolecular science through optical dark-field spectroscopy, applied to systems such as DNA, proteins, complexes, and cells, with potential for in vivo applications soon.

title: Highly stable and ultra-fast vibration-responsive flexible iontronic sensors for accurate acoustic signal recognition
abstract: A wearable acoustic vibration pressure sensor was developed based on an interfacial-enhanced iontronic dielectric and integrated with an acoustic recognition system using a deep learning classifier.

title: Unraveling the molecular and growth mechanism of colloidal black In 2 O 3− x
abstract: Precursors, ligands, and reaction environment dictate the path for the colloidal synthesis of oxygen-deficient black indium oxide nanoparticles with enhanced optical properties.

title: FeAu mixing for high-temperature control of light scattering at the nanometer scale
abstract: Mixing of immiscible elements in single nanoparticles allows controlling the light scattering via a temperature-induced phase change at 800 °C, paving the way to design high-temperature optical triggers at the nanometer scale.

title: Self-assembly of water-filled molecular saddles to generate diverse morphologies and high proton conductivity
abstract: A saddle that protons can ride on! A saddle-shaped aza-crown-like macrocycle produces diverse morphologies and high solid-state proton conduction up to 21.1 mS cm −1 (at 27 °C and 95% RH).

title: Nanoscopic visualization of microgel-immobilized cytochrome P450 enzymes and their local activity
abstract: The distribution and local catalytic activity of Cytochrome P450 enzymes immobilized in microgels by electrostatic interaction has been determined with a combination of in situ super-resolution fluorescence microscopy experiments.

title: Ultrabright fluorescent particles via physical encapsulation of fluorescent dyes in mesoporous silica: a mini-review
abstract: Harnessing the power of mesoporous silica to encapsulate organic fluorescent dyes has led to the creation of an extraordinary class of nanocomposite photonic materials.

title: Fabrication of one-dimensional nanostructures standing vertically on a substrate through layer-by-layer deposition
abstract: LBL deposition has been widely used to prepare nanocomposites with controllable properties, where the controllability of nanocomposite properties can be further enhanced by combining the self-assembly of hard materials in soft materials.

title: A suitably fabricated ternary nanocomposite (Cu-CuO@rGO-SiO 2 ) as a sustainable and common heterogeneous catalyst for C–S, C–O and C–N coupling reactions
abstract: A unique nanocomposite, Cu-CuO@rGO-SiO 2 , exhibits enhanced catalytic activity in C–X (X = S, N, O) coupling reactions. The high performance presumably originates from synergism of different co-existing copper species spread over rGO-SiO 2 matrices.

title: Fabrication of palladium-enriched metallic structures by direct focused He + and Ne + beam nanowriting from organometallic thin films: a comparison with Ga + and e − beams
abstract: A direct nanowriting method using He + and Ne + ion beams with spin-coated palladium acetate thin films enables the fabrication of metallic structures in a single and fast step, showcasing versatility with the four most commonly used focused ion beams.

title: Stable room-temperature ferromagnetism and gate-tunable quantum anomalous Hall effect of two-dimensional 5d transition-metal trihalide OsX 3 (X = Cl, Br, I) monolayers
abstract: The robustness of FM phases above room temperature is confirmed and room temperature QAHE is expected in 2D OsI 3 monolayers.

title: Semisynthetic ferritin-based nanoparticles with high magnetic anisotropy for spatial magnetic manipulation and inductive heating
abstract: The core of the semi-synthetic magnetic nanoparticle magnetoferritin was doped with cobalt or zinc in order to evaluate its applicability as a nanoagent for intracellular target redistribution or localised heat dissipation via magnetic hyperthermia.

title: Multivalent nanobody engineering for enhanced physisorption and functional display on gold nanoparticles
abstract: Engineered multivalent nanobodies improve gold nanoparticle bioconjugate stability and improve sensitivity of nanobody-based lateral flow assay.

title: An Au 5 Ag 12 (SR) 9 (dppf) 4 alloy nanocluster: structural determination and optical property and photothermal conversion investigation
abstract: We synthesized the Au5Ag12 nanocluster with a PLQY near zero, leading to minimal luminescence. This enhances energy release via non-radiative transitions, boosting photothermal efficiency.

title: Pair correlations of the easy magnetisation axes of superparamagnetic nanoparticles in a ferrofluid/ferrocomposite
abstract: The easy magnetisation axes of superparamagnetic nanoparticles align parallel to the direction of an applied magnetic field, and interparticle magnetic particle interactions strengthen the effect of easy-axis correlation.

title: Tuneable C3 product selectivity of glycerol electrooxidation on cubic and dendritic Pt nanocatalysts
abstract: The glycerol electrooxidation reaction's C3 product selectivity and glycerol conversion in an alkaline medium are more efficient on Pt CUBE than on Pt DEND , with lactate selectivity reaching up to 68%.

title: Pyrene monomer–excimer dynamics to reveal molecular organization in mesoporous hybrid silica films
abstract: Pyrene derivative is used as a molecular probe to investigate the interactions in hybrid mesoporous films through fluorescence spectroscopy. The dynamic formation of pyrene excimer provides insight into the nanoscale organization of the material.

title: Novel nitrogen-doped carbon-coated SnSe 2 based on a post-synthetically modified MOF as a high-performance anode material for LIBs and SIBs
abstract: Carbon-coated SnSe 2 /C–N composites with optimized hetero-atom doping and electrical conductivity exhibit superior electrochemical performance in lithium-ion batteries and sodium-ion batteries.

title: Dense r-Ru/FeCoP heterointerfaces induced by a defect-assisted strategy for ultrastable alkaline overall water splitting
abstract: FeCoP nanosheets anchored by Ru NPs (r-Ru/FeCoP) were synthesized using an oxygen defect-assisted-gas-phase phosphorization strategy. The η 100 values of r-Ru/FeCoP are 0.8 and 0.58 times those of Pt/C and RuO 2 during the alkaline HER and OER.

title: Tailoring sensitization properties and improving near-infrared photon upconversion performance through alloying in superatomic molecular Au 25 nanoclusters
abstract: The alloying of rod-shaped Au 25 nanoclusters with Cu atoms significantly enhanced the triplet sensitization and near-infrared photon upconversion properties when paired with 9,10-bis(phenylethynyl)anthracene annihilators.

title: A novel electrical depercolation model for stretchable nanocomposite strain sensors
abstract: Our new piezoresistive model unleashes the full sensing potential of nanocomposites, enabling accurate calibration across the entire conducting strain range and establishing a new framework for exploring key insights into optimising performance.

title: Ferroelectric field enhanced tribocatalytic hydrogen production and RhB dye degradation by tungsten bronze ferroelectrics
abstract: The BSNT ferroelectric submicron powder tribocatalytic water splitting for hydrogen with rate of 200 μmol/h/g and tribocatalytic degradation of RhB dyes with efficiency 96% in 2 hours, •OH and •O 2 − play crucial roles in tribocatalysis.

title: Carbon quantum dots (CQDs)-modified polymers: a review of non-optical applications
abstract: CQD–polymer composite structure for enhanced self-healing and shape memory characteristics.

title: Synergistic nucleation regulation using 4,4′,4′′-tris(carbazol-9-yl)-triphenylamine and moisture for stably air-processed high-performance perovskite photodetectors
abstract: A synergistic nucleation strategy for producing an air-processed high-performance perovskite photodetector.

title: Influence of the TiN diffusion barrier on the leakage current and ferroelectricity in an Al-doped HfO x ferroelectric memristor and its application to neuromorphic computing
abstract: The TiN electrode as the diffusion barrier of W suppresses the leakage current and induces a larger remanent polarization and a smaller coercive voltage. The improved ferroelectric memristors can also be applied to neuromorphic computing.

title: Microscopy aided detection of the self-intercalation mechanism and in situ electronic properties in chromium selenide
abstract: Atomic microscopy detection directly reveals the coexistence of multiple stoichiometric ratios in self-intercalated chromium selenide and clearly shows the intercalation mechanism of Cr atoms according to the periodicity of the cross section and surface.

title: A Se-induced heterostructure electrode with polymetallic-CoNiFe towards high performance supercapacitors
abstract: A heterogeneous interface nanoarray (CoNiFe-Se) containing electroactive polymetallic-CoNiFe with abundant redox active sites is constructed by a facile selenization step.

title: Aminotriazine derived N-doped mesoporous carbon with a tunable nitrogen content and their improved oxygen reduction reaction performance
abstract: Mesoporous N-doped carbon with a tunable nitrogen content showed excellent performance for oxygen reduction due to the pyridinic/graphitic nitrogen and structural defects, offering a promising strategy for designing electrocatalysts.

title: Advances in functionalization and conjugation mechanisms of dendrimers with iron oxide magnetic nanoparticles
abstract: The various functionalization and conjugation mechanisms of dendrimers with magnetic nanoparticles (MPNs).

title: Uncovering thermally activated purple-to-blue luminescence in Co-modified MgAl-layered double hydroxide
abstract: Co-modified nano-sandrose layered double hydroxide containing 15 mol% Co shows thermally-activated luminescence in the purple-to-blue visible-light region at room temperature.

title: Efficient degradation of organic pollutants without any external assistance over a wide pH range using carbon vacancy-modified Fe–N–C catalysts
abstract: Cv-Fe–N–C SACs were developed for the effective degradation of organic pollutants without external light sources or oxidants. The Cv-Fe–N–C SACs could remove over 99% of RhB within 10 min at room temperature.

title: Passive fractionating mechanism for oil spill using shear-wettability modulation
abstract: An efficient oil separation device using graphene-like nanochannels, based on wettability, to address oil spills and chemical leaks across diverse environmental conditions.

title: Correction: Magnetic aerogels from FePt and CoPt 3 directly from organic solution
abstract: Correction for ‘Magnetic aerogels from FePt and CoPt 3 directly from organic solution’ by L. Schoske et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, 16 , 4229–4238, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3NR05892A.

title: Electronic redistribution through the interface of MnCo 2 O 4 –Ni 3 N nano-urchins prompts rapid In situ phase transformation for enhanced oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: In this study, the in situ electrochemical phase transformation of MnCo 2 O 4 –Ni 3 N into MnCo 2 O 4 –NiOOH is described. The catalyst has an exceptional overpotential of 224 mV to drive a current density of 10 mA cm −2 . Strong contact between MnCo 2 O 4 and Ni 3 N is responsible for the phase tra…

title: Deformation mechanisms based on the multiscale molecular dynamics of a gradient TA1 titanium alloy
abstract: The heterogeneous gradient TA1 titanium alloy holds great potential for a wide range of industrial applications.

title: De novo Cu-MOF@CNS nanocomposite coated on a cotton fibrils framework for sustainable solar-driven desalination
abstract: We developed a solar absorber derived from a Cu-MOF and CNS that were coated on cotton fibrils for generating potable water from saline water using solar radiation for efficient water evaporation that offers scalable production and promotes sustainability.

title: Anisotropic thermo-mechanical response of layered hexagonal boron nitride and black phosphorus: application as a simultaneous pressure and temperature sensor
abstract: The anisotropic thermal and mechanical response of hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) and black phosphorus (bP) have been investigated in situ using high resolution X-ray diffraction.

title: Controlled evolution of surface microstructure and phase boundary of ZnO nanoparticles for the multiple sensitization effects on triethylamine detection
abstract: Possible sensing mechanism and surface process diagram of ZnO-650 sensor.

title: Ultrahigh-speed absolute temperature sensing using ferroelectric HfO 2 enabled by transient negative differential capacitance
abstract: Using short electric pulse, our sensor, made from ferroelectric hafnium zirconium oxide nanolaminates, offers ultrafast response time of ∼50 ns and high accuracy (1.0 K). This technology is compatible with USB interfaces for real-time monitoring.

title: Fluorinated polymer zwitterions on gold nanoparticles: patterned catalyst surfaces guide interfacial transport and electrochemical CO 2 reduction
abstract: We report the use of fluorinated polymer zwitterions to build hybrid systems for efficient CO 2 electroreduction.

title: Phase control of solid-solution RuIn nanoparticles and their catalytic properties
abstract: We achieved the phase control of solid-solution RuIn NPs from fcc to hcp crystal structures by H 2 heat treatment for the first time. The hcp RuIn NPs exhibited enhanced HER catalytic performance in comparison with the fcc RuIn NPs.

title: Recent advances in functional micro/nanomaterials for removal of crude oil via thermal effects
abstract: Overview of research development in crude oil adsorption and removal by thermal affection from different heating methods.

title: Synthesis of self-pillared pentasil zeolites without organic templates and seeds
abstract: Self-pillared pentasil (SPP) zeolites with pure MFI topology have been successfully synthesized without the assistance of organic templates and seeds.

title: Beam induced heating in electron microscopy modeled with machine learning interatomic potentials
abstract: Molecular dynamics based on machine learning potentials are combined with EELS data to model electron beam heating in high-resolution transmission electron microscopy.

title: Photoresponsive arylazopyrazole surfactant/PDADMAC mixtures: reversible control of bulk and interfacial properties
abstract: Photoswitchable surfactants enable remote control of bulk and interfacial properties in PDADMAC/C n AAP mixtures. E/Z photoisomerization facilitates tuning of binding, surface charging, and foam stability while the sample composition is preserved.

title: Revealing large room-temperature Nernst coefficients in 2D materials by first-principles modeling
abstract: This study reveals exceptionally large Nernst coefficients in two-dimensional materials at room temperature by employing first principles calculations. Notably, ABA-stacked trilayer graphene exhibits a Nernst coefficient as high as 112 μV (KT) −1 .

title: Tuning the electronic structure of Pd by the surface configuration of Al 2 O 3 for hydrogenation reactions
abstract: The distinct acid–base properties of γ-Al 2 O 3 and η-Al 2 O 3 confer electron-rich and electron-deficient characteristics on it supported Pd nanoparticles, selectively enhancing nitrobenzene and phenylacetylene hydrogenation, respectively.

title: Laser polarization as a critical factor in the SERS-based molecular sensing performance of nano-gapped Au nanowires
abstract: An interesting paradigm that demonstrates the determining role of laser polarization incidence angle in the Raman signal enhancement in nano-gapped Au nanowires is reported, showing its key role in the SERS-based molecular sensing efficiency.

title: Coupling of two-dimensional MXenes and graphene for boosting the hydrogen storage performance of MgH 2
abstract: MgH 2 used in solid-state hydrogen storage still suffers from high thermal stability and slow hydrogen absorption–desorption kinetics.

title: Recent progress in critical electrode and electrolyte materials for flexible zinc-ion batteries
abstract: In this review article, some recent advances in the electrode and electrolyte materials for FZIBs are summarized. The efforts to explore the applications of FZIBs have been considered and challenges for the development of FZIBs are also discussed.

title: Ion transport mechanisms in pectin-containing EC–LiTFSI electrolytes
abstract: Using all-atom molecular dynamics simulations, we report the structure and ion transport characteristics of a new class of solid polymer electrolytes that contain the biodegradable and mechanically stable biopolymer pectin.

title: Sensitizing chemotherapy for glioma with fisetin mediated by a microenvironment-responsive nano-drug delivery system
abstract: In this work, we designed a microenvironment-responsive nano-delivery system to enhance the therapeutic effect of chemotherapy, which provided a feasible scheme to solve drug resistance and achieve targeted therapy.

title: Recent advances in MXene-based composites for piezoelectric sensors
abstract: This review systematically discusses various MXene composites, exploring the impact of incorporating different materials on the piezoelectric properties and the overall efficiency of sensors. Images reproduced with permission, full details in paper.

title: A novel dual S-scheme Co 9 S 8 /MnCdS/Co 3 O 4 heterojunction for photocatalytic hydrogen evolution under visible light irradiation
abstract: A dual S-scheme heterojunction was constructed which expands the light absorption range and extends the carrier lifetime. The hydrogen evolution rate of Co 9 S 8 /MnCdS/Co 3 O 4 was 3.49 and 1.49 times higher than those of pristine MnCdS and MnCdS/Co 3 O 4 .

title: A two-dimensional PtSe 2 thin film coupled with a graphene/Si Schottky junction for a high-performance photodetector
abstract: The built-in electric field is enhanced by coupling a 2D PtSe 2 thin film and a graphene/Si Schottky junction, and the double-heterojunction device shows better photodetection performance under near-infrared illumination.

title: Support effect on Ni-based mono- and bimetallic catalysts in CO 2 hydrogenation
abstract: Zirconia- and ceria-supported Ni,Fe catalysts show higher activity than MgO-supported catalysts in CO 2 hydrogenation. This effect is due to differences in reducibility and oxygen vacancy formation resulting in modified metal-support interactions.

title: Exciton diffusion in organic semiconductors: precision and pitfalls
abstract: The fundamentals of and techniques for measuring exciton diffusion length in organic semiconductors are described, focusing on inherent challenges and developed solutions. Discrepancies between measurements are explored and their origins explained.

title: Study of a charge transition-driven resistive switching mechanism in TiO 2 -based random access memory via density functional theory
abstract: This study investigated changes in the oxidation state of oxygen vacancies and their kinetics within the conducting filament, establishing a crucial relationship to resistive random access memory (RRAM) switching behavior.

title: Beam-profile compensation for quantum yield characterisation of Yb–Tm codoped upconverting nanoparticles emitting at 474 nm, 650 nm and 804 nm
abstract: A novel method for accurately characterising the power density-dependent quantum yield of UCNPs. The method accounts for the beam profile distortions, which is important for application of UCNPs in various fields, especially for biophotonics.

title: Physical and chemical parameters determining the formation of gold–sp metal (Al, Ga, In, and Pb) nanoalloys
abstract: Gold–sp metal (Al, Ga, In, and Pb) nanoalloy formation via laser ablation in liquid evidenced that alloying rules are solvent-dependent and can be described with only two parameters in acetone ( R 2 0.98812) and three parameters in ethanol ( R 2 0.99393).

title: BP/GaN and BP/GaP core/shell nanowires: theoretical insights into photovoltaic and gas-sensing abilities
abstract: BP/GaN and BP/GaP core/shell nanowires (CSNWs) show good sensitivity for some small oxide gases. Also, BP/GaP CSNWs show >33% photovoltaic conversion efficiency.

title: A spin wave driven skyrmion-based diode on a T-shaped nanotrack
abstract: Utilizing spin waves driven skyrmion, a T-shaped nanotrack enables diode functionality, crucial for spintronic devices. It is achieved with 60–80 GHz and 0.07–0.4 T field, offering speed upto 100 m s −1 . This innovation heralds non-charge-based device.

title: BODIPY directed one-dimensional self-assembly of gold nanorods
abstract: Self-assembly of gold nanorods is facilitated by plasmon–molecule interactions between gold and BODIPY, and the chemical structure of BODIPY plays a crucial role in forming a linear chain.

title: pH-Responsive self-assembled polymer-photosensitizer conjugate for activable photodynamic therapy
abstract: This paper reports synthesis, aqueous self-assembly and relevance of the pH-triggered activable photodynamic therapy of amphiphilic polyurethane (P1S) functionalized with a heavy-atom free organic photosensitizer.

title: Twist angle-dependent interlayer hybridized exciton lifetimes in van der Waals heterostructures
abstract: The interlayer twist angle has a direct effect on exciton lifetimes in van der Waals heterostructures.

title: A van der Waals p–n heterostructure of GaSe/SnS 2 : a high thermoelectric figure of merit and strong anisotropy
abstract: The van der Waals p–n heterostructure of GaSe/SnS2 with a high thermoelectric figure of merit and strong anisotropy.

title: Stability of [10–12]cycloparaphenylene complexes with pristine fullerenes C 76,78,84 and endohedral metallofullerenes M 3 N@C 78,80
abstract: The stability and charge distributions of noncovalent complexes of [10–12]CPP with the pristine fullerenes C 76/78/84 and the endohedral metallofullerenes Sc 3 N@ D 3h -C 78 , Sc 3 N@ D 5h -C 80 and M 3 N@ I h -C 80 (M = Sc, Y, Lu, Gd) are studied in the gas phase.

title: A new material built with alternating Cu sulfide and (Al,Mg) hydroxide molecular sheets: hydrothermal synthesis and selected characteristics
abstract: Two-dimensional materials with new physical features are gaining popularity due to their unique properties.

title: Synergistic effect of atomically dispersed Cu species and Ti-defects for boosting photocatalytic CO 2 reduction over hierarchical TiO 2
abstract: The synergistic effect of atomically dispersed Cu species and Ti-defects on hierarchical TiO 2 significantly enhances the separation of photogenerated charges, thereby promoting the highly selective conversion of CO 2 into CO, mediated by water.

title: Boron nanosheets boosting solar thermal water evaporation
abstract: Boron nanosheets improve evaporation rates of hydrogel-based solar vapour generators. The extensive B–O bonds lead to high energy conversion efficiency and reduce water evaporation enthalpy. The system also shows high salt resistance and high durability.

title: Design and synthesis of a nucleobase functionalized peptide hydrogel: in vitro assessment of anti-inflammatory and wound healing effects
abstract: Over the past several years, a significant increase in the expanding field of biomaterial sciences has been observed due to the development of biocompatible materials based on peptide derivatives that have intrinsic therapeutic potential.

title: Nanoscale visualization of phase separation in binary supported lipid monolayer using tip-enhanced Raman spectroscopy
abstract: Nanoscale phase separation in a d 62 -DPPC : DOPC lipid monolayer is visualized in a label-free and non-destructive manner using hyperspectral tip-enhanced Raman spectroscopy imaging.

title: Morphology does not matter: WSe 2 luminescence nanothermometry unravelled
abstract: Applying multiple linear regression to WSe 2 quantum dots, we achieve enhanced thermal sensitivity (30% K −1 ) and reduced temperature uncertainty (0.1 K), highlighting the potential of WSe 2 as a versatile nanothermometer for microfluidics, nanofluidics, and biomedical assays.

title: Fast access of the lattice thermal conductivity and phonon quasiparticle spectra of Mo 2 TiC 2 T 2 (T = –O and –F) and Janus Mo 2 TiC 2 OF MXenes from machine learning potentials
abstract: The presence of strong anharmonic effects in surface functionalized MXenes greatly challenges the use of harmonic lattice dynamics calculations to predict their phonon spectra and lattice thermal conductivity at finite temperatures.

title: Macrophage reprogramming combined with enhanced photodynamic therapy increases the patency of malignant esophageal obstruction after stenting
abstract: Manganese-based nanoparticles achieve macrophage reprogramming combined with enhanced photodynamic therapy for malignant esophageal obstruction via the STAT/HMGB1 pathway.

title: Direct visualization of domain wall pinning in sub-100 nm 3D magnetic nanowires with cross-sectional curvature
abstract: Direct laser writing and deposition is used to produce sub-100 nm 3D magnetic nanowires with cross-sectional and longitudinal curvature. Magnetic force microscopy images domain walls within the nanowire, allowing the energy landscape to be probed.

title: Highly stable two-dimensional Ruddlesden–Popper perovskite-based resistive switching memory devices
abstract: Two-dimensional Ruddlesden–Popper perovskites (TEA) 2 PbBr 4 - and (TEA) 2 PbI 4 -based resistive switching random-access memory devices exhibit high ON/OFF ratio, excellent data retention, and good endurance along with remarkable environmental stability.

title: Flash nanoprecipitation assisted self-assembly of ionizable lipid nanoparticles for nucleic acid delivery
abstract: Flash nanoprecipitation utilizing the confined impinging jet mixer allows the self-assembly of ionizable lipid nanoparticles that can effectively deliver nucleic acids both in vitro and in vivo .

title: Advances in lipid nanoparticle mRNA therapeutics beyond COVID-19 vaccines
abstract: This review highlights the latest advances in lipid nanoparticle mRNA based nanomedicines under preclinical and clinical investigation.

title: Understanding associative polymer self-assembly with shrinking gate fluorescence correlation spectroscopy
abstract: Shrinking gate fluorescence correlation spectroscopy (sgFCS) enhances sensitivity to polymer self-assembly.

title: Atomic insight into the effects of precursor clusters on monolayer WSe 2
abstract: By controlling the Se/W ratio, the interaction between precursor clusters and WSe 2 and the electronic properties of the prepared WSe 2 monolayer can be effectively tuned, which is significant for the high-quality growth and applications of WSe 2 .

title: Engineering light-driven micromotors with fluorescent dye coatings for easy detection and tracking
