abstract: The article investigates the use of monoethanolamine-functionalized CuO nanoparticles for enhancing the electrochemical reduction of CO 2 . Key findings include significant improvements in current density and stability over 100 hours.

title: Exploring ssDNA translocation through α-hemolysin using coarse-grained steered molecular dynamics
abstract: Coarse-Grained Steered Molecular Dynamics (SMD) have proven to be successfull to study single-stranded DNA (ssDNA) translocation through biological nanopores, especially alpha-hemolysin (αHL).

title: Unveiling cutting-edge developments: architectures and nanostructured materials for application in optoelectronic artificial synapses
abstract: The significant achievements and progress in the field of biomimetic devices, including breakthroughs in research on neural function, neuron designs, prosthetic synapses, and artificial neural programming.

title: The use of collective variables and enhanced sampling in the simulations of existing and emerging microporous materials
abstract: This review summarizes how enhanced sampling methods are used to investigate the complex properties of microporous materials.

title: External stimuli-triggered photodynamic and sonodynamic therapies in combination with hybrid nanomicelles of ICG@PEP@HA: laser vs. ultrasound
abstract: A type of hybrid nanomicelles (ICG@PEP@HA) for membrane penetration and tumor targeting were fabricated, and a powerful ROS production, superb ROS-induced cell apoptosis and excellent cancerous elimination have been realized in vivo .

title: Scalable solid-state synthesis of 2D transition metal oxide/graphene hybrid materials and their utilization for microsupercapacitors
abstract: A microwave-assisted solid-state synthesis approach for 2D metal oxides and their graphene nanocomposites was developed, and it also shows high potential in energy-storage applications.

title: Carbon carrier-based rapid Joule heating technology: a review on the preparation and applications of functional nanomaterials
abstract: Fundamentals of the CJH technique and its application in preparing a wide range of materials.

title: DNA origami-templated gold nanorod dimer nanoantennas: enabling addressable optical hotspots for single cancer biomarker SERS detection
abstract: Label-free detection of single molecule of epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) is achieved through surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) utilizing precisely engineered DNA origami-templated gold nanorod (Au NR) dimers.

title: Understanding the nanoscale adhesion forces between the fungal pathogen Candida albicans and antimicrobial zinc-based layered double hydroxides using single-cell and single-particle force spectroscopy
abstract: ZnAl LDH NPs showed remarkable antifungal properties against C. albicans fungal cells. The antifungal activity was linked to the adhesion-specific interactions between ZnAl nanoparticles and C. albicans , which were probed by both SPFS and SCFS.

title: Bi-directional charge transfer channels in highly crystalline carbon nitride enabling superior photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Crystalline carbon nitride with bi-directional charge transfer channel (TCCN-K) is prepared. Donor–acceptor units and K intercalation set up a bi-directional charge transfer channel, endowing TCCN-K with a superior photocatalytic hydrogen evolution activity.

title: Dynamic memristor for physical reservoir computing
abstract: This minireview explores the tunable dynamic properties and potential applications of memristor-based physical reservoir computing.

title: Strain-mediated multistate skyrmion for neuron devices
abstract: A novel way to realized the energy-feeicient and high-density neuromorphic computing: Our work proposed a compact IF spiking neuron device by controlling the modalities of skyrmion using voltage-induced strain in multilayer heterostructure.

title: Improved optical quality of heteroepitaxially grown metal–organic framework thin films by modulating the crystal growth
abstract: The high-quality 3D-oriented thin film of metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) with improved optical transparency will pave the way to integrate MOFs into advanced optical and electrical devices.

title: 2D petal-like PdAg nanosheets promote efficient electrocatalytic oxidation of ethanol and methanol
abstract: 2D petal-like PdAg nanosheets with ultrathin 2D structure and jagged edges promote efficient electrocatalytic oxidation of ethanol and methanol.

title: Plasma-based post-processing of colloidal nanocrystals for applications in heterogeneous catalysis
abstract: This review summarizes the work on converting ligand-capped colloidal nanocrystals into active heterogeneous catalysts by using non-thermal plasmas, which remove the ligands and control surface chemistry. Questions for future work are discussed.

title: Revealing microscopic dynamics: in situ liquid-phase TEM for live observations of soft materials and quantitative analysis via deep learning
abstract: By leveraging liquid-phase TEM and deep learning, this work explores quantitative aspects of soft matter dynamics-encompassing phenomena such as surfactant molecule nucleation, polymer self-assembly, and Au nanoparticle ripening in solution.

title: Controlled synthesis and characterization of porous silicon nanoparticles for dynamic nuclear polarization
abstract: Investigating the dynamic nuclear polarization mechanism in controllably synthesized porous Si nanoparticles, we measure the longest relaxation time to date.

title: Tunable Zn 2+ de-solvation behavior in MnO 2 cathodes via self-assembled phytic acid monolayers for stable aqueous Zn-ion batteries
abstract: Special phytic acid (PA) layer facilitates desolvation of zinc ions and protects MnO 2 with excellent electrochemical capacity.

title: Facile green synthesis of wasted hop-based zinc oxide nanozymes as peroxidase-like catalysts for colorimetric analysis
abstract: A facile method producing zinc oxide nanozymes (ZnO NZs) using wasted hop extract (WHE) was demonstrated for the first time.

title: Gradient oxygen doping triggered a microscale built-in electric field in CdIn 2 S 4 for photoelectrochemical water splitting
abstract: Gradient oxygen doping triggered a microscale built-in electric field in CdIn 2 S 4 for highly efficient photoelectrochemical water splitting.

title: Photocatalytic antibacterial agents based on inorganic semiconductor nanomaterials: a review
abstract: A systematic summary of recent disinfection developments based on inorganic nanomaterials, which hold great potential for future photocatalytic antibacterial applications, has been exhibited.

title: Thermally stable Pd/CeO 2 @SiO 2 with a core–shell structure for catalytic lean methane combustion
abstract: The results of activity indicated that Pd/CeO 2 @SiO 2 exhibited the optimal catalytic performance. The remarkable catalytic performance was attributed to the synergistic effect of strengthened metal–support interactions and the core–shell structure.

title: Size and temperature dependent shapes of copper nanocrystals using parallel tempering molecular dynamics
abstract: Parallel-tempering molecular dynamics simulations reveal the temperature- and size-dependent shapes of Cu nanocrystals.

title: Mimicking the extracellular world: from natural to fully synthetic matrices utilizing supramolecular biomaterials
abstract: Different extracellular matrix mimicking hydrogels exist, ranging from fully natural to synthetic to hybrid versions. Herein, supramolecular hydrogels are emerging due to their inherent dynamics, modularity and orthogonal control over properties.

title: First demonstration of 2T0C-FeDRAM: a-ITZO FET and double gate a-ITZO/a-IGZO FeFET with a record-long multibit retention time of >4-bit and >2000 s
abstract: We successfully demonstrated a 2T0C-FeDRAM with a record-long multibit retention time exceeding 2000 seconds and 4-bit (19 states) capability, attributed to a V SN of 0 V leveraging the non-volatile properties of ferroelectrics.

title: Asymmetric ion transport through “Janus” MoSSe sub-nanometer pores
abstract: Janus MoSSe sub-nm pore exhibits polarity-driven ion adsorption and charge-induced transport dynamics, yielding asymmetrical I – V curves, with potential nanofluidics applications.

title: Sculpting liquid metal stabilized interfaces: a gateway to liquid electronics
abstract: Sculpting of Ga NPs stabilized droplet is achieved here by interfacial jamming of metal–phenolate network at oil–water interface for low-voltage electronic applications.

title: A flexible precontact CNT-Al 2 O 3 fiber sensor resistant to extreme temperatures
abstract: By means of atomic layer deposition (ALD) technology, different thicknesses of Al 2 O 3 dielectric layers can be deposited on surface of the carbon nanotube fiber (CNTF) to give it different colors and high temperature resistance sensing properties.

title: Exploring unconventional ferromagnetism in hole-doped LaCrAsO: insights into charge-transfer and magnetic interactions
abstract: Canonical double exchange mechanism at low doping, new mechanism at high doping.

title: Selenium reduction pathways in the colloidal synthesis of CdSe nanoplatelets
abstract: We discuss how elemental selenium can be reduced by solvents that contain CC double bonds or carboxylic acid, and how this impacts the CdSe nanoplatelet synthesis.

title: Tuning the morphology and chemical distribution of Ag atoms in Au rich nanoparticles using electrochemical dealloying
abstract: Dealloying of Ag–Au nanoparticles strongly differs from bulk alloys and is controlled by electrochemical conditions and Au surface atom mobility.

title: Advanced dual-atom catalysts on graphitic carbon nitride for enhanced hydrogen evolution via water splitting
abstract: This study explores novel DACs for enhanced hydrogen evolution using DFT calculations, identifying materials with high electrochemical stability and low energy barriers, paving the way for advanced sustainable energy technologies.

title: Decoupled electron–phonon transport in Ag 2 Se thermoelectric materials through constructing TiO 2 /MoS 2 co-decorated cell-membrane-mimic grain boundaries
abstract: Inspired by the cell membrane with selective permeability, we construct bio-mimic grain boundaries with TiO 2 and MoS 2 co-decoration in Ag 2 Se to decouple electron scattering from strong phonon scattering, increasing the zT value of Ag 2 Se by up to 60%.

title: Extrinsic chirality tailors Stokes parameters in simple asymmetric metasurfaces
abstract: Metasurfaces tailor electromagnetic confinement at the nanoscale and can be appropriately designed for polarization-dependent light–matter interactions.

title: Doxorubicin-loaded DNA origami nanostructures: stability in vitreous and their uptake and toxicity in ocular cells
abstract: Doxorubicin-loaded DNA origami required additional Mg 2+ to be stable in cell medium and vitreous at 37 °C. In retinal epithelial cells (ARPE-19), they were well-tolerated, but limited in their uptake.

title: Accelerated size-focusing light activated synthesis of atomically precise fluorescent Au 22 (Lys–Cys–Lys) 16 clusters
abstract: A new method for light activated size focussing of peptide stabilized gold clusters converting to a single, photocatalytically active cluster.

title: First-principles guidelines to select promising van der Waals materials for hybrid photovoltaic–triboelectric nanogenerators
abstract: Hybrid photovoltaic–triboelectric nanogenerator (PV–TENG) devices are promising multi-energy harvesters. We provide theoretical guidelines to identify TMD chemical compositions for optimal PV–TENG electric power generation.

title: Surface thermodynamics of yttrium titanate pyrochlore nanomaterials
abstract: Critical particle size can be determined with known surface energy. The surface enthalpy of yttrium titanate pyrochlores was determined to be 4.07 ± 0.32 J m −2 by calorimetry, and the lower limit of critical particle size for this is around 5.0 nm.

title: Rapid, in situ synthesis of ultra-small silicon particles for boosted lithium storage capability through ultrafast Joule heating
abstract: We introduce a novel strategy for the in situ , rapid synthesis of ultra-small silicon nanoparticles uniformly embedded within carbonized nanosheets (us-Si/C) through swift high-temperature thermal radiative heating of sizable silicon nanoparticles.

title: An enhanced electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction by the photothermal effect and its induced micro-electric field
abstract: NiS x coupled NiFe(OH) y on nickel foam [NiS x @NiFe(OH) y /NF] was synthesized for the investigation of the photothermal effect and induced micro-electric field-enhanced electrocatalytic oxygen evolution reaction.

title: Schottky barrier reduction on optoelectronic responses in heavy ion irradiated WSe 2 memtransistors
abstract: LRS–LRS switching behaviors under light illumination were achieved in heavy ion irradiated WSe 2 based devices, which was ascribed to the reduction of the Schottky barrier height.

title: Tuning the product selectivity of single-atom catalysts for CO 2 reduction beyond CO formation by orbital engineering
abstract: Orbital engineering by axial ligands offers promising strategies to tune the catalytic activity and product selectivity of single-atom catalysts (SACs) with 3d transition metal dopants (Fe, Co, Ni) for CO2 reduction reaction.

title: Variability in HfO 2 -based memristors described with a new bidimensional statistical technique
abstract: A new two-dimensional statistical technique has been developed to describe cycle-to-cycle variability in resistive memories. A two-dimensional coefficient of variation is introduced to characterize variability in a better manner.

title: Atomically dispersed Co-based species containing electron withdrawing groups for electrocatalytic oxygen reduction reactions
abstract: A new hybrid material, composed of atomically dispersed Co species containing electron-withdrawing CF 3 groups and N-doped reduced graphene oxide, shows excellent electrocatalytic properties for electrochemical oxygen reduction reaction.

title: Nanomaterials in electrochemical nanobiosensors of miRNAs
abstract: Nanomaterial-based miRNA biosensors have received significant attention owing to their unique properties, especially enhanced sensitivity.

title: Digestion of lipid micelles leads to increased membrane permeability
abstract: Our all-atom molecular dynamics simulations characterize the interactions of a short chain lipid and its digestion products with disordered & ordered lipid membranes, and provide a mechanistic understanding of the resulting permeability enhancement.

title: Fabrication of a wearable and foldable photodetector based on a WSe 2 -MXene 2D–2D heterostructure using a scalable handprint technique
abstract: Several studies on semiconductor material-based single-band, high-performance photosensitive, and chemically stable photodetectors are available; however, the lack of broad spectral response, device flexibility, and biodegradability prevents them from being used in wearable and flexible electronics.

title: Deciphering the mechanistic insights of 4-nitrophenol reduction catalyzed by a 1D–2D Bi 2 S 3 nanostructured catalyst
abstract: An anisotropic 1D-2D Bi 2 S 3 nanostructured catalyst is developed for 4-nitrophenol (4-NP) reduction and highlighted the critical roles of water as proton source and adsorbed/absorbed hydrogen species on the catalyst surface in 4-NP reduction.

title: Electrical transport phenomena in two-dimensional metallic 2H-NbSe 2 : an experimental and theoretical study
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) metallic transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDCs) have attracted extensive interest in various fields owing to their unique electronic properties.

title: Multi-MXene assisted large-scale manufacturing of electrochemical biosensors based on enzyme-nanoflower enhanced electrodes for the detection of H 2 O 2 secreted from live cancer cells
abstract: A strategy of large-scale manufacturing of electrochemical biosensors for early cancer diagnosis based on HRP nanoflower-enhanced MXene/CMCS composite ink electrodes via a user-friendly screen-printing procedure.

title: Bridging colloidal and electrochemical syntheses of metal nanocrystals with seeded electrodeposition for tracking single nanocrystal growth
abstract: Metal nanocrystals (NCs) with different structural features are produced by seeded-electrodeposition.

title: Metallopeptide nanoreservoirs for concurrent imaging and detoxification of lead (Pb) from human retinal pigment epithelial (hRPE1) cells
abstract: We engineered self-assembling nanostructures for precise lead quantification and therapeutic potential in intracellular lead aggregation, advancing biotechnological applications in materials science.

title: Effect of ABX 3 site changes on the performance of tin–lead mixed perovskite solar cells
abstract: The advantage of tin–lead mixed perovskite solar cells is that the ABX3 site can be adjusted to approach the Shockley–Queisser limit for photovoltaic applications, and it have been rapidly developed and achieved a PCE of 23.7%.

title: An electrografted monolayer of polyaniline as a tuneable platform for a glucose biosensor
abstract: Vertically Aligned Conducting Polymers: The article emphasizes the fabrication of an electrografted vertically oriented polyaniline and its application in a highly sensitive electrochemical glucose enzymatic biosensor utilizing this monolayer.

title: The enhanced electrocatalytic performance of nanoscopic Cu 6 Pd 12 Fe 12 heterometallic molecular box encaged cytochrome c
abstract: Privileged to be Encaged: Encaging Cytochrome c into nanoscopic Cu 6 Pd 12 Fe 12 heterometallic molecular boxes significantly enhances its electrochemical stability and electrocatalytic performance toward nitrite ion sensing and Oxygen Reduction Reaction.

title: In situ synergistic halogen passivation of semiconducting PbS quantum dot inks for efficient photovoltaics
abstract: An in situ mixed halogen passivation (MHP) strategy is reported in the direct synthesis of semiconducting PbS CQD inks, resulting in the optimal surface configuration and passivation.

title: Femtosecond laser direct writing Bombyx mori sericin-based nanocomposites into intrinsically soft wearable micro-/nano-electronics
abstract: Here, we fabricated intrinsically flexible and soft wearable micro/nano-electronics for distinguishing human-body movements, based on femtosecond-laser-induced photoreduction of metal ions into nanoparticles by sericin and their nano-compositing.

title: Achieving a balance of rapid Zn 2+ desolvation and hydrogen evolution reaction inertia at the interface of the Zn anode
abstract: It is difficult to achieve fast kinetics of Zn 2+ (H 2 O) 6 desolvation as well as HER inertia at the same electrolyte/Zn interface during long-term cycling of Zn plating/stripping in aqueous Zn-ion batteries.

title: Numerical modeling and in situ small angle X-ray scattering characterization of ultra-small SPION magnetophoresis in a high field and gradient separator
abstract: Ultra-small SPIONs were successfully separated using high magnetic fields and gradients. In s itu SAXS and numerical simulation were used to investigate the magnetophoresis process.

title: Bright near-infrared emission from the Au 39 (SR) 29 nanocluster
abstract: A highly luminescent Au 39 (SR) 29 nanocluster is obtained, with its emission at 915 nm and 19% quantum yield in solution and 32% in films at room temperature.

title: Tunable abundant valley Hall effect and chiral spin–valley locking in Janus monolayer VCGeN 4
abstract: Based on the first-principles calculations, when the magnetization is along out-of-plane, the intriguing spontaneous valley polarization exists in the Janus monolayer VCGeN 4 , and the tunable abundant valley Hall effect can be found.

title: Photochemical synthesis, characterization, and electrochemical sensing properties of CD–AuNP nanohybrids
abstract: Carbon dots (CDs) have been prepared from olive solid wastes and employed as capping agents in photo-activated gold-nanoparticles synthesis, thus creating CD–Au nanohybrids which act as an efficient material for electrochemical sensing hydroquinone.

title: Recent advances in the role of interfacial liquids in electrochemical reactions
abstract: This minireview examines how the interfacial liquid, comprising water molecules and ionic species, profoundly impacts key electrochemical reactions: HER, OER/ORR, and CO 2 RR/CORR.

title: Determining the orderliness of carbon materials with nanoparticle imaging and explainable machine learning
abstract: We propose a set of features for the ordered arrangement of palladium nanoparticles that are consistent with the intuitive understanding of researchers and allow quantification of the data in terms of easily interpretable physical parameters.

title: Microstructure evolution and the deformation mechanism in nanocrystalline superior-deformed tantalum
abstract: The temperature-controlled relationship between the mechanical properties and deformation mechanism of tantalum (Ta) enables the extension of its application potential in various areas of life, including energy and electronics industries.

title: Control of metal–support interaction for tunable CO hydrogenation performance over Ru/TiO 2 nanocatalysts
abstract: Ru/TiO 2 nanocatalysts could be modified by tailoring the SMSI state through changing the crystal phase of the TiO 2 support and a corresponding thermal treatment strategy to enhance FTO performance.

title: A comprehensive review on singlet oxygen generation in nanomaterials and conjugated polymers for photodynamic therapy in the treatment of cancer
abstract: Photodynamic Therapy (PDT): A minimally invasive treatment for a range of benign disorders and malignancies.

title: Engineered nanomicelles targeting proliferation and angiogenesis inhibit tumour progression by impairing the synthesis of ceramide-1-phosphate
abstract: Tumour cells secrete various proangiogenic factors like VEGF, PDGF, and EGF that result in the formation of highly vascularized tumours with an immunosuppressive tumour microenvironment.

title: Enzyme-mimic catalytic activities and biomedical applications of noble metal nanoclusters
abstract: This review delves into enzyme-mimic catalysis of metal nanoclusters based on a hierarchical structure scheme reminiscent of proteins. Biomedical applications established by the enzyme-mimic catalysis of clusters have also been outlined.

title: Exploring the potential of CuCoFeTe@CuCoTe yolk-shelled microrods in supercapacitor applications
abstract: The CuCoFeTe@CuCoTe yolk-shelled microrods are synthesized for supercapacitors.

title: Carbon-supported Au 25 cluster catalysts partially decorated with dendron thiolates: enhanced loading weight and durability for hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The loading amount and durability of the carbon-supported Au 25 cluster catalyst were successfully enhanced by introducing bulky, dendron thiolates, due to (1) strong non-covalent interactions between ligands and support, and (2) steric repulsion.

title: Preparation and application of high thermal conductivity phase change microcapsules with fluorescence characteristics based on a ZnWO 4 shell
abstract: This method produces microcapsules that possess a high thermal rate and good UV absorption and fluorescence properties, making them suitable for use in fluorescent buildings, textiles, coatings, and other applications.

title: Elevated temperature-driven coordinative reconstruction of an unsaturated single-Ni-atom structure with low valency on a polymer-derived matrix for the electrolytic oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Elevated temperature-driven pyrolysis can generate coordinatively unsaturated NiSA-NC-900, crucial for the oxygen evolution process in water splitting, through coordination rebuilding and N-loss processes.

title: The effect of sulfuration reaction rates with sulphur concentration gradient dependence on the growth pattern and morphological evolution of MoS 2 in laminar flow
abstract: Space-uniform MoS 2 limited by surface chemistry can grow through CVD within an underdeveloped laminar flow boundary layer, with crystal growth patterns being governed by the growth rate.

title: Assembling Fe 4 single-molecule magnets on a TiO 2 monolayer
abstract: A monolayer of tetrairon( iii ) (Fe 4 ) molecules, featuring single-molecule magnet (SMM) behavior, is used to decorate a TiO 2 film. We evaluated the modifications occurring at the interface, a crucial issue for future applications of SMM materials.

title: Superalkali halide perovskites with suitable direct band gaps for photovoltaic applications
abstract: Li(H 2 O) 3 + is introduced into 3D Sn/PbI 3 frames. Li(H 2 O) 3 MI 3 perovskites have direct band gaps and excellent electron transitions. PCE of 33.49% was obtained for the cubic Li(H 2 O) 3 PbI 3 perovskite.

title: High yield exfoliation of sub-micron hexagonal boron nitride using a solvent free method
abstract: Schematic of intercalation and exfoliation of submicron hBN.

title: A self-aligned assembling terahertz metasurface microfluidic sensor for liquid detection
abstract: Compared with some recently related work, the three key indicators ( S , Q -factor, and the FOM) of the proposed THz metasurface microfluidic sensor are increased by 40%, 3–5 times, and 5 times, respectively.

title: Performance optimization and comparison of vertical motion-based triboelectric nanogenerators
abstract: Output performance and comparison of vertical motion-based triboelectric nanogenerators based on a novel sandwich-structured dielectric layer and a DC-TENG.

title: Self-templated fabrication of P-doped CoMoO 4 –Co 3 O 4 hollow nanocages for the efficient oxygen evolution reaction
abstract: Finding reservoir-rich and efficient electrocatalysts for the alkaline oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is crucial for further sustainable energy development.

title: Effects of the surface energy and surface stress on the phase stability of spin crossover nano-objects: a thermodynamic approach
abstract: Calculated temperature dependence of the high spin fraction for spin crossover thin films. All input parameters are measurable physical quantities.

title: An atomistic scale simulation study of structural properties in the silk–fibrohexamerin complex
abstract: This study analyzed the interfacial/mechanical properties of N -glycan engineered P25-Silkworm silk. The results confirmed that N -glycan in orders of the mannose molecules increased the hydrogen bonding of the amorphous domain.

title: Correction: pH-Responsive fluorescent graphene quantum dots for fluorescence-guided cancer surgery and diagnosis
abstract: Correction for ‘pH-Responsive fluorescent graphene quantum dots for fluorescence-guided cancer surgery and diagnosis’ by Zetan Fan et al. , Nanoscale , 2017, 9 , 4928–4933, https://doi.org/10.1039/C7NR00888K.

title: Modulating luminescence through anion variation in lead-free Cs 2 NaInX 6 (X = Cl, Br, and I) perovskites: a first-principles study
abstract: The luminescence performance of A 2 B + B′ 3+ X 6 -type lead-free halide perovskite Cs 2 NaInCl 6 can be effectively modulated and enhanced through anion variation.

title: Oxidation-induced modulation of photoresponsivity in monolayer MoS 2 with sulfur vacancies
abstract: This study investigates how sulfur vacancies modulate the photoresponsivity of monolayer MoS 2 in oxidizing environments, revealing critical role of oxygen in defect dynamics and offering insights for developing robust 2D material-based electronics.

title: Low-temperature synthesis of porous organic polymers with donor–acceptor structure and β-ketoenamine for photocatalytic oxidative coupling of amines
abstract: In light of the widespread use of fossil fuels and the resulting environmental pollution, it is crucial to develop efficient photocatalysts for renewable energy applications that utilize visible light.

title: N-Heterocyclic carbene-stabilized gold–copper nanoclusters: synthesis, bonding and mechanochromism
abstract: The first gold–copper alloy cluster protected by both NHC and alkyne ligands was reported. The cluster displays interesting mechanochromic properties, highlighting the bright future of NHC-functionalized metal nanoclusters in materials science.

title: Opportunities and challenges in perovskite–organic thin-film tandem solar cells
abstract: In this work, we focus on perovskite–organic tandem solar cells (PO TSCs) with advantages in device performance, manufacturing cost, and diverse applications and conclude the critical factors influencing the future development of PO TSCs.

title: Rapid fabrication of tunable structural color patterns by spray-coating
abstract: We propose a simple spray-coating process that enables the control of both ordered and disordered structures, with tunable optical properties, suitable for colloidal patterning on various substrates.

title: Cooperative dynamics of DNA-grafted magnetic nanoparticles optimize magnetic biosensing and coupling to DNA origami
abstract: Single-stranded DNA on MNPs shows a transition from being coiled to being brushed out at low and high grafting densities, respectively. Thereby, MNPs exhibit cooperative dynamics, which is highly relevant for magnetic biosensing and binding to DNA origami.

title: Advancing elastomer performance with dynamic bond networks in polymer-grafted single-chain nanoparticles: a molecular dynamics exploration
abstract: This study explores how varying quantities and spatial distributions of functional groups on grafted polymer chains influence the mechanical properties of nanocomposites.

title: Enhanced double resonance Raman scattering in multilayer graphene with broadband coherent anti-Stokes Raman spectroscopy
abstract: Using broadband coherent anti-Stokes Raman spectroscopy, enhanced double resonance Raman LOZO' mode in multilayer graphene is observed with an intensity ratio to G mode significantly greater than standard Raman due to single pulse amplification.

title: Size-tunable silicon nanoparticles synthesized in solution via a redox reaction
abstract: A new liquid-phase redox synthesis to form silicon nanoparticles allows the solvent used during synthesis to determine particle size and fluorescent properties.

title: A single-gold-atom addition regulates sharp redshift in the fluorescence of atomically precise nanoclusters
abstract: This work provides an ideal platform to study the significant impact of small differences in the core structures of fluorescents with as little as one atom changes on their optical properties.

title: Titania (TiO 2 ) nanotube surfaces doped with zinc and strontium for improved cell compatibility
abstract: Titanium-based orthopedic implants are gaining popularity in recent years due to their excellent biocompatibility, superior corrosion resistance and lightweight properties.

title: Differences in interaction of graphene/graphene oxide with bacterial and mammalian cell membranes
abstract: Weak bonding among bacteria phospholipids and less repulsive force when graphene materials approach, result in graphene materials interacting differently with the bacteria compared to mammalian cells.

title: Protein-guided biomimetic nanomaterials: a versatile theranostic nanoplatform for biomedical applications
abstract: This article reviews the general principles and strategies for constructing protein-mineralized nanomaterials (PMNMs). Subsequently, the progress of PMNMs in bioimaging, anti-tumor, anti-bacterial, and anti-inflammatory therapies is summarized.

title: Advances in zeolitic-imidazolate-framework-based catalysts for photo-/electrocatalytic water splitting, CO 2 reduction and N 2 reduction applications
abstract: Characteristics and physiochemical advantages of ZIFs are reviewed with promising regulatory approaches for advancing the kinetic processes in emerging photo/electrocatalytic water splitting, CO 2 reduction and N 2 reduction applications.

title: Transforming friction: unveiling sliding-induced phase transitions in CVD-grown WS 2 monolayers under single-asperity sliding nanocontacts
abstract: 1H and 1T′ phases of WS 2 monolayers were studied using AFM. The 1H phase shows higher friction (∼52%) than the 1T′ phase. Notably, friction of 1H phase was reduced to friction measured on 1T′ phase due to the lateral strain induced by sliding AFM tip.

title: Platinum-based chemotherapy: trends in organic nanodelivery systems
authors: Luís A. E. Batista de Carvalho
abstract: This review explores organic nanodelivery systems in the development of platinum-based anticancer treatments, highlighting benefits, challenges, and potential for groundbreaking therapies.

title: Understanding magnetic hyperthermia performance within the “Brezovich criterion”: beyond the uniaxial anisotropy description
abstract: Our study shows that intrinsic magnetocrystalline anisotropy plays a critical role in magnetic hyperthermia. Results indicate that even small deviations from spherical shape have an important impact on optimizing SLP.

title: Insights into the formation of CdSe nanoplatelets using a flow reactor
abstract: A novel synthesis to produce CdSe nanoplatelets in a flow reactor is introduced. Furthermore, the connection between CdSe magic-size clusters and the growth of these anisotropic nanoplatelets is investigated.

title: Caged gold nanostars: a novel plasmonic nanoplatform with potential theranostic applications
abstract: Here we introduce caged gold nanostars, a novel nanoplasmonic platform that integrates the tunable optical properties of gold nanostars with hollow, loadable core–shell structures.

title: Accelerating the prediction of inorganic surfaces with machine learning interatomic potentials
abstract: This minireview summarizes recent applications of machine learning interatomic potentials for predicting the stability and structures of solid-state surfaces.

title: Cell membrane coated nanoparticles: cutting-edge drug delivery systems for osteoporosis therapy
abstract: The review delves into exploring diverse cellular sources for coating nanoparticles to treat osteoporosis. Furthermore, it highlights the pivotal role of these nanoparticles in restoring the immune microenvironment, offering promising avenues for combating osteoporosis.

title: Self-assembled π-conjugated chromophores: preparation of one- and two-dimensional nanostructures and their use in photocatalysis
abstract: Nanostructures obtained from self-assembled organic dyes are emerging as alternatives to molecular and semiconductor photocatalysts. With their unique properties, they offer a promising way to bridge homo- and heterogeneous (photo)catalysis.

title: Reactivity-matched synthesis of monodisperse Ag(In,Ga)S 2 QDs with efficient luminescence
abstract: We present a method of preparing highly efficient monodisperse Ag(In,Ga)S 2 , which suppresses the activity mismatch of multiple elements by using InI 3 . The PLQY value reaches 90% at 530 nm with an optimum FWHM of 31 nm by cladding the shell layer.

title: Nature-inspired protein mineralization strategies for nanoparticle construction: advancing effective cancer therapy
abstract: The biomimetic mineralization strategy based on natural proteins and metal ions provides an effective method for constructing nanoparticles. By regulating the proportion of ingredients, the precise control assembly of nanoparticles can be achieved.

title: Autonomous nanomanufacturing of lead-free metal halide perovskite nanocrystals using a self-driving fluidic lab
abstract: We present a self-driving fluidic lab for accelerated synthesis science studies of lead-free metal halide perovskite nanocrystals.

title: Advancements in mercury detection using surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy (SERS) and ion-imprinted polymers (IIPs): a review
abstract: A comprehensive review on SERS and imprinted polymer platforms in mercury detection, and a perspective on integrating the two for an improved analytical method.

title: Destructive quantum interference in meta -oligo(phenyleneethynylene) molecular wires with gold–graphene heterojunctions
abstract: Quantum interference effects on molecular conductance can be regulated by changing anchoring groups and electrode materials.

title: Enhancing ammonium-ion storage in Mo-doped VO 2 (B) nanobelt-bundles anode for aqueous ammonium-ion batteries
abstract: The introduction of molybdenum (Mo) atoms into the VO 2 (B) material can optimize its electronic structure and morphology, thereby improving the storage capacity and cycling stability of the resulting aqueous ammonium ion rechargeable batteries.

title: Fe-doped Cu-based bimetallic metal–organic frameworks as nanoscale microwave sensitizers for enhancing microwave thermal and dynamic therapy for hepatocellular carcinoma
abstract: Illustration of FCM nanoparticles synthesis and its anticancer mechanism, highlighting its high microwave sensitivity and thermal conversion efficiency for synergistic microwave thermal therapy (MTT) and microwave dynamic therapy (MDT).

title: Biosensors for detection of airborne pathogenic fungal spores: a review
abstract: An overview of biosensors for airborne pathogenic fungal spore detection.

title: A wearable AuNP enhanced metal–organic gel (Au@MOG) sensor for sweat glucose detection with ultrahigh sensitivity
abstract: The demands for sensitive, non-invasive and real-time glucose monitoring have grown considerably recently. This work has developed a AuNPs@MOGs-based wearable sensor for sweat glucose detection with ultrahigh sensitivity and wide linear detection.

title: Overcoming Debye screening effect in field-effect transistors for enhanced biomarker detection sensitivity
abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive review of the latest research advancements in overcoming the Debye screening effect in field-effect transistor (FET) sensors.

title: Assessing the overflowing pile-up effect on the photoluminescence lifetime of nanomaterials
abstract: A new insight for accurately interpreting the excited state lifetime has been provided, which is highly misunderstood due to the incomplete decay in heterogeneous luminescent nanomaterials.

title: Non-metallic plasmon-assisted upconversion fluorescence for ultrasensitive hydrogen peroxide detection from nM to μM
abstract: Non-metallic plasmon-assisted upconversion fluorescence for ultrasensitive and quantitative detection of hydrogen peroxide from nM to μM.

title: Kinetics of spontaneous phase transitions from wüstite to magnetite in superparamagnetic core–shell nanocubes of iron oxides
abstract: The spontaneous wüstite-to-magnetite phase transition in 23 nm nanocubes was investigated by time-resolved XRD. By using three coupled reaction–diffusion master equations, we created a prediction model capable of showing good agreement with the experimental data.

title: Enhancing the electronic structure of Ni-based electrocatalysts through N element substitution for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The notion of orbital-regulated electronic levels on Ni sites introduces a distinctive methodology for the systematic development of catalysts used in hydrogen evolution and other applications.

title: PExM: polyplex expansion microscopy for cell trafficking studies
abstract: Polymeric nanoparticles expansion microscopy scheme. Left: cells with no expansion in where individual nanoparticles visualization is complicated. Right: system expand allowing visualization of individual nanoparticles inside cells.

title: A controllable interlayer shielding effect in twisted multilayer graphene quantum dots
abstract: Effect of stacking layers, disc size, twist angle and vertical electric field on the shielding and CT effects of twisted multilayer graphene quantum dots.

title: Atomically precise rhodium–indium carbonyl nanoclusters: synthesis, characterization, crystal structure and electron-sponge features
abstract: The reactivity of [Rh 7 (CO) 16 ] 3− with InCl 3 led to the isolation of three new clusters, including [Rh 12 In(CO) 28 ] 3− , which extends the [Rh 12 E(CO) 27 ] n − series (E = Sn, Ge, Sb, Bi). Its multivalence has been experimentally and theoretically unravelled.

title: Controllable synthesis of hollow mesoporous organosilica nanoparticles with pyridine-2,6-bis-imidazolium frameworks for CO 2 conversion
abstract: The effects of a hard template, stepwise addition of precursors, and Si/CTAB and organosilica/TEOS molar ratios have been systematically studied during the synthesis of hollow mesoporous organosilica nanoparticles with pyridine-bis-imidazolium units.

title: Structure evolution and specific effects for the catalysis of atomically ordered intermetallic compounds
abstract: Structure evolution and specific effects of atomically ordered intermetallic compounds (IMCs) have been extensively studied for exploring catalysts with high activity, selectivity, and longevity.

title: Unraveling the excitonics of light emission from metal-halide perovskite quantum dots
abstract: Light emission in metal halide perovskite nanocrystal quantum dots arises from multiexcitons that can be resolved in time. These electronic excitations coupled with the lattice and support spatial coherence.

title: Metal chalcogenide nanorings for temperature–strain dual-mode sensing
abstract: Sn 0.2 Mo 0.8 S 2 nanorings with a mixed 1T/2H phase were synthesized by etching SnS 2 cores from Sn 1− x Mo x S 2 /SnS 2 lateral heterostructures. Flexible electronic sensors based on these nanorings exhibited excellent temperature and strain sensing performance.

title: High-load nanoparticles with a chemotherapeutic SN-38/FdUMP drug cocktail
abstract: A cocktail of chemotherapeutic SN-38 and 5-FU/FdUMP in [Gd(OH)] 2+ [(SN-38) 0.5 (FdUMP) 0.5 ] 2− inorganic-organic hybrid nanoparticles shows promising efficacy on mouse pancreatic cancer cell lines.

title: Functionalization of three-dimensional epitaxial graphene with metal nanoparticles
abstract: We report a new technique for fabricating metal-functionalized three-dimensional epitaxial graphene on porous SiC. The process is clean and scalable. The fabricated material exhibits high chemical and thermal stability, and versatility.

title: The interplay of chromophore–spacer length in light-induced gold nanocluster self-assembly
abstract: The chromophore-spacer length controls the cis-trans isomerization of azobenzene ligands in precision nanoclusters and regulates the kinetics of self-assembled superstructure formation.

title: Magnetic dimeric metal–porphyrin rings mechanically bonded around carbon nanotubes: the role of nanotube defects modulating magnetic properties
abstract: A DFT study of mechanically bonded magnetic Co 2+ and Cu 2+ dimeric metal–porphyrin rings around carbon nanotubes (mMINTs) points to defcts in the NT as responsible for the different dimer–nanotube interactions and the loss of the Co 2+ EPR signal.

title: Analytical method for the determination of the absorption coefficient of DNA-stabilized silver nanoclusters
abstract: A new analytical method to determine the absorption coefficient of DNA-stabilized silver nanoclusters by combining UV-Vis spectroscopy, mass spectrometry, and inductively coupled plasma-optical emission spectrometry.

title: Few-layered graphene increases the response of nociceptive neurons to irritant stimuli
abstract: Few-layered graphene (FLG) treatment increases the functional response of the nociceptive subpopulation of sensory neurons of the dorsal root ganglia (DRG) in response to irritants specific for chemoreceptors TRPV1 and TRPA1.

title: Investigation and development of photocathodes using polyaniline Encapsulated Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene nanosheets for dye-sensitized solar cells
abstract: Polyaniline (PANI) modified two-dimensional Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene composites (PANI-Ti 3 C 2 T x ) as photocathodes for dye-sensitized solar cells (DSSCs).

title: Doping-mediated excited state dynamics of diphosphine-protected M@Au 12 (M = Au, Ir) superatom nanoclusters
abstract: Strong core–shell (Ir–Au n ) electronic coupling significantly expands the energy gap, resulting in a weak nonadiabatic coupling matrix element, which in turn increases the carrier lifetime.

title: Three-dimensional N-doped carbon nanosheets loaded with heterostructured Ni/Ni 3 ZnC 0.7 nanoparticles for selective and efficient CO 2 reduction
abstract: A Ni/Ni 3 ZnC 0.7 -NC catalyst with heterojunctions of Ni and Ni 3 ZnC 0.7 was synthesized by controlling the annealing temperature, demonstrating enormous potential in efficient CO 2 conversion and Zn–CO 2 energy storage applications.

title: Mo-X 4 (X = O, NH and S)-mediated triphenylene-based two-dimensional carbon-rich conjugate frameworks for an efficient nitrogen reduction reaction
abstract: The electrocatalytic nitrogen reduction reaction (NRR) activities of two-dimensional carbon-rich conjugate frameworks (2D-CCFs) can be regulated by changing the atoms X (X = O, NH and S) in the Mo-X 4 motifs.

title: Rethinking the stability of metal nanoclusters: the individual versus the collective
abstract: This work demonstrated that the stability of a nanocluster-based crystalline system should be assessed from both the cluster individual and the cluster collective aspects.

title: Exploring nitrogen-mediated effects on Fe and Cu cluster development in graphene: a DFT study
abstract: The controlled growth and stability of transition metal clusters on N-doped materials have become the subject of intense investigation for unveiling comprehension on the cluster growth evolution.

title: Maximizing liposome tumor delivery by hybridizing with tumor-derived extracellular vesicles
abstract: This investigation highlights the maximizing of nanoparticle tumor delivery by hybridizing nanoparticles with tumor-derived-extracellular vesicles.

title: CsZnPbBr 3 /ZnS core/shell perovskite nanocrystals for stable and efficient white light-emitting diodes
abstract: CsZnPbBr 3 /ZnS core/shell nanocrystals were synthesized by combining alloyed cores to stabilize the internal lattice with OAmBr to protect the outer labile surface of cores, rendering them efficient and stable emitters for white light-emitting diodes.

title: Emergence of quasi-1D spin-polarized states in ultrathin Bi films on InAs(111)A for spintronics applications
abstract: Submonolayer Bi film grown on the InAs(111)A surface demonstrates quasi-1D and Rashba spin-polarized states characterizing the system as an advanced spintronics material fabricated in the ultimate 2D limit.

title: Lysosome passivation triggered by silver nanoparticles enhances subcellular-targeted drug therapy
abstract: Lysosome passivation strategy for enhancing subcellular-targeted drug therapy.

title: Ru doping and interface engineering synergistically boost the electrocatalytic performance of a WP/WP 2 nanosheet array for an efficient hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The surface electronic structure and morphology of catalysts have a crucial impact on the electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction performance.

title: A fast response and highly sensitive flexible humidity sensor based on a nanocomposite film of MoS 2 and graphene oxide
abstract: A flexible humidity sensor based on MoS 2 /GO was realized with a high responsivity of 98%, a fast response/recovery time of 1.3/12.1 s, and excellent stability and linearity. It was applied for human respiration and fingertip proximity detection.

title: Probing geometry-induced magnetic defects in cylindrical modulated nanowires with optically detected spin resonance in nitrogen-vacancy center in diamond
abstract: We report on magnetic imaging capability with non-perturbative field distribution mapping and minimally invasive magnetic sensing using scanning nitrogen-vacancy magnetometry in axial magnetized nanowires.

title: Recent advances in graphene-based electroanalytical devices for healthcare applications
abstract: Recent advances in graphene-based electroanalytical biodevices: different methods for graphene synthesis, functionalization, device fabrication, and transduction mechanisms are discussed for various healthcare applications.

title: PSMA-targeted dendrimer as an efficient anticancer drug delivery vehicle for prostate cancer
abstract: We present PD-CTT1298 nanoplatform as a versatile approach for selective systemic delivery of high payloads of potent chemotherapeutics to PSMA (+) prostate cancer, where dose related systemic side-effects are a major concern.

title: Blue emission-dominated NaYbF 4 @NaYF 4 :2%Ho@NaYF 4 upconversion nanoparticles for detecting ascorbic acid
abstract: Schematic illustration of blue emission-dominated NaYbF 4 @NaYF 4 :2%Ho@NaYF 4 (CSS) design and CSS/MnO 2 -based ascorbic acid detection.

title: Three-layered nanoplates and amorphous/crystalline interface synergism boost CO 2 photoreduction on bismuth oxychloride nanospheres
abstract: Three-layered nanoplates and amorphous/crystalline interfaces in BiOCl nanospheres synergistically accelerate the charge carrier kinetics and exploit the active sites to photoreduce CO 2 -to-CO with high activity.

title: Orientation and morphology control in acid-catalyzed covalent organic framework thin films
abstract: Wafer-scale, high quality crystalline covalent organic framework thin films were synthesized via a liquid–liquid interfacial synthesis approach with strict morphological and orientation control.

title: Revealing nanoscale structure and interfaces of protein and polymer condensates via cryo-electron microscopy
abstract: This study uses cryo-electron microscopy to reveal the distinct nanoscale structures within protein condensates, highlighting the potential correlation between their internal organization and material properties.

title: Coatable strain sensors for nonplanar surfaces
abstract: A versatile coatable strain (CS) sensor was developed using a wet process, achieving a high sensitivity (GF ≈ 100). It overcame traditional polymer limitations, enabling real-time growth measurements on complex biological and non-planar surfaces.

title: Influence of molecular structure on the coupling strength to a plasmonic nanoparticle and hot carrier generation
abstract: By selecting appropriately the orientation of an asymmetric molecule in a strongly coupled plasmon–molecule system, the coupling strength can be significantly increased, modifying the energy spectrum and spatial distribution of hot charge carriers.

title: Optimal bilayer composites for temperature-tracking wireless electronics
abstract: The study presents a cost-effective temperature sensing solution by embedding wireless electronics in a multi-layered elastomeric composite, to concurrently mitigate thermal transience from air and maximize thermal conductivity from the skin.

title: Luminescent metal nanoclusters and their application in bioimaging
abstract: This review focuses on the structure–property correlation and bioimaging application of luminescent metal nanoclusters, which feature atomically precise structures, diverse optical properties and excellent biocompatibility.

title: Machine-learning-guided quantitative delineation of cell morphological features and responses to nanomaterials
abstract: This work shows the integration of machine learning with label-free phase-contrast or brightfield microscopy imaging to quantitatively delineate multiple cell types, phenotypes, and states for monitoring live cell responses to external stimuli.

title: Magnetic aerogels from FePt and CoPt 3 directly from organic solution
abstract: Schematic overview of the aerogel synthesis from iron platinunm and cobalt platinum nanoparticles.

title: Carbon materials and their metal composites for biomedical applications: A short review
abstract: Carbon materials and their hybrid metal composites have garnered significant attention in biomedical applications due to their exceptional biocompatibility.

title: Formation and properties of spindle-shaped aragonite mesocrystals from Mg-bearing solutions
abstract: Spindle-shaped aragonite mesocrystals form by the attachment of Mg-bearing amorphous calcium carbonate particles that convert to aragonite nanocrystals through dissolution-reprecipitation.

title: Effect of two-fold single-atom substitutions (S, Se; C, N) in band gap engineered donor–acceptor conjugated microporous polymers on the efficient aerobic photooxidation of aryl sulfides
abstract: Substitution of S with more metallic Se in D–A CMPs improved the photocatalytic detoxification of sulfur mustards with up to 97% yield, in contrast to a second substitution of C with a more electronegative N.

title: Valley polarization and magnetic anisotropy of two-dimensional Ni 2 Cl 3 I 3 /MoSe 2 heterostructures
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) Janus trihalides have attracted widespread attention due to their potential applications in spintronics.

title: Recent advances in batch production of transfer-free graphene
abstract: This review discusses recent advances in batch production of transfer-free graphene, presenting the key challenges and outlining the future directions.

title: A flexible capacitive pressure sensor with a hybrid porous PDMS/SA hydrogel structure for touch/pain detection
abstract: Mimicking the skin with tactile perception to distinguish noxious pressure stimuli is challenging. Our flexible capacitive pressure sensor employs two working mechanisms according to the pressure stimuli range for both touch and pain detection.

title: Effects of bromine-containing counterion salts in directing the structures of medium-sized silver nanoclusters
abstract: The effect of bromine-containing salts in directing the structures of silver nanoclusters was investigated, and three clusters with medium-sized metallic kernels, Ag 52 , Ag 54 , and Ag 58 , were controllably synthesized and structurally determined.

title: A doping strategy to regulate the adsorption energy of Li 2 S 4 and Li 2 S to promote sulfur reduction on Chevrel phase Mo 6 Se 8 in lithium–sulfur batteries
abstract: The reaction mechanism of sulfur reduction reaction on metal doped Mo 6 Se 8 system and the linear relationship between the overpotential and adsorption energy of Li 2 S and Li 2 S 4 were studied.

title: Dynamic 3D in vitro lung models: applications of inorganic nanoparticles for model development and characterization
abstract: In this review, the authors elaborate on the most recent advances of lung in vitro models and inform on the potential of nanoparticles as a means to create dynamic models as well as nanoparticle-based characterization strategies.

title: Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy, another arrow in the arsenal to study the biodegradability of two-dimensional materials
abstract: Carbon nitrides represent an emerging class of two-dimensional materials exploited for energy storage, for catalysis, in composites, and in biomedicine. Their biodegradability has been studied under the conditions of the photo-Fenton reaction.

title: Selective photocatalytic C–C coupling of benzyl alcohol into hydrobenzoin using Pt-deposited CdS nanosheets passivated with cysteamine
abstract: We demonstrate selective photocatalytic synthesis of hydrobenzoin via the C–C coupling of benzyl alcohol using Pt/CdS nanosheets.

title: Biomarker-triggered, spatiotemporal controlled DNA nanodevice simultaneous assembly and disassembly
abstract: A versatile technique that enabled simultaneous assembly and disassembly of homogeneous DNA nanodevices was reported. The DNA nanodevices assembly and disassembly process can realize the sequential detection and imaging of a variety of intracellular biomarkers.

title: Efficient reduction of CO 2 and inhibition of hydrogen precipitation by polyoxometalate photocatalyst modified with the metal Mn
abstract: A vanadium borate cluster was designed by embedding the V12 cluster in the B18 ring and the transition metal Mn on the periphery of the B18 ring for photocatalytic CO 2 RR.

title: Laser patterning captured in real-time: surface modifications of multilayer thin-films under nanosecond laser heating
abstract: Rapid formation and evolution of nanoscale wrinkling in multilayer thin films was captured using ultrafast transmission electron microscopy.

title: Retraction: An MSN-PEG-IP drug delivery system and IL13Rα2 as targeted therapy for glioma
abstract: Retraction of ‘An MSN-PEG-IP drug delivery system and IL13Rα2 as targeted therapy for glioma’ by Jinlong Shi et al. , Nanoscale , 2017, 9 , 8970–8981, https://doi.org/10.1039/C6NR08786H.

title: Correction: Phase behavior of patchy colloids confined in patchy porous media
abstract: Correction for ‘Phase behavior of patchy colloids confined in patchy porous media’ by Yurij V. Kalyuzhnyi et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, 16 , 4668–4677, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3NR02866F.

title: An instantaneous self-powered wireless sensing system based on a triboelectric nanogenerator and the human body
abstract: This study harnesses the human body as both an energy source and a transmission medium for wearable sensors, achieving instantaneous wireless sensing over 8 meters.

title: On the unique temperature-dependent interplay of a B-exciton and its trion in monolayer MoSe 2
abstract: Plasmonics in metal nanoparticles can enhance their near field optical interaction with matter, promoting emission into selected optical modes.

title: A rapid and specific antimicrobial resistance detection of Escherichia coli via magnetic nanoclusters
abstract: The peptide@PEG@MNCs can specifically capture E. coli from a bacterial mixture. The antimicrobial susceptibility of the captured E. coli can be analyzed thereafter by applying a sensitive luminescent probe within 30 min.

title: Graphene oxide activates canonical TGFβ signalling in a human chondrocyte cell line via increased plasma membrane tension
abstract: This study provides new insights into the molecular mechanisms underlying GO activated TGFβ signalling in chondrogenic cells. Contact with GO increased plasma membrane tension, activated mechanosensory pathways and endogenous TGFβ-1 as a mechanism for TGFβ signal activation.

title: Trifunctional electrocatalysts based on a bimetallic nanoalloy and nitrogen-doped carbon with brush-like heterostructure
abstract: 1D–1D heterostructured FeCo-N-doped carbon with brush-like structure has abundant active sites and interconnected conductive networks. The catalyst shows remarkable activities in oxygen reduction, oxygen evolution, and hydrogen evolution reactions.

title: Design of a new process for the stabilization of FeS–Bi 2 S 3 hybrid nanostructure and its application as a field emitter
abstract: Design of a new process for the fabrication of FeS–Bi 2 S 3 anisotropic nanostructure for field emission application.

title: Chiral nanomaterials in tissue engineering
abstract: A timely review on chiral nanomaterials in tissue engineering, with a discussion on their evaluation prior to clinical applications.

title: Reduced graphene oxide-wrapped ZnS–SnS 2 heterojunction bimetallic hollow cubic boxes as high-magnification and long lifespan supercapacitor anode materials
abstract: Metal sulfides have attracted extensive attention due to their excellent electrochemical performance.

title: Unravelling abnormal in-plane stretchability of two-dimensional metal–organic frameworks by machine learning potential molecular dynamics
abstract: The concept of negative in-plane stretchability is proposed taking a 2D MOF, namely NiF 2 (pyrazine) 2 , as a case study, combining high-precision first-principles calculations and machine-learning potential (MLP) approaches.

title: Tailoring the pore structure of iron oxide core@stellate mesoporous silica shell nanocomposites: effects on MRI and magnetic hyperthermia properties and applicability to anti-cancer therapies
abstract: The controlled growth of stellate silica shells around iron oxide nanoparticles allows the design of novel efficient theranostics for MRI, magnetic hyperthermia and photothermia.

title: Plasmon-enhanced second harmonic generation of metal nanostructures
abstract: In this review, the basic principles of plasmon-enhanced SHG, different methods to enhance the SHG intensity of metal nanostructures, and related applications of SHG based on metal nanostructures are introduced.

title: A floating-gate field-effect transistor memory device based on organic crystals with a built-in tunneling dielectric by a one-step growth strategy
abstract: A facile one-step liquid-surface drag coating method is developed to fabricate high-quality organic single-crystal semiconductor and tunneling dielectric layer in floating-gate organic transistors, which exhibit a multi-level data storage capability.

title: Fatigue behavior of freestanding nickel–molybdenum–tungsten thin films with high-density planar faults
abstract: Ni–Mo–W thin films with atomic-scale planar faults possess an unprecedented fatigue strength exceeding 2 GPa beyond 10 6 cycles.

title: Parylene-MoO x crossbar memristors as a volatile reservoir and non-volatile readout: a homogeneous reservoir computing system
abstract: A transition between the volatile and non-volatile resistive switching regimes is demonstrated in nanocomposite parylene-MoO x crossbar memristors and used for the development of a homogeneous reservoir computing system.

title: One-step regeneration and upgrading of spent LiFePO 4 cathodes with phytic acid
abstract: The regeneration and upgrading of spent LiFePO 4 cathodes were achieved via a one-step hydrothermal treatment.

title: Machine learning-enabled performance prediction and optimization for iron–chromium redox flow batteries
abstract: Iron–chromium flow batteries (ICRFBs) are regarded as one of the most promising large-scale energy storage devices with broad application prospects in recent years. In this work, active learning is used to explore the most optimized cases considering the highest energy efficiency and capacity.

title: Dual-responsive magnetic nanodroplets for controlled oxygen release via ultrasound and magnetic stimulation
abstract: Magnetic oxygen-loaded nanodroplets (MOLNDs) are a promising class of nanomaterials sensitive to both ultrasound and magnetic field, which can be employed as carriers for drug delivery applications, particularly for treating hypoxic tissue diseases.

title: 2D MXene electrochemical transistors
abstract: Here we show that not only conducting polymers, but also 2D MXenes can be used as materials for electrochemical transistors ECTs. MXene extend the capabilities of ECTs with properties such as extreme heat resistance, and higher conductivity/speeds.

title: Lattice capacity-dependent activity for CO 2 methanation: crafting Ni/CeO 2 catalysts with outstanding performance at low temperatures
abstract: A series of Ca 2+ -doped CeO 2 solid solutions with 10 wt% Ni loading (named Ni/Ca x Ce 1− x O y ) was used for effective CO 2 methanation.

title: Heterointerface MnO 2 /RuO 2 with rich oxygen vacancies for enhanced oxygen evolution in acidic media
abstract: The design and synthesis of oxygen evolution reaction (OER) electrocatalysts that operate efficiently and stably under acidic conditions are important for the preparation of green hydrogen energy.

title: Rational design of dual-ion doped cobalt-free Li-rich cathode materials for enhanced cycle stability of lithium-ion pouch cell batteries
abstract: This study introduces a new method for cobalt-free lithium-ion cathodes, improving cycling stability and capacity via dual-ion doping and morphological modulation.

title: Minimizing defect states through multidentate coordination and morphology regulation for enhancing the performance of inverted perovskite solar cells
abstract: The interaction between CO and Pb 2+ regulated grain growth, enhanced crystallinity that effectively passivated non-radiative recombination centres and CF 3 group stabilizes the perovskite structure by interacting with FA and Pb cationic defects.

title: Perovskite CoSn(OH) 6 nanocubes with tuned d-band states towards enhanced oxygen evolution reactions
abstract: An Fe 3+ doping strategy is used to optimize the d-band state of the CoSn(OH) 6 perovskite hydroxide, which promotes the OER performance of materials.

title: Coumarin-embedded [5]helicene derivatives: synthesis, X-ray analysis and photoconducting properties
abstract: Two novel coumarin-embedded π-extended [5]helicene derivatives (3a and 6a) have been strategically synthesized and characterized, and the structure of 3a was determined via single crystal X-ray analysis.

title: Recent advances in the interfacial engineering of MOF-based mixed matrix membranes for gas separation
abstract: This is a comprehensive review for resolving compatibility challenges in fabricating practical metal–organic framework composite membranes for gas separation.

title: A biocompatible polydopamine platform for targeted delivery of nicotinamide mononucleotide and boosting NAD+ levels in the brain
abstract: A biocompatible polydopamine platform to target-deliver NMN for boosting brain NAD+ levels is developed for the first time.

title: Crystalline architectures of C 84 with tunable morphology and linearly polarized red emission
abstract: C 84 microprisms with similar crystalline structures are prepared, which exhibit polymorphism depending on the preparation conditions. These microprisms show linearly polarized red emissions with a high polarization ratio ( ρ ) of 0.42–0.58.

title: Interfacial-engineered living drugs with “ON/OFF” switching for oral delivery
abstract: A "stealth coating" was implemented to enable an "ON/OFF" switching of probiotics' communications with the gastrointestinal (GI) tract. This strategy aimed to optimize the bioactivity of living drugs as they traverse the GI tract.

title: Investigation of electrocatalysis for tiered-tower micro-electro-mechanical-system-based biosensors: application in the early detection of the thrombosis factor trimethylamine N -oxide
abstract: The proposed tiered-tower biosensor array, designed using a MEMS process compatible with CMOS frontend technology, aims to detect and enable the early prevention of thrombosis by targeting the biomarker trimethylamine N -oxide (TMAO).

title: Nanosilica polyamidoamine dendrimers for enhanced direct air CO 2 capture
abstract: Nanosilica polyamidoamine dendrimers showed an impressive CO 2 uptake in humid indoor air with superior adsorption–desorption cycles and low regeneration temperature for efficient CO 2 recovery to utilize it for formate and CaCO 3 synthesis.

title: Sulfurized polyacrylonitrile as cathodes for advanced lithium–sulfur batteries: advances in modification strategies
abstract: The latest progress in the last five years concerning the modification of S@PAN cathode materials for Li–S batteries.

title: Novel hollow MoS 2 @C@Cu 2 S heterostructures for high zinc storage performance
abstract: Novel hollow MoS 2 @C@Cu 2 S heterostructures with sizes of ∼600 nm were fabricated for Zn 2+ storage. These heterostructures exhibit a high initial specific capacity of 679.1 mA h g −1 .

title: Unraveling a volcanic relationship of Co/N/C@Pt x Co catalysts toward oxygen electro-reduction
abstract: A (inverted) volcanic relationship between Pt/Co ratios and oxygen electro-reduction properties is unraveled for composite Co/N/C@Pt x Co catalysts.

title: Nanoscale fabrication of heterostructures in thermoelectric SnTe
abstract: Among the strategies for introducing secondary phases in thermoelectric materials, the highly energetic electrons provide a facile way to tailor local phase change in materials for designing heterostructures at the atomic scale.

title: Inducing preferential intercalation of Zn 2+ in MnO 2 with abundant oxygen defects for high-performance aqueous zinc-ion batteries
abstract: Regulating the electronic structure of MnO 2 and inducing preferential intercalation of Zn 2+ by oxygen defects for high-performance aqueous zinc-ion batteries are presented.

title: Electrically tunable interlayer recombination and tunneling behavior in WSe 2 /MoS 2 heterostructure for broadband photodetector
abstract: A dual-gated WSe 2 /MoS 2 phototransistor is fabricated and investigated. Its conduction and rectification characteristics can be tuned by dual gates showing p–i, p–n, i–n and n–n states, due to the charging and depletion of WSe 2 and MoS 2 .

title: Advances in sustainable nano-biochar: precursors, synthesis methods and applications
abstract: Nano-biochar, characterized by its environmentally friendly nature and unique nanostructure, offers a promising avenue for sustainable carbon materials.

title: Facile synthesis of Zn 0.5 Cd 0.5 S nanosheets with tunable S vacancies for highly efficient photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
abstract: Porous Zn 0.5 Cd 0.5 S nanosheets with a controlled amount of S vacancies were prepared by a multistep chemical transformation strategy. The photocatalytic activity of Zn 0.5 Cd 0.5 S nanosheets prepared by 4 h hydrolysis is 20.5 times higher than that of Zn 0.5 Cd 0.5 S(en) x intermediates.

title: Chemically synthesized poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) conducting polymer as a robust electrocatalyst for highly efficient dye-sensitized solar cells
abstract: PEDOT counter electrodes, optimized via spin-coating a DMSO-dispersed solution from scalable synthesis, were evaluated for cobalt reduction in dye-sensitized solar cells. PEDOT was a superior electrocatalyst compared to Pt.

title: Metasurface inverse designed by deep learning for quasi-entire terahertz wave absorption
abstract: Ultra-broadband and efficient terahertz (THz) absorption is of paramount importance for the development of high-performance detectors.

title: Electronic properties of MoSe 2 nanowrinkles
abstract: The electronic properties of MoSe 2 nanowrinkles are simulated from first principles. Wave-function distributions and effective masses are analyzed to disclose the potential of such systems for charge transport and optoelectronics.

title: Multi-atomic loaded C 2 N 1 catalysts for CO 2 reduction to CO or formic acid
abstract: Triple-atom catalysts exhibit moderate adsorption energy for intermediate species, enabling the optimal performance of the CO 2 electrocatalytic reduction reaction.

title: Correction: Hybrid 2D perovskite and red emitting carbon dot composite for improved stability and efficiency of LEDs
abstract: Correction for ‘Hybrid 2D perovskite and red emitting carbon dot composite for improved stability and efficiency of LEDs’ by Amandeep Singh Pannu et al. , Nanoscale , 2023, 15 , 2659–2666, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2NR06942C.

title: Introduction to fundamental processes in optical nanomaterials
abstract: Alison Funston, Eva Hemmer, Arindam Chowdhury and Jon Veinot introduce this Nanoscale and ChemComm themed collection on fundamental processes in optical nanomaterials.

title: Autonomous generation of single photon emitting materials
abstract: Single photon quantum materials discovery based on large dataset synthetic data generation.

title: Magnetic iron oxide nanogels for combined hyperthermia and drug delivery for cancer treatment
abstract: Simultaneous hyperthermia and drug delivery with magnetic iron oxide nanogels.

title: Highly efficient and stable tandem luminescent solar concentrators based on carbon dots and CuInSe 2− x S x /ZnS quantum dots
abstract: In this work, we fabricated the among green, environment-friendly tandem LSCs using CDs and near-infrared CuInSeS/ZnS QDs as PL emitters. Tandem LSCs exhibits high PCE (0.46%) and η ext (2.57%) and improve the stability of bottom LSCs.

title: Single and dual-atom catalysts towards electrosynthesis of ammonia and urea: a review
abstract: Illustration of atomic catalysts in five different reactions: nitrogen reduction, nitrate reduction, nitrite reduction, nitric oxide reduction and urea synthesis. Advantages of diatom catalysts include those of single atom catalysts.

title: Graphene-based iron single-atom catalysts for electrocatalytic nitric oxide reduction: a first-principles study
abstract: The electrocatalytic NO reduction reaction (NORR) emerges as an intriguing strategy to convert harmful NO into valuable NH 3 .

title: Interlaboratory comparison of endotoxin contamination assessment of nanomaterials
abstract: Endotoxin contamination is a significant hurdle to the translation of nanomaterials for biomedical applications.

title: Quantifying particle concentration via AI-enhanced optical coherence tomography
abstract: Al-enhanced OCT imaging module resulted in an accurate and robust quantification of particle concentration of gold nanorods, which exemplify nanoparticles with high light scattering signals (OCT signals).

title: Circular dichroism and circularly polarized luminescence of ligand-protected molecular metal clusters: insights into structure–chiroptical property relationships
abstract: The potential of circular dichroism and circularly polarized luminescence spectroscopies to unravel the structure–chiroptical properties of ligand-protected, atomically precise noble metal clusters is discussed.

title: Recent progress in atomically precise silver nanocluster-assembled materials
abstract: This review outlines the precise synthesis, structure, applications, and structure–property correlation of Ag( i )-based emerging cluster-assembled materials.

title: Er 3+ -activated Ba 2 V 2 O 7 upconversion nanosheets for dual-mode temperature sensing
abstract: Downshifting and upconversion photoluminescence properties of Ba 2 V 2 O 7 :Er 3+ nanosheets are investigated. Temperature-dependent luminescence intensity ratios of thermally coupled as well as non-thermally coupled levels are used for temperature sensing.

title: Liquid-templated graphene aerogel electromagnetic traps
abstract: This study coined a practical strategy entitled “Aerogel Electromagnetic Traps” toward blocking the scattered electromagnetic waves from metallic substrates through aerogel engineering, leading to absorption dominant shielding constructs.

title: Gate-defined quantum point contacts in a germanium quantum well
abstract: Quantum point contacts made in a strained Ge/SiGe heterostructure show ballistic charge transport characteristics, featuring quantized conductance plateaus and magnetic field-induced Zeeman splitting of the subbands.

title: Active nanoparticle targeting of MUC5AC ameliorates therapeutic outcome in experimental colitis
abstract: We report anti-MUC5AC-conjugated PLGA nanoparticles with betametasone for active targeted treatment of mice TNBS-induced colitis.

title: Theoretical insights into single-atom catalysts for improved charging and discharging kinetics of Na–S and Na–Se batteries
abstract: Density functional theory simulations were employed to investigate the charging and discharging kinetics of Na–S and Na–Se electrodes by utilizing single transition metal atoms supported on reduced graphitic carbon nitride surfaces.

title: Magnetic-assisted sequential templated self-assembly of hybrid colloid nanoparticle systems
abstract: The assembly of hybrid nanoparticles is a pioneering route for developing nanoscale functional devices, including targeted drug delivery systems.

title: Structural diversity of copper( i ) alkynyl cluster-based coordination polymers utilizing bifunctional pyridine carboxylic acid ligands
abstract: A series of copper( i ) alkynyl cluster-based polymers (CACPs) have been successfully synthesized by employing bifunctional ligands. Additionally, the selection of auxiliary ligands plays a crucial role in shaping the structure of CACPs.

title: Calculations of the effect of catalyst size and structure on the electrocatalytic reduction of CO 2 on Cu nanoclusters
abstract: DFT studies of CO 2 reduction to methane, methanol on Cu nanoclusters obtained by global optimisation reveals the promise of irregular, defected particles for reduced onset potentials.

title: Nanostructured electroless Ni deposited SnO 2 for solar hydrogen production
abstract: Herein, Ni-decorated SnO 2 (Ni@SnO 2 ) nanostructures have been synthesized over SnO 2 nanoparticles via a simple electroless deposition method for the generation of hydrogen, a potent near-future fuel.

title: The existence of a strongly bonded layer in associating liquids within silica pores – a spectral and molecular dynamics study
abstract: In this paper, we present a novel experimental approach allowing us to reveal the formation of an irreversible adsorption layer in two phenyl alcohols infiltrated into mesoporous silica templates.

title: Gallic acid-loaded HFZIF-8 for tumor-targeted delivery and thermal-catalytic therapy
abstract: HFZIF-8/GA is prepared via a simple stirring method for loading GA. After HA modification, HFZIF-8/GA@HA is used for the targeted delivery of Fe ions and GA, thereby achieving the in situ synthesis of an Fe-GA complex for thermal catalytic therapy.

title: Single-atom alloy structure and unique bonding properties of Au 104 Ag 40 (PET) 60 nanoclusters
abstract: X-ray absorption spectroscopy is utilized to study the distribution of Au and Ag within the metal core of the Au 104 Ag 40 (PET) 60 nanocluster, revealing a Ag single-atom alloy structure associated with unique bonding properties.

title: Flexible and stretchable implantable devices for peripheral neuromuscular electrophysiology
abstract: The peripheral nervous and muscular system, a cornerstone of human physiology, plays a pivotal role in ensuring the seamless functioning of the human body.

title: Understanding vapor phase growth of hexagonal boron nitride
abstract: High-quality grown hexagonal boron nitride by chemical vapor deposition using ammonia borane and borazine on (non-)catalytic substrates.

title: Improved electrochemical reduction of CO 2 to syngas with a highly exfoliated Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene–gold composite
abstract: Transforming carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) into valuable chemicals via electroreduction presents a sustainable and viable approach to mitigating excess CO 2 in the atmosphere.

title: Construction of novel Ag(0)-containing silver nanoclusters by regulating auxiliary phosphine ligands
abstract: Two superatomic silver nanoclusters (Ag17 and Ag32) with similar outer shells were constructed by adjusting phosphine ligands, and their PL intensity and QY varied greatly because of the different surface rigidity.

title: Click catalysis and DNA conjugation using a nanoscale DNA/silver cluster pair
abstract: Silver cluster–DNA conjugates catalyze alkyne–azide cycloadditions. The catalytic activity of the cluster is regulated by the DNA host.

title: Stable dispersion of carbon nanotubes in a molten salt of KNO 3 –NaNO 3 –NaNO 2 –LiNO 3 –LiOH
abstract: Chemical bonds and adsorption between Li + and π electrons of CNTs contribute to dispersion of CNTs.

title: Co 3 X 8 (X = Cl and Br): multiple phases and magnetic properties of the Kagome lattice
abstract: We found a novel Kagome lattice, Co 3 X 8 , in which the spontaneous movement of Co or X leads to distinct phase and physical properties. The anisotropic transmission properties of 12-Co 3 X 8 make it possible to apply this lattice in nanodevices.

title: Unveiling the charge storage mechanisms of Co-based perovskite fluoride in a mild aqueous electrolyte
abstract: Unveiling the charge storage mechanism of KCoF 3 in a 1 M Na 2 SO 4 electrolyte by using a variety of characterisation methods, showing the hybrid mechanisms of adsorption, multi-electron bulk phase conversion and insertion/extraction reactions.

title: Optically induced trion formation and its control in a MoS 2 /graphene van der Waals heterostructure
