abstract: Monolayer 2D transition metal dichalcogenides are sensitive to charge transfer leading to modified optoelectronic properties.

title: SERS nanostructures with engineered active peptides against an immune checkpoint protein
abstract: Engineered peptides on plasmonic nanostructures show a good targeting activity against the immune checkpoint programmed death ligand 1 protein (PD-L1) expressed by tumor cells. SERS signals allow quantitative evaluation of the targeting activity.

title: High sensing performance flexible nanocomposite sensor with a hybrid nanostructure constructed via nanoscale confined motion of nanofibers and nanoplatelets
abstract: Hybrid 1D nanofiber/2D nanoplatelet nanocomposite foam sensor and Monte-Carlo modelling of complex 1D/2D sensing nanostructure.

title: Novel three-dimensional fibrous covalent organic frameworks constructed via silver amalgam bridging for efficient organic dye adsorption and removal
abstract: Highly stable 3D COF nanofiber was fabricated using silver amalgam as a bridging agent to assemble 1D-extended PA-COF modules into a tubular structure, which exhibit fast adsorption and superior adsorption capacity to cationic/anionic organic dyes.

title: A dual-template synergistic assembly strategy towards the synthesis of extra-small nitrogen-doped mesoporous carbon nanospheres with large pores
abstract: Based on the synergistic effect of the combination of surfactants, a dual-template synergistic assembly strategy was used to synthesize extra-small nitrogen-doped mesoporous carbon nanospheres with large pores.

title: Carbon dots on LAPONITE® hybrid nanocomposites: solid-state emission and inter-aggregate energy transfer
abstract: Solid-state and aquous emission by hybrids nanocomposites based on carbon dots/LAPONITE® – energy transfer (ET) between aggregates.

title: Wafer scale growth of single crystal two-dimensional van der Waals materials
abstract: This minireview focuses on wafer-scale synthesis of single-crystal 2D van der Waals thin films such as graphene, hBN and metal chalcogenides, summarizing the state-of-the-art synthesis routes, discussing the current challenges and future outlook.

title: Flexible solid-state Zn–air battery based on polymer-oxygen-functionalized g-C 3 N 4 composite membrane
abstract: Polymer membrane comprised of oxygen functionalized g-C 3 N 4 fillers in PVA matrix with optimised conductivity without compromising the mechanical behavior of the composite.

title: Amino-functionalization enhanced CO 2 reduction reaction in pure water
abstract: Amino groups can enhance the adsorption of CO 2 and also be protonated to adsorb protons, this property makes amino-modification highly effective in improving the CO 2 RR performance of the catalyst in zero-gap CO 2 electrolyzers utilizing pure water.

title: Anapole assisted self-hybridized exciton–polaritons in perovskite metasurfaces
abstract: We propose a new strategy to significantly enhance optical responses of the anapole excited in perovskite metasurfaces with a low index by using metallic substrates and further to maximize the coupling strength and Rabi splitting.

title: Enhanced photocatalytic performance of the N-rGO/g-C 3 N 4 nanocomposite for efficient solar-driven water remediation
abstract: N-rGO/g-C 3 N 4 as a metal-free photocatalyst has been synthesised via a facile and eco-friendly hydrothermal route that exhibits remarkable photodegradation efficiency towards organic pollutants and antibiotics under the simulated solar irradiation.

title: Reticular synthesis of two-dimensional ionic covalent organic networks as metal-free bifunctional electrocatalysts for oxygen reduction and evolution reactions
abstract: Four redox active viologen-based ionic covalent organic networks (vCONs) were synthesized by the solvothermal-assisted Zincke reaction which are directly used as metal-free bifunctional electrocatalysts for ORR and OER applications.

title: Crafting “brick–mud” segregated nanocomposites: a novel approach to superior electromagnetic interference shielding, electrical insulation, and thermal conductivity in biopolymers
abstract: “Brick–mud” segregated biopolymer nanocomposites exhibit remarkable electromagnetic interference shielding, thermal conductivity, and electrical insulation, representing an innovative strategy in the design of multifunctional polymer composites.

title: Review of molecular dynamics simulations in laser-based micro/nano-fabrication
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive examination of the application of Molecular Dynamics (MD) simulations in laser-based micro/nano fabrication.

title: Quantum engineering of the radiative properties of a nanoscale mesoscopic system
abstract: We demonstrate new insights into the interactions between molecules and electromagnetic fields by carefully shaping their energy levels through their strong matter–wave coupling, demonstrating a new type of fluorescent probe for imaging.

title: Unravelling the origin of reaction-driven aggregation and fragmentation of atomically dispersed Pt catalyst on ceria support
abstract: CO-induced fragmentation of platinum nanoparticles on undercoordinated ceria support.

title: A polymeric iron oxide nanocomplex loaded with sulfasalazine: an approach for inducing ferritinophagy-assisted ferroptosis for anti-cancer therapy
abstract: A ferritinophagy-assisted ferroptosis strategy addresses the limitations in iron-based anti-cancer therapy by inhibiting GSH, inducing ferritin degradation, overloading labile iron ions, and enhancing ferroptosis.

title: Self-brushing for nanopatterning: achieving perpendicular domain orientation in block copolymer thin films
abstract: The self-brushing capability of block copolymers enables perpendicular thin film assembly on various substrates without the need for additional coatings.

title: Sensing nature's alarm: SnO 2 /MXene gas sensor unveils methyl jasmonate signatures of plant insect stress
abstract: A chemoresistive sensor is developed that selectively identify stress signature volatile methyl jasmonate.

title: Sign-flipping intrinsic anomalous Hall conductivity with Berry curvature tunability in a half-metallic ferromagnet NbSe 2 –VSe 2 lateral heterostructure
abstract: Schematic of the half-metallic character of the lateral NbSe 2 –VSe 2 heterostructure, corresponding charge transfer mechanism and sign-flipping anomalous Hall conductivity under an external electric field.

title: The multivariate interaction between Au and TiO 2 colloids: the role of surface potential, concentration, and defects
abstract: The interaction between a Au particle and a defective TiO 2 surface was simulated over 6000 unique conditions, and the relative importance of the parameters was determined by logistic regression.

title: Dual-targeting of tumor cells and subcellular endoplasmic reticulum via AgPPIX-based Janus nanoparticles for photodynamic/immunotherapy against TNBC
abstract: The synthetic strategy of PAPC and the function of PAPC Janus nanoparticles: tumor cells targeting, endoplasmic reticulum targeting, immunotherapy and photodynamic therapy.

title: Photoactive imaging and therapy for colorectal cancer using a CEA-Affimer conjugated Foslip nanoparticle
abstract: Theranostic nanoparticles hold promise for simultaneous imaging and therapy in colorectal cancer.

title: Recent developments of artificial intelligence in MXene-based devices: from synthesis to applications
abstract: The role of AI technology in MXene-based devices and the latest research papers on AI-based MXene devices were comprehensively reviewed.

title: An oxygen vacancy-rich BiO 2− x /COF heterojunction for photocatalytic degradation of diclofenac
abstract: BiO 2− x /COF has abundant oxygen vacancies and photocatalyzes the degradation of diclofenac by forming heterojunctions with ˙O 2 − and h + active substances.

title: Elucidating the reversible and irreversible self-assembly mechanisms of low-complexity aromatic-rich kinked peptides and steric zipper peptides
abstract: Alternating stickers (Tyr) and spacers (Gly/Ser) drive the reversible assembly of LARKS peptides into highly dynamical oligomers, while hydrophobic patches in the steric zipper peptides promote the irreversible formation of β-sheet-rich assemblies.

title: Optimizing TDP-43 silencing with siRNA-loaded polymeric nanovectors in neuronal cells for therapeutic applications: balancing knockdown and function
authors: 'Annamaria Russo', 'Gabriele Maiorano', 'Barbara Cortese', 'Stefania D'Amone', 'Alessandra Invidia', 'Angelo Quattrini', 'Alessandro Romano', 'Giuseppe Gigli', 'Ilaria E. Palamà'
abstract: A novel nanovector system for TDP-43 siRNA delivery based on a biopolymer combination demonstrates comparable TDP-43 silencing to gold-standard lipofection, superior stress granule reduction, and accelerated stress granule degradation.

title: The structure and application portfolio of intricately architected silver cluster-assembled materials
abstract: In this review, we provide a brief sketch of silver cluster-assembled materials research with crucial insights into atomic-level structural correlations with the phenomena at the nanoscale.

title: Visualizing localized nematic states in twisted double bilayer graphene
abstract: A new valence band (VB) state localized in the ABAB and ABCA stacking regions appears at negative voltage. STS mapping of this state reveals an underlying C 2 symmetry, reflecting the electronic structure modulation within the moiré pattern.

title: Mechanistic study of the competition between carbon dioxide reduction and hydrogen evolution reaction and selectivity tuning via loading single-atom catalysts on graphitic carbon nitride
abstract: We computationally screened metal-single-atom catalysts (M-SAC) on carbon nitride for selective CO 2 reduction. NiCN significantly lowered the rate-determining potential for CO 2 conversion to formic acid via the *OCHO key intermediate.

title: Properties of silicon nanoparticles prepared by pulse discharge and ball milling
abstract: During the pulse discharge process, bulk silicon materials vaporize at high temperatures to obtain micro nanoparticles with a certain amorphous structure. High degree of amorphization of silicon nanoparticles was obtained through ball milling.

title: Nanostructure-based orbital angular momentum encryption and multiplexing
abstract: This review focuses on the study of encryption, multiplexing and demultiplexing of vortex beams based on nanostructure platforms.

title: Enhanced magnetic susceptibility in Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene with Co and Ni incorporation
abstract: We incorporate dilute concentrations of Ni and Co into Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene, resulting in a >50 fold increase in magnetic susceptibility.

title: Circularly polarized luminescence in quantum dot-based materials
abstract: Quantum dots (QDs) have emerged as fantastic luminescent nanomaterials with significant potential due to their unique photoluminescence properties.

title: Molten salt modulation of porous MoP@PC nanosheets as an ultra-stable anode for lithium-ion batteries
abstract: This study employed molten salt method to fabricate in situ sheet-like porous P-doped carbon-supported MoP nanoparticles (MoP@PC). Due to the unique structure of this material, MoP@PC obtains better electrochemical performance.

title: Emerging electrospinning platform toward nanoparticle to single atom transformation for steering selectivity in ammonia synthesis
abstract: A “top down” strategy for the transformation of Co NPs to Co SAs was proposed and the synthesized Co SAs/CNF electrocatalyst exhibited excellent electrocatalytic NO 3 RR activity with a FE of 91.30% at −0.70 V vs. RHE.

title: A study of complexation and biological fate of polyethyleneimine-siRNA polyplexes in vitro and in vivo by fluorescence correlation spectroscopy and positron emission tomography imaging
abstract: Fluorescence correlation spectroscopy and positron emission tomography are used to study the formation of PEI siRNA polyplex nanoparticles and to trace their biological fate at cell level and in vivo .

title: Finite size effects on the metamagnetic phase transition in a thick B2 FeRh nanocluster film
abstract: An inhomogeneous grain coarsening distribution to explain the metamagnetic transition in a thick film of assembled B2 FeRh nanoclusters.

title: Multiparametric modulation of magnetic transduction for biomolecular sensing in liquids
abstract: The transducing capacity of magnetic nanoparticles for biomarker detection in AC magnetometry lies in a number of modulating parameters. This is assessed through the variations of AC magnetic hysteresis area in absence (black colour, A 0 ) and presence (violet colour, A) of analytes.

title: Machine learning-aided understanding of the structure–activity relationship: a case study of MoS 2 supported metal–nonmetal pairs for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Understanding the structure–performance relationship is crucial for designing highly active electrocatalysts, yet this remains a challenge.

title: Photocatalytic activity of dual defect modified graphitic carbon nitride is robust to tautomerism: machine learning assisted ab initio quantum dynamics
abstract: Transformations between multiple tautomeric forms of defective graphitic carbon nitride occur on nanosecond timescales, but these transformations have little influence on charge carrier lifetimes.

title: Advances in nanoporous materials for next-generation battery applications
abstract: The tunable nanoporous features in metal–organic frameworks and covalent organic frameworks show great potential for batteries, such as promoting lithium-ion conduction and stabilising lithium metal anodes/high nickel cathodes.

title: Heterogeneous binding of polymers on curved nanoparticles
abstract: Unraveling protracted polymer binding on curved surfaces of nanoparticles (NPs) is important for the fabrication of multifunctional nanostructures in cutting-edge research disciplines such as directional self-assembly and nanomedicine.

title: Peptide-mediated targeted delivery of SOX9 nanoparticles into astrocytes ameliorates ischemic brain injury
abstract: We describes the development of a poly-lactic-coglycolic acid (PLGA) nanoparticle-based system for conjugation of targeting peptides to PLGA nanoparticles and delivery of the therapeutic gene SOX9 to damaged astrocytes in a mouse stroke model.

title: Design of a photocatalyst combining graphdiyne (g-C n H 2 n −2 ) nanosheets with a PVP-modified co-metal coordination polymer and its hydrogen-evolution performance
abstract: Metal coordination polymers are organometallic frameworks in which a metal and an organic ligand are linked via a dative bond.

title: Expression of concern: Gadolinium embedded iron oxide nanoclusters as T 1 – T 2 dual-modal MRI-visible vectors for safe and efficient siRNA delivery
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Gadolinium embedded iron oxide nanoclusters as T 1 – T 2 dual-modal MRI-visible vectors for safe and efficient siRNA delivery’ by Xiaoyong Wang et al. , Nanoscale , 2013, 5 , 8098–8104, https://doi.org/10.1039/C3NR02797J.

title: Retraction: Microchip-based structure determination of low-molecular weight proteins using cryo-electron microscopy
abstract: Retraction of ‘Microchip-based structure determination of low-molecular weight proteins using cryo-electron microscopy’ by Michael A. Casasanta et al. , Nanoscale , 2021, 13 , 7285–7293, https://doi.org/10.1039/D1NR00388G.

title: nanoFeatures: a cross-platform application to characterize nanoparticles from super-resolution microscopy images
abstract: Single-Molecule Localization Microscopy (SMLM) is a powerful tool to characterize synthetic nanomaterials used for many applications such as drug delivery. nanoFeatures streamlines the feature extraction of those nanomaterials from SMLM images.

title: Insights into the electrochemical catalytic mechanism of atomically dispersed metal on h-BCN monolayer
abstract: Exploiting efficient and inexpensive electrocatalysts for the oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) and oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is of great significance for the rapid development of renewable energy technologies.

title: Restoring physiological parameters of the pancreas and kidney through treatment with a polymeric nano-formulation of C-peptide and lisofylline combination in diabetic nephropathy
abstract: Diabetic nephropathy (DN) is a progressive kidney disorder that develops as a complication of diabetes due to long-term exposure to elevated blood glucose levels (BGLs).

title: Multilayer ferromagnetic spintronic devices for neuromorphic computing applications
abstract: Spintronic devices, which are built upon ferromagnetic thin film systems, exhibit significant promise for energy-efficient memory, logic operations, and neuromorphic computing applications.

title: Chemical activation of atom-precise Pd 3 nanoclusters on γ-Al 2 O 3 supports for transfer hydrogenation reactions
abstract: Pd 3 nanoclusters on Al 2 O 3 supports were activated by reducing agents. The activity and selectivity of the catalysts for the transfer hydrogenation of cinnamaldehyde was a function of both the identity and loading of the reducing agent used.

title: Point-of-care devices engaging green graphene: an eco-conscious and sustainable paradigm
abstract: The healthcare landscape has experienced a profound and irreversible transformation, primarily driven by the emergence of green nanomaterial-assisted point-of-care (POC) devices.

title: Dual second harmonic generation and up-conversion photoluminescence emission in highly-optimized LiNbO 3 nanocrystals doped and co-doped with Er 3+ and Yb 3+
abstract: Simultaneous emission of second harmonic and up-conversion signals from colloidal suspensions of highly-doped LiNbO 3 nanocrystals with Er 3+ and Yb 3+ ions.

title: A review of metal phosphides with catalytic effects in Li–S batteries: boosting the redox kinetics
abstract: The application of metal phosphides from the aspects of characteristics, material structure design, and material interface control. Enhancing the understanding of the operational mechanism and providing guidance for the development of Li–S batteries.

title: Unveiling efficient S-scheme charge carrier transfer in hierarchical BiOBr/TiO 2 heterojunction photocatalysts
abstract: Insights into the synthesis and structure of an n–n type BiOBr/TiO 2 heterostructure with an S-scheme charge-carrier mechanism and its effect on photocatalytic dye degradation are presented.

title: Attaining inhibition of sneak current and versatile logic operations in a singular halide perovskite memristive device by introducing appropriate interface barriers
abstract: We have created ReRAMs using ITO/UVO-treated a-ZnO/MAPbI 3 /Ag to minimize cross-talk currents. Remarkably, a single unit can execute 12 different 2-input sequential logic functions seamlessly.

title: Structure-driven tuning of catalytic properties of core–shell nanostructures
abstract: Changes in the atomic structure of nanoparticles, core-to-shell ratio and composition lead to fine tuning of the catalytic properties.

title: Transport, trapping, triplet fusion: thermally retarded exciton migration in tetracene single crystals
abstract: Temperature and material quality strongly influence spatiotemporal exciton dynamics in the endothermic singlet fission material tetracene. Underlying transport mechanisms are disentangled by combining spectral and spatial data from TRPL experiments.

title: Controlling nanoparticle-induced endothelial leakiness with the protein corona
abstract: Gold nanoparticle-induced endothelial leakiness and paracellular transport can be controlled or partially negated by preformed plasma protein coronas.

title: Multifunctional phase-change composites for green electromagnetic interference shielding and thermal response prepared under the guidance of an impedance matching strategy
abstract: Schematic diagram of heat dissipation of composite materials.

title: Accelerating and breaking adaptive nano-colloids (<100 nm) into unsteady state operation via push–pull effects
abstract: The mobility of nanosized organosilica Janus colloids can be controlled precisely, allowing them to be halted at a desired position.

title: Thermal evaporation-driven fabrication of Ru/RuO 2 nanoparticles onto nickel foam for efficient overall water splitting
abstract: A self-supported substrate material of Ru/NF could be obtained by a two-stage metal–organic thermal evaporation strategy. The synergistic effect between Ru and RuO 2 species greatly promotes water splitting.

title: Shape-dependent cellular uptake of iron oxide nanorods: mechanisms of endocytosis and implications on cell labeling and cellular delivery
abstract: The effects of nanoparticle morphology, especially size and shape, on their interactions with cells are of great interest in understanding the fate of nanoparticles in biological systems and designing them for biomedical applications.

title: Electrosprayed Eudragit RL100 nanoparticles with Janus polyvinylpyrrolidone patches for multiphase release of paracetamol
abstract: Janus nanoparticles with soluble polyvinylpyrrolidone patches located on the insoluble Eudragit RL100 sides were prepared using a side-by-side electrospraying method and are demonstrated to provide biphasic release of paracetamol and in turn faster action and longer time periods of blood drug concen…

title: MgH 2 nanoparticles confined in reduced graphene oxide pillared with organosilica: a novel type of hydrogen storage material
abstract: Reduced graphene oxide pillared with organosilica hosts MgH 2 nanoparticles, enhancing its hydrogen storage performance with the small particle size.

title: Ni,S co-doped Cu dendrites decorated with core–shell architecture assisted by MOF and Fe 0.92 Co 0.08 S nanoflakes on nanocellulose/graphene fibers for fabrication of flexible wire-type micro-supercapacitor
abstract: A flexible 1D micro-SC was fabricated based on a multidimensional nanoarchitecture derived from MOF on foam-like microwires and FeCoS nanoflakes on nanocellulose/graphene fibers and exhibited high energy storage capability.

title: In situ self-assembly of molybdenum carbide and iron carbide heterostructures on N-doped carbon for an efficient oxygen reduction reaction
abstract: This study presents a novel, Pt-free Mo 2 C/Fe 3 C on N-doped carbon catalyst synthesized via eco-friendly, low-cost, low-temperature self-assembly. It demonstrates superior ORR performance, presenting a significant breakthrough for Zn–air batteries.

title: Advanced micro/nano-electroporation for gene therapy: recent advances and future outlook
abstract: Advanced micro/nano fabrication techniques have revolutionized electroporation technology. Electroporation devices with micro/nanostructures mediating safe, precise and controllable electrotransfection continue to adapt to the field of gene therapy.

title: Self-propelling bacteria-based magnetic nanoparticles (BacMags) for targeted magnetic hyperthermia therapy against hypoxic tumors
abstract: Anaerobic bacteria deliver anisotropic magnetic nanocarriers to hard-to-reach hypoxic tumor cores. The enhanced heating effect at the cores of tumors acts as a smart therapeutic approach for improved magnetic hyperthermia-based cancer therapy.

title: Interplay between superconducting fluctuations and weak localization in disordered TiN thin films
abstract: The interplay between superconducting fluctuations (SFs) and weak localization (WL) has been probed by temperature dependent resistance [ R ( T )] and magnetoresistance (MR) measurements in two-dimensional disordered superconducting TiN thin films.

title: Production of mixed phase Ti 3+ -rich TiO 2 thin films by oxide defect engineered crystallization
abstract: Oxygen vacancies and Ti 3+ defects in the amorphous phase of TiO 2 predetermine vacuum annealing induced crystallization. Anatase, nanocrystalline rutile or mixed phases can be created by defect engineering without affecting the O/Ti ratio.

title: A 2D low-buckled hexagonal honeycomb Weyl-point spin-gapless semiconductor family with the quantum anomalous Hall effect
abstract: The Li 12 X 10 Cr 2 Y 12 (X = Mg, Zn, Cd; Y = P, As) monolayers are ideal 2D Weyl-point spin-gapless semiconductors (WPSGSs) with the quantum anomalous Hall effect.

title: Supramolecular polymerization of [6]helicene-based cyano-luminogens: on the overall efficiency of self-assembled circularly polarized emitters
abstract: The supramolecular polymerization of the [6]helicene-based luminophores 1 and 2 , showing a rich dichroic pattern, is reported. The inherent chirality of both the monomeric and aggregated species of 1 and 2 provides the systems with a remarkable overall CPL-efficiency.

title: Hierarchical superstructure aerogels for in situ biofluid metabolomics
abstract: A superstructure is designed for simultaneous metabolite enrichment, separation of interfering salt and direct biofluids analysis.

title: Photomanipulatable colloidal clusters from the aggregation of azo molecular glass spheres
abstract: Controllable fabrication of photoresponsive colloidal clusters with a wide range of adjustable sizes and complex architectures are fabricated through the aggregation, fusion and photoinduced deformation of azo colloidal spheres.

title: Effects of radiation damage on the yielding and fracture of nanowires
abstract: Irradiated nanowires' strength can change with their diameter. Though damaged by radiation, defects get cleaned during loading, returning the nanowires to their pristine state. This suggests that radiation has little impact on their fracture behavior.

title: Fe–Co heteronuclear atom pairs as catalytic sites for efficient oxygen electroreduction
abstract: Single-site Fe–N–C catalysts are the most promising Pt-group catalyst alternatives for the oxygen reduction reaction, but their application is impeded by their relatively low activity and unsatisfactory stability as well as production costs.

title: Novel mixed self-assembled monolayers of l -cysteine and methanol on gold surfaces under ambient conditions
abstract: Novel mixed self-assembled monolayer of neutral and zwitterionic l -cysteine pairs surrounded by methanol on Au(100), which is stable under ambient conditions.

title: A hydrogel-functionalized silver nanocluster for bacterial-infected wound healing
abstract: An Ag NC-gel with exceptional bactericidal activity, anti-inflammatory performance and biosafety was prepared, which can significantly accelerate the healing of infected wounds.

title: Nanocatalysis in cathode pre-lithiation for lithium-ion batteries: progress and challenges
abstract: This review focuses on nanocatalysis in cathode pre-lithiation, summarizes recent progress in versatile nanocatalysts for efficient pre-lithiation additive decomposition and highlights future challenges and directions for commercialization.

title: Superstructures of copper nanoclusters as NIR TADF emitters: solvent-dependent optical and morphological modulation
abstract: Self-assembled thiol-templated CuNCs display NIR-TADF emission which can be modulated by solvent, accompanied by remarkable change in the morphology. The electronic structure of the surface ligands can also be used to tailor the properties of CuNCs.

title: DNA-templated fluorescent metal nanoclusters and their illuminating applications
abstract: After the discovery of DNA during the mid-20 th century, a multitude of novel methodologies have surfaced which exploit DNA for its various properties.

title: Interplay between the oxidation process and cytotoxic effects of antimonene nanomaterials
abstract: Study of three antimony-based nanomaterials' behaviour in biological conditions and the influence of the synthesis on their oxidation state and stability in this environment.

title: A silver cluster-assembled material as a matrix for enzyme immobilization towards a highly efficient biocatalyst
abstract: This study reports the designed construction of a novel (3,6)-connected two-dimensional silver cluster-assembled material leveraged, for the first time, as a support matrix for enzyme immobilization.

title: Nanosized Chevrel phases for dendrite-free zinc–ion based energy storage: unraveling the phase transformations
abstract: Nanoscale tailoring of Chevrel phase Mo 6 S 8 unveils its properties as a host for zinc ions. Rapid ion transport, coupled with the emergence of a previously unknown phase, transform the understanding of zinc ion (de)intercalation in these materials.

title: Photosystem I complexes form remarkably stable self-assembled tunneling junctions
abstract: This paper describes large-area molecular tunneling junctions comprising self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) of light-harvesting protein complexes using eutectic Ga–In (EGaIn) as a top contact.

title: Magnetism of metastable γ-Fe 85 Pd 15 nanowire arrays across an unusually broad temperature range (5 K to 800 K)
abstract: Arrays of Fe 85 Pd 15 cylindrical nanowires of the γ-Fe(Pd) fcc phase were magnetically characterized from 5 K to 800 K. Ms is well described by two contributions: spin-glass-like from the surface and ordered core spins.

title: Efficient and stable semitransparent perovskite photovoltaics via a Lewis base incorporation
abstract: Owing to improved crystallinity and low trap-state density and matched energy levels of the perovskite, efficient and stable semitransparent perovskite solar cells were achieved.

title: The effect of water on gold supported chiral graphene nanoribbons: rupture of conjugation by an alternating hydrogenation pattern
abstract: The effect of water on gold-supported chiral graphene nanoribbons has been studied. The results show a spontaneous hydrogenation of the ribbons with a well-defined periodic pattern, even at room temperature and with no other external activation.

title: Dual-color photoluminescence modulation of zero-dimensional hybrid copper halide microcrystals
abstract: Green–red dual emission in hybrid copper halides is controlled by a conducting surface-induced deactivation of the red-emitting state.

title: Single-frequency fiber lasers based on metal thiophosphites with sub-kHz linewidths
abstract: Thiophosphites nanosheets with great optical nonlinearities were employed as saturable absorbers to construct stable single frequency fiber lasers, which have extremely narrow output linewidths and high signal-to-noise ratios.

title: Metal oxide-embedded carbon-based materials for polymer solar cells and X-ray detectors
abstract: This study examines the effects of hybrid nanoparticles made of NiO@rGO (reduced graphene oxide) and NiO@CNT (carbon nanotubes) on PCDTBT and PCBM active layers in glass/ITO/HTL/active-layer/LiF/Al structured bulk heterojunction (BHJ) polymer solar cells (PSCs) and X-ray photodetectors.

title: Triphenylphosphonium-functionalized dimeric BODIPY-based nanoparticles for mitochondria-targeting photodynamic therapy
abstract: This study reports the synthesis, characterization, and photodynamic therapy activity of two orthogonal BODIPY dimers functionalized with TPP cations for mitochondrial targeting.

title: Suppression of surface optical phonon scattering by AlN interfacial layers for mobility enhancement in MoS 2 FETs
abstract: By considerably suppressing surface optical phonon scattering through the insertion of AlN interfacial layers, MoS 2 FETs show enhanced field-effect mobility at room temperature, compared with those using only Al 2 O 3 .

title: Multi-functional GdEu x Tb 1− x O 3 ( x = 0 to 1) nanoparticles: colour tuning optical properties, water proton spin relaxivities, and X-ray attenuation properties
abstract: Multi-functional properties of GdEu x Tb 1− x O 3 @PAA/PDA nanoparticles ( x = 0 to 1), i.e. , colour tuning, high water proton spin relaxivities, and high X-ray attenuation, suitable for various applications, were investigated.

title: Localized three-photon upconversion enhancement in silver nanowire networks and its effect in thermal sensing
abstract: Localized enhancement of upconversion luminescence in silver nanowires allows the direct visualization of plasmonic hot-spots; however, the effect on the emission spectra of Er 3+ ions must be corrected for accurate ratiometric thermometry analysis.

title: Recent advances in bismuth oxychalcogenide nanosheets for sensing applications
abstract: This minireview explores the unique properties and potential applications of bismuth oxychalcogenide nanosheets in chemical and biological sensing, and photodetection.

title: Active site-exposed Bi 2 WO 6 @BiOCl heterostructures for photocatalytic hydrogenation of nitroaromatic compounds
abstract: Active site-exposed Bi 2 WO 6 @BiOCl with a compact Z-scheme heterostructure exhibits enhanced photoactivity towards the hydrogenation reaction of nitroaromatic compounds.

title: 3D microscaffolds with triple-marker sensitive nanoprobes for studying fatty liver disease in vitro
abstract: A triple-marker sensitive nanoprobe-laden 3D in vitro liver disease platform for in situ monitoring of progressive changes in pH and MMP expression.

title: Intracellular self-aggregation of biomimetic Fe 3 O 4 nanoparticles for enhanced ferroptosis-inducing therapy of breast cancer
abstract: A supramolecular and cell membrane co-modified drug delivery system was constructed to enhance Fe 3 O 4 and SAS synergistically induced ferroptosis anti-tumor therapy.

title: Exceptional behavior of a high-temperature superconductor in proximity to a ferromagnet in a bilayer film, La 0.67 Sr 0.33 MnO 3 /YBa 2 Cu 3 O 7
abstract: We studied the electronic properties of a high-temperature superconductor in proximity to a ferromagnetic material in a bilayer film of La 0.67 Sr 0.33 MnO 3 (LSMO)/YBa 2 Cu 3 O 7 (YBCO).

title: Manipulating the interfacial water structure by electron redistribution for the hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Introducing Ni in a NiRu alloy triggers charge transfer from Ni to Ru, creating electron-rich Ru sites. This attracts K + cation-hydrated water molecules with an H-down configuration, improving hydrogen evolution reaction performance.

title: Movable-type printing method to fabricate ternary FeCoNi alloys confined in porous carbon towards oxygen electrocatalysts for rechargeable Zn-air batteries
abstract: FeCoNi confined in nitrogen-doped carbon was fabricated via movable-type printing method, where a range of transition metal- based catalysts could be prepared by the different metal precursors, demonstrating highly activities for ORR and OER.

title: Nanoscale dynamical investigation of the hemoglobin complex with the bacterial protein IsdB: is their interaction stabilized by catch bonds?
abstract: Iron surface determinant B (IsdB) is a surface protein of Staphylococcus aureus that plays essential roles in host cell invasion by mediating both bacterial adhesion and hemic iron acquisition.

title: Automated statistical analysis of raman spectra of nanomaterials
abstract: We present a computational tool to automatically analyze the Raman spectra of nanomaterials. The software manages hundreds of spectra simultaneously providing statistical information of the main spectral features and publication-ready figures.

title: Exploration of structural influences on the ferroelectric switching characteristics of ferroelectric thin-film transistors
abstract: An HZO/IGZO FeFET has been comprehensively analyzed using capacitor test element groups, which showed that the contact region determines the memory window and exhibits an oxygen vacancy-related imprint near the bottom gate and charge trapping.

title: Folic acid-targeted redox responsive polylactic acid-based nanoparticles co-delivering pirarubicin and salinomycin suppress breast cancer tumor growth in vivo
abstract: Schematic illustration of design, administration, and mechanism of action of Pira/Sal dual-loaded FA-conjugated redox responsive [S-(PLA- b -PEG-CONH)] 2 block copolymeric nanoparticles.

title: Nanoparticle induced fusion of lipid membranes
abstract: We found that the optimal fusogenic nanoparticle is able to form a stalk but does not overstabilize it, facilitating the opening of a fusion pore.

title: Controlling Pt nanoparticle sintering by sub-monolayer MgO ALD thin films
abstract: Metal nanoparticle (NP) sintering is a major cause of catalyst deactivation, as NP growth reduces the surface area available for reaction.

title: Advanced strategies for controlling three-phase boundaries in photocatalysis
abstract: This review highlights the crucial role of TPBs in enhancing photocatalytic efficiency for nitrogen, oxygen, and water reduction. It covers recent progress, challenges, and future directions to optimize TPBs for sustainable energy technologies.

title: Carrier-free nano-prodrugs for minimally invasive cancer therapy
abstract: SNC4DC, a dimer of SN-38 derivatives, has been synthesized. SNC4DC nanoparticles called nano-prodrugs are internalized into the cell and dissociate, and the active SN-38 is released after reduction of the S–S bond in the presence of GSH occurs.

title: Molecular-level periodic arrays of long-chain poly(3-hexylthiophene-2,5-diyl) driven by an electric field
abstract: Poly (3-hexylthiophene-2,5-diyl) assemble into monolayers featuring a face-on orientation, extended chain conformation and isolated packing, which is thermodynamically more stable than folded chains in 2D polycrystals.

title: High-performance flexible resistive random-access memory based on SnS 2 quantum dots with a charge trapping/de-trapping effect
abstract: High-quality SnS 2 quantum dots were successfully prepared and applied to a flexible memristor with excellent resistive transition performance.

title: Multiple-polarization-sensitive photodetector Based on a plasmonic metasurface
abstract: By combining monolayer MoSe 2 with plasmonic nanostructure, the photodetector demonstrates a high photocurrent circular dichroism (CD) of 0.35 at 810 nm and a linear polarization (LP) of 0.4 at 633 nm.

title: Nanostructured CeO 2 photocatalysts: optimizing surface chemistry, morphology, and visible-light absorption
abstract: This review highlights the often-misunderstood physics of charge excitation and polaron formation in CeO 2 -based photocatalysts. We review strategies to control CeO 2 morphology, surface chemistry, and visible-light absorption.

title: Nanoscale assembly of enantiomeric supramolecular gels driven by the nature of solvents
abstract: Different co-assembly modes depending on the nature of the solvents observed in multi-component systems with structurally similar components.

title: Imidazolium-based zwitterionic liquid-modified PEG–PLGA nanoparticles as a potential intravenous drug delivery carrier
abstract: Zwitterionic liquid surface-modified PEG–PLGA nanoparticles are developed to explore their potential as intravenous drug delivery carriers via RBC hitchhiking and affinity towards triple-negative breast cancer cells.

title: A natural food-grade supramolecular self-assembly system for creation of hierarchically structured hydrogels
abstract: We develop a hierarchically structured hydrogel by supramolecular self-assembly of all-natural building blocks, glycyrrhizic acid and carrageenan, which can serve as responsive active cargo delivery platforms for food and biomedical applications.

title: Unveiling a robust and high-temperature-stable two-dimensional ZnAl layered double hydroxide nanosheet based flexible triboelectric nanogenerator
abstract: Triboelectric nanogenerators have the ability to harvest low- and mid-frequency vibrational energy from the environment; however, achieving stable performance of the nanogenerator device in high-temperature conditions remains challenging.

title: Immunomodulatory nanoparticles activate cytotoxic T cells for enhancement of the effect of cancer immunotherapy
abstract: This review explores recent advances in the development of cancer immunotherapy using nanoparticles for tumor vaccine, stimulating tumor specific T cells, and delivering immuno-activation and modulatory agents to activate cytotoxic T cells.

title: Regulating magnetic skyrmions in multiferroic monolayer MnOBr
abstract: Two-dimensional multiferroic materials that exhibit both ferroelectricity and ferromagnetism provide a new platform for the discovery and regulation of magnetic skyrmions.

title: Synthesis of RhH-doped Au–Ag alloy nanoclusters and dopant evolution
abstract: Two-step kernel evolution occurs during the transformation of [RhH(AuAg) 24 ] 2− into [Rh(AuAg) 24 ] 1− nanocluster. Hydrogen desorption from the RhH(AuAg) 12 4+ kernel is followed by the kernel oxidation to Rh(AuAg) 12 5+ .

title: In-process monitoring of graphene nanoplatelet production using Raman spectroscopy and NMR relaxation
abstract: In-process measurements of GNP production provide real-time information about material properties, enhancing process control.

title: Biocatalytic ZIF-8 surface-functionalized micromotors navigating in the cerebrospinal fluid: toward Alzheimer management
abstract: In the presence of Cu, ZIF-8@Au@catalase micromotor results in a reduction of speed in cerebrospinal fluid, as an indicator of Alzheimer's disease in just 1 min and using only 1 μL of the sample, becoming a screening tool for its fast diagnosis.

title: Effect of SiO 2 nanofluid concentration on micro-mechanical weakening behavior of coal
abstract: The SiO 2 nanofluids enhance coal wettability by promoting hydrogen bond formation, increasing hydrophilicity and water absorption. This weakens coal cohesion, causing softening.

title: A frogspawn inspired twin Mo 2 C/Ni composite with a conductive fibrous network as a robust bifunctional catalyst for advanced anion exchange membrane electrolyzers
abstract: Inspired by frogspawn, a dual Mo 2 C/Ni encapsulated nanofiber network was constructed (Mo 2 C/NCNTs@Ni) and it exhibits good catalytic activity and durability as a bifunctional catalyst for water splitting.

title: Bamboo-like nitrogen-doped carbon supported chlorine-doped Fe 2 P as an antibacterial oxygen reduction catalyst
abstract: Bio-inspiration and biomimetics offer guidance for designing and synthesizing advanced catalysts for the oxygen reduction reaction (ORR) in microbial fuel cells (MFCs).

title: Impact of channel nanostructures of porous carbon particles on their catalytic performance
abstract: We produce a series of porous carbon particles (PCPs) with controlled channel nanostructures, and systematically investigate the structure–property relationships and catalytic performance of these particles.

title: Liquid crystal-mediated self-assembly of copper nanoclusters with induced circular dichroism and amplified circularly polarized luminescence
abstract: A schematic representation of the incorporation of chiral metal NCs into lyotropic LCs to accomplish chirality transfer to the LC matrix. By adjusting the concentrations of TX-100 and G-SH-Cu NCs, chirality reversal can be effectively regulated.

title: Effect of composition and architecture on the thermodynamic behavior of AuCu nanoparticles
abstract: Atomistic snapshots of AuCu bimetallic nanoparticles with different architectures at 950 K, indicating diverse thermodynamic behaviors despite the same component.

title: Retarding anion exchanges in lead halide perovskite nanocrystals by ligand immobilization
abstract: Plasma treatment causes the crosslinking of passivating molecules around CsPbX 3 (X = Cl − , Br − , I − ) perovskite nanocrystals (PNCs), which retards the anion exchanges.

title: Chiral spin-transfer torque induced by curvature gradient
abstract: A curvature-induced chiral spin-transfer torque (CSTT) is responsible for causing handedness in DW motion along a nanowire with a curvature gradient. The current-driven transport depends on DW chirality and current direction.

title: Z-scheme heterojunction enhanced photocatalytic performance for CO 2 reduction to CH 4
abstract: Due to the high charge separation efficiency leading to high photocatalytic activity, there has been significant interest in enhancing the charge separation ability of photocatalysts by controlling the heterojunction structure.

title: Photo-assisted epitaxial growth from nanoparticles to enhance multi-materialization for advanced surface functionalization
abstract: In this study, we discovered the photo-assisted epitaxial growth from nanoparticles and proposed a strategy for multimaterialization based on rapid crystal growth through the epitaxial growth.

title: Synthesis of tunable thickness-to-diameter ratio microcapsules via a diffusion-controlled process for temperature-responsive release
abstract: A mixed organic solvent is used to control the diffusion of monomers during interfacial polymerization, resulting in the successful preparation of microcapsules with tunable thickness-to-diameter ratios as well as T g .

title: Near-infrared light-boosted antimicrobial activity of minocycline/hyaluronan/carbon nanohorn composite toward peri-implantitis treatments
abstract: A near-infrared responsive composite material (MC/HA/CNH) was developed for the treatment of peri-implantitis. The MC/HA/CNH showed enhanced antimicrobial effect by near-infrared light irradiation, which lasted for 48 hours.

title: Not all silicon quantum dots are equal: photostability of silicon quantum dots with and without a thick amorphous shell
authors: 'I Teng Cheong', 'LiYifan Yang Szepesvari', 'Chuyi Ni', 'Cole Butler', 'Kevin M. O'Connor', 'Riley Hooper', 'Alkiviathes Meldrum', 'Jonathan G. C. Veinot'
abstract: The photostability of ∼5 nm diameter SiQDs with an amorphous shell was compared with that of over-etched SiQDs of equivalent dimensions that bore an a-Si shell of negligible thickness.

title: Electrochemical control of the morphological evolution of PEDOT on a Ni–Co(OH) 2 /carbon cloth surface to modulate the performance of wearable H 2 O 2 sensors
abstract: Electrochemical precise control of the morphology of PEDOT on the surface of the electrode enhances the sensing performance of the sensor.

title: Solvent-induced structural transformation in a one-dimensional coordination polymer
abstract: 1D coordination polymer, termed 1D-DGIST-18, featuring structural flexibility, exhibits hierarchical porosity and turn-on fluorescence upon solvent exchange.

title: Wurtzite vs . rock-salt MnSe epitaxy: electronic and altermagnetic properties
abstract: It is possible to obtain thin films of both the intriguing altermagnetic wurtzite phase of MnSe and more common rock-salt MnSe using molecular beam epitaxy on GaAs substrates.

title: Role of additive size in the segmental dynamics and mechanical properties of cross-linked polymers
abstract: The effects of additive size on the segmental dynamics and mechanical properties of cross-linked polymers are studied, revealing that additive aggregation increases with greater segmental size disparity between cross-linked polymer and additives.

title: Engineering functionalization and properties of graphene quantum dots (GQDs) with controllable synthesis for energy and display applications
abstract: This review systematically covers controllable synthetic methods for functionalization, known as key determinants influencing the properties of GQDs.

title: Light concentration and electron transfer in plasmonic–photonic Ag,Au modified Mo-BiVO 4 inverse opal photoelectrocatalysts
abstract: Synergy of plasmonic–photonic effects in Ag,Au modified Mo-BiVO 4 inverse opal photoelectrocatalysts enhance recalcitrant pharmaceutical degradation.

title: Innovative solvent-free compound-direct synthesis of defect-rich ultra-thin NiS nanosheets for high-performance supercapacitors
abstract: Novel GLNS-NiS nanosheets with excellent electrochemical performance were facilely synthesized by an innovative solvent-free compound-direct reaction strategy.

title: Insights into the mechanical stability of tetrahydrofuran hydrates from experimental, machine learning, and molecular dynamics perspectives
abstract: The multi-scale approaches including experimentation, MD simulations and ML models improve the understanding of hydrate mechanics and provide a predictive framework for estimating the mechanical properties of hydrates under complex conditions.

title: Experimental and simulation study of self-assembly and adsorption of glycerol monooleate in n -dodecane with varying water content onto iron oxide
abstract: The interplay between surfactant reverse-micelle formation and surface adsorption in glycerol monooleate/dodecane/water/iron oxide systems is revealed using neutron reflectometry experiments and molecular dynamics simulations.

title: Light-driven dynamical tuning of the thermal conductivity in ferroelectrics
abstract: Dynamical tuning of the thermal conductivity is critical for the development of novel phononic devices. Here, we show that such a control can be efficiently achieved in a wide class of ferroelectrics by a photoinduced phase transition.

title: A copper missile-triggered power coalescence and death vortex within tumor cell mitochondria for synergistic cuproptosis/phototherapy/chemotherapy
abstract: The CCTH nanoparticles trigger power coalescence and death vortex within tumor cell mitochondria, producing potent anti-tumor efficacy through cuproptosis/phototherapy/chemotherapy synergistic therapy.

title: Atomically precise Au and Ag nanoclusters doped with a single atom as model alloy catalysts
abstract: This minireview focuses on single-atom doping effects on thermal catalysis, electrocatalysis, and photocatalysis of atomically precise gold or silver nanoclusters.

title: Dendritic nanoparticles for immune modulation: a potential next-generation nanocarrier for cancer immunotherapy
abstract: Dendrimers and dendritic NPs are emerging as potential nanoplatforms for cancer immunotherapy. This minireview provides an updated overview of dendrimer conjugates, dendritic hybrids, and dendron-based copolymers for targeted therapy.

title: A high-sensitivity SnSe/Si heterojunction position-sensitive detector for ultra-low power detection
abstract: A large-area uniform SnSe nanorod array film was fabricated to form a SnSe/Si heterostructure PSD to realize high sensitivity and weak light detection.

title: Manipulation of electrochemical properties of MXene electrodes for supercapacitor applications by chemical and magnetic disorder
abstract: The ferromagnetic configuration promotes a significantly high charge transfer and thus, a substantially large capacitance can be achieved by designing the system with both surfaces having ferromagnetic order.

title: Nanoparticle-mediated co-delivery of inflammasome inhibitors provides protection against sepsis
abstract: A dual-drug loaded nanoparticle demonstrates superior inhibition of NLRP3 inflammasome activation and improves the survival rate in a mouse model of septic peritonitis.

title: Electrospinning-derived transition metal/carbon nanofiber composites as electrocatalysts for Zn-air batteries
abstract: The morphology, mechanism and application of TM/CNF composites.

title: A molecular dynamics study on the ion-mediated self-assembly of monolayer-protected nanoclusters
abstract: We studied the effects of metal and molecular cations on the aggregation of atomically precise monolayer-protected nanoclusters (MPCs) in an explicit solvent using atomistic molecular dynamics simulations.

title: Symmetry lowering through surface engineering and improved thermoelectric properties in Janus MXenes
abstract: Increased anharmonicity by lowering of symmetry boosts thermoelectric performances of MXenes.

title: Correction: Recent advances in nano-fertilizers: synthesis, crop yield impact, and economic analysis
abstract: Correction for ‘Recent advances in nano-fertilizers: synthesis, crop yield impact, and economic analysis’ by Badr-Eddine Channab et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, https://doi.org/10.1039/d3nr05012b.

title: Graphene-coated nickel in biological environments: role of structural defects
abstract: Graphene (Gr) is a promising material for addressing microbially induced corrosion (MIC) issues that cause staggering economic losses, estimated at nearly $55 billion annually in the US alone.

title: Localized charge-induced ORR/OER activity in doped fullerenes for Li–air battery applications
abstract: The utility of B/N-doped and BN co-doped fullerenes as cathode materials with reduced overpotentials for Li–air batteries.

title: Surface modification effect on contrast agent efficiency for X-ray based spectral photon-counting scanner/luminescence imaging: from fundamental study to in vivo proof of concept
abstract: X-Ray imaging techniques are among the most widely used modalities in medical imaging and their constant evolution has led to the emergence of new technologies.

title: Protecting Li-metal anode with LiF-enriched solid electrolyte interphase derived from a fluorinated graphene additive
abstract: An LiF-enriched (composition-wise) and organic/inorganic uniform-distributed (structure-wise) SEI film architecture was derived by fluorinated graphene (F-Gr) sacrificial additive to promote efficient Li-metal protection.

title: Probing surface interactions in CdSe quantum dots with thiocyanate ligands
abstract: Our study explores quantum dots, specifically CdSe, using FTIR and 2D-IR spectroscopy. Findings reveal surface passivation of SCN − ions. Combined with computational calculations, these insights impact quantum dots' performance optimization.

title: The dawn of MXene duo: revolutionizing perovskite solar cells with MXenes through computational and experimental methods
abstract: Through increased conductivity, customised surface groups, and mechanical strength, MXene improves perovskite solar cell efficiency and stability. Experimental and computer modelling optimise MXene's addition in PSC layers, guiding future study.

title: ‘When is a hotspot a good nanospot’ – review of analytical and hotspot-dominated surface enhanced Raman spectroscopy nanoplatforms
abstract: Substrate development in surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy (SERS) continues to attract research interest.

title: Graphene and metal–organic framework hybrids for high-performance sensors for lung cancer biomarker detection supported by machine learning augmentation
abstract: Conventional diagnostic methods for lung cancer, based on breath analysis using gas chromatography and mass spectrometry, have limitations for fast screening due to their limited availability, operational complexity, and high cost.

title: Bio-gel nanoarchitectonics in tissue engineering
abstract: Nanoarchitectonics may be highly compatible with applications in biological systems. Construction strategies and functions of bio-gel nanoarchitectonics in medical applications and tissue engineering are discussed.

title: Nanoscale, surface-confined phase separation by electron beam induced oxidation
abstract: Electron-assisted oxidation of Co–Si-based focused electron beam induced deposition (FEBID) materials is shown to form an oxide bilayer with a total thickness of less than 15 nm by phase separation.

title: Near infra-red luminescent osmium labelled gold nanoparticles for cellular imaging and singlet oxygen generation
abstract: Dual modality nanoprobes with enhanced theranostic properties are designed by decoration of gold nanoparticles with osmium complexes for imaging in the near-infrared and singlet oxygen production, leading to new localised therapy approaches.

title: Highly dispersed ultrafine Ru nanoparticles on a honeycomb-like N-doped carbon matrix with modified rectifying contact for enhanced electrochemical hydrogen evolution
abstract: Herein, a Schottky electrocatalyst of Ru NPs@HNC is demonstrated. The rectifying contact between Ru NPs and HNC generates an internal electronic field, enhancing HER performance in alkaline medium.

title: Cell invasive amyloid assemblies from SARS-CoV-2 peptides can form multiple polymorphs with varying neurotoxicity
abstract: Preformed amyloid seeds increase the stability and toxicity of peptide amyloids formed from viral proteins of SARS-CoV-2, these viral protein amyloids may cause some of the neurological symptoms of COVID-19.

title: Co-presence of PtNi nanowires and ionic liquid in carbon mesopores enhances electrocatalytic oxygen reduction activity
abstract: The electrocatalyitc activity of PtNi nanowires for the oxygen reduction reaction is enhanced by the co-presence of ionic liquid in carbon mesopores, confirmed by electrochemical measurements, 3D-TEM tomography and in situ SEIRA spectroscopy

title: Origami nanogap electrodes for reversible nanoparticle trapping
abstract: Drawing inspiration from the time-honored origami technique, we crafted electrodes from a conductive plastic sheet in a single step, enabling the reversible trapping of nanoparticles for analysis within the image plane.

title: Two-dimensional silicene-based technologies in oncology: an emerging avenue
abstract: Silicene-based technologies can be applied in photonic hyperthermia and drug delivery for oncology treatment.

title: Self-assembled viologens on HOPG: solid-state NMR and AFM unravel the location of the anions
abstract: Complete organization of a network of viologens and PF 6 − anions on HOPG is unraveled by combining solid-state NMR and AFM.

title: Polarized Raman spectroscopy study of CVD-grown Cr 2 S 3 flakes: unambiguous identification of phonon modes
abstract: We have unambiguously identified 7 phonon modes by helicity-dependent polarized Raman spectroscopy for CVD-grown 2D Cr 2 S 3 together with variation of temperature.

title: Automatic in situ short-distance deposition of PLGA/PLLA composite nanofibrous membranes for personalized wound dressings
abstract: We demonstrate a self-designed, automatic, short-distance, in situ deposition device based on solution blow spinning (SBS) for wound dressing using PLGA/PLLA/TA composite nanofibrous membranes.

title: Nanoporous air filtering systems made from renewable sources: benefits and challenges
abstract: Nanoporous air filtering systems made from renewable sources offer more sustainable alternatives for filter structures from conventional polymers.

title: An ionic Cu 9 Na 4 -phenylsilsesquioxane/bis(triphenylphosphine)iminium complex: synthesis, unique structure, and catalytic activity
abstract: The first example of a metallasilsesquioxane/phosphazene compound, a Cu 9 Na 4 -based complex, exhibits high catalytic activity in toluene oxidation.

title: Research progress on photocatalytic reduction of CO 2 based on ferroelectric materials
abstract: Transforming CO 2 into renewable fuels or valuable carbon compounds could be a practical means to tackle the issues of global warming and energy crisis.

title: Rationally designed hierarchical hollow CuS/CdIn 2 S 4 heterostructure nanoboxes for boosted photoreduction of CO 2
abstract: A double-shelled CuS/CdIn 2 S 4 photocatalyst was rationally designed using CdIn 2 S 4 sheets in situ grown upon exterior of hollow CuS nanocubes, which exhibits boosted CO yield, significantly improved CH 4 selectivity and excellent catalytic stability.

title: Effect of the Hammett substituent constant of para -substituted benzoic acid on the perovskite/SnO 2 interface passivation in perovskite solar cells
abstract: The positive Hammett substituent constant of para-substituted benzoic acids is found to be more effective than the negative one in passivating the perovskite/SnO 2 interface of perovskite solar cells.

title: All-optical switching based on spatial self-phase modulation of Ag/violet phosphorus heterojunctions
abstract: We have demonstrated that the nonlinear optical effect of Ag/VP heterojunctions is stronger than that of VP Ns using SSPM. Furthermore, we have investigated the physical mechanism and all-optical switching performance of Ag/VP heterojunctions.

title: Layer-dependent ultrafast carrier dynamics of PdSe 2 investigated by photoemission electron microscopy
abstract: This study utilized time- and energy-resolved photoemission electron microscopy with ultrahigh spatial resolution and wide-field real-time detection advantages to reveal the layer-dependent ultrafast dynamics of excited-state electrons in PdSe 2 .

title: Structure dependent activation of a Co molecular catalyst through photoinduced electron transfer from CdTe quantum dots
abstract: Complexes of quantum dots with molecular catalysts are promising building blocks for photo-catalytic applications.

title: Guiding uniform Zn deposition with a multifunctional additive for highly utilized Zn anodes
abstract: As a multifunctional additive for 2 M ZnSO 4 electrolyte, l -leucine with abundant carboxyl and amino groups, exhibits strong interactions with Zn-ion to disrupt its solvation structure, inhibiting the occurrence of side reactions and promoting uniform Zn electrodeposition.

title: Exploring single-entity electrochemistry beyond conventional potential windows: mechanistic insights into hydrazine/hydrazinium ion oxidation
abstract: Single-entity electrochemistry (SEE) enables research into the electrochemical properties of nanoparticles (NPs) at the individual NP level.

title: Short-branched alkyl sulfobetaine-passivated CsPbBr 3 nanocrystals for efficient green light emitting diodes
abstract: Inorganic cesium lead bromide nanocrystals (CsPbBr 3 NCs) hold promising prospects for high performance green light-emitting diodes (LEDs) due to their exceptional color purity and high luminescence efficiency.

title: Ru incorporated into Se vacancy-containing CoSe 2 as an efficient electrocatalyst for alkaline hydrogen evolution
abstract: Through a strategy of Ru doping and introduction of Se vacancies, a Ru-doped CoSe 2 catalyst for alkaline hydrogen precipitation has been developed.

title: Evidence of thickness-dependent surface-induced ferroelectricity in few-layer germanium sulfide obtained via scanning tunneling spectroscopy
abstract: An overview of an STM tip with positive and negative biases, a schematic of the GeS polarization switching, and thickness-dependent STS curves.

title: Synergetic antibacterial nanosheet based on Ti 3 C 2 T x photothermal therapy and cationic polymer to eradicate drug-resistant bacterial biofilms
abstract: The BPG nanosheets, based on Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene and cationic bornyl-guanidine polymers, exhibited broad-spectrum antibacterial activity and antibiofilm ability under near-infrared irradiation.

title: Preparations of spherical nanoparticles of chiral Cinchona alkaloid-based bridged silsesquioxanes and their use in heterogeneous catalysis of enantioselective reactions
abstract: Chiral spherical nanoparticulate organosilica materials containing Cinchona alkaloid derivatives were prepared and used as heterogeneous catalysts of enantioselective dihydroxylation of alkenes and chlorolactonization of 4-arylpent-4-enoic acids.

title: Facile roll-to-roll production of nanoporous fiber coatings for advanced wound care sutures
abstract: A novel electrospinning technique for fast and cost-effective roll-to-roll production of nanofiber-coated sutures with smart drug-releasing capability is introduced, enabling advanced wound care.

title: Hexagonal Mg 2 B 2 and Ca 2 B 2 monolayers as promising anode materials for Li-ion and Na-ion batteries
abstract: Expanding the MBene family to non-transition metal borides, we report two hexagonal MBenes, Mg 2 B 2 and Ca 2 B 2 monolayers, which exhibit excellent overall anode performances as anode materials for Li/Na ions.

title: Ultrathin Ba 0.75 Sr 0.25 TiO 3 nanosheets with highly exposed {001} polar facets for high-performance piezocatalytic application
abstract: This work presents a novel strategy for designing high-performance piezocatalysts and provides new insights for the optimization of the piezocatalytic activity by combining multiple advantages.

title: A facile and scalable fabrication method of scrolled graphene/boron nitride-based van der Waals superlattice heterostructure materials for highly stable supercapacitor electrode application
abstract: The fabricated 1D G/Fe/BN(NS) exhibited an enhanced specific capacitance compared to the G/Fe/BN, with outstanding cycling stability due to the synergistic effects between the enhanced mesopore structure and the heterojunction interfacial area.

title: Visualization of the structural transformation of NiO/YSZ/BZY nanocomposite particles using in situ gas environmental transmission electron microscopy
abstract: In situ gas ETEM reveals the transformation of NiO nanoparticles on the NiO/YSZ/BZY shell into Ni particles during heat treatments in H 2 gas. Ni migrated along composite particle surfaces, causing agglomeration in interparticle spaces.

title: Controlling nanoparticle placement in Au/TiO 2 inverse opal photocatalysts
authors: Jessi E. S. van der Hoeven
abstract: Nanoparticle placement in gold-loaded titania (Au/TiO 2 ) inverse opals impacts photocatalytic activity and stability.

title: Hydrothermal synthesis of metal nanoparticles@hydrogels and statistical evaluation of reaction conditions’ effects on nanoparticle morphologies
abstract: We report a hydrothermal one-pot approach using polysaccharides and salt precursors to synthesize and selectively localize metal nanoparticles in hydrogels, leveraging multivariate regression fits to further evaluate synthesis parameter effects.

title: Sensitive and selective detection of heavy metal ions and organic pollutants with graphene-integrated sensing platforms
abstract: Graphene based sensing platforms offer effective and selective detection of heavy metal ions and organic pollutants in the environment.

title: Liquid exfoliation of a series of expanded layered Cu( ii )-paddlewheel metal–organic frameworks to form nanosheets
abstract: The effects of linker length on nanosheet dimensions and properties are investigated through the ultrasonic liquid exfoliation of an isoreticular series of layered metal–organic frameworks.

title: Benchmarking break-junction techniques: electric and thermoelectric characterization of naphthalenophanes
authors: Herre S. J. van der Zant
abstract: Break-junction techniques provide the possibility to study electric and thermoelectric properties of single-molecule junctions in great detail.

title: Understanding the nanoscale phenomena of nucleation and crystal growth in electrodeposition
abstract: This paper reviews the latest findings on the early stages of electrochemically driven nucleation and crystal growth. A thorough understanding of the associated nanoscale phenomena is crucial for its widespread adoption across various industries.

title: Designing van der Waals magnetic tunnel junctions with high tunnel magnetoresistance via Brillouin zone filtering
abstract: Verified by transport calculations, Brillouin zone filtering is the key to high tunnel magnetoresistance in the van der Waals magnetic tunnel junction, guiding the material selection principle for the application of magnetic memory devices.

title: A magnetically responsive photonic crystal of graphene oxide nanosheets
abstract: In this work, we systematically investigated the magnetically responsive behavior of a photonic crystal consisting of graphene oxide nanosheets and water, leading to the switching of its structural color by applying a magnetic field.

title: The design engineering of nanocatalysts for high power redox flow batteries
abstract: Nanoelectrocatalysts with optimized chemical composition, structure, and integration methods can reduce reaction activation energy, improve electrolyte mass transfer, and thereby enhance the power output of redox flow batteries.

title: Membrane fluidity properties of lipid-coated polylactic acid nanoparticles
abstract: C-Laurdan generalized polarization (GP) measurements were applied to probe the temperature-dependent polarity of hybrid membranes consisting of a lipid monolayer adsorbed onto a polylactic acid (PLA) polymer core as function of lipid composition.

title: Monolayer SnS 2 Schottky barrier field effect transistors: effects of electrodes
abstract: An ML SnS 2 SBFET with an n-type Ohmic-contact 1T-NbTe 2 electrode exhibits remarkably better performance than a device with a Schottky-contact 2H-NbTe 2 electrode and can exceed the IRDS targets for both low-power and high-performance applications.

title: X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy of high-throughput mechanically exfoliated van der Waals materials
abstract: Quantitative elemental analysis of van der Waals materials can be done by XPS by using high-throughput mechanical exfoliations, which favour the coverage of large area substrates with flakes using minimal quantities of starting materials.

title: Woven fabric-based separators with low tortuosity for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: A textile-assisted yarn-alignment method is developed to design separators with low tortuosity, concentrated pore size distribution, and excellent mechanical properties for sodium-ion batteries to achieve fast ion transport.

title: The luminescence modulation of rare earth-doped/containing lead-free double perovskites toward multifunctional applications: a review
abstract: This review summarized the optical performances of RE doped/containing lead-free DPs, which involve the extension of luminescence range, the improvement of PLQY, the realization of multi-mode excitation and multi-color emission.

title: Dual enhancement in strength and ductility of Fe-rich medium-entropy alloys via an in situ formed heterogeneous multi-phase structure
abstract: An exceptional strength–ductility balance is achieved in the brittle Ni 0.6 CoFe 1.4 medium-entropy alloy through in situ formation of a heterogeneous structure with the addition of Si.

title: Band alignment of one-dimensional transition-metal dichalcogenide heterotubes
abstract: One-dimensional (1D) van der Waals (vdW) heterotubes, where different kinds of 1D nanotubes coaxially nest inside each other, offer a flexible platform for promising applications.

title: The support effect on the performance of a MOF-derived Co-based nano-catalyst in Fischer Tropsch synthesis
abstract: The catalyst plays a central role in the Fischer–Tropsch synthesis (FTS) process, and the choice of catalyst support significantly impacts FTS catalyst performance by enhancing its attributes.

title: Advances in biomimetic AIE nanoparticles for diagnosis and phototherapy
abstract: An overview of the development of biomimetic AIE nanoparticles and their biomedical applications.

title: Multifunctional siRNA/ferrocene/cyclodextrin nanoparticles for enhanced chemodynamic cancer therapy
abstract: A supramolecular nanoformulation for the co-delivery of MTH1 siRNA and a Fenton reagent into a cancer cell is reported.

title: Composition-dependent morphologies of CeO 2 nanoparticles in the presence of Co-adsorbed H 2 O and CO 2 : a density functional theory study
abstract: A theoretical framework for analysing surface speciation and predicting the thermodynamically stable particle morphologies accessible under a set of environmental conditions. This is applied to cerium dioxide but can be used on other materials.

title: Hydrogen bonding-regulated miscibility of graphene oxide and nonionic water-soluble polymers
abstract: The miscibility between GO and nonionic water-soluble polymers is regulated by hydrogen bonding, and preheat mixing is proposed to prepare homogeneous mixed solution and composite papers with a renewal high performance trend.

title: Configurable swellability of hydrogel microstructure for structural-color-based imaging concealment/encryption
abstract: Multi-stage MHM nanocavities with configurably swellable hydrogel interlayers are achieved through high-resolution electron-beam exposure, resulting in structural-color-based imaging concealment/encryption driven by humidity change.

title: Photoluminescence modal splitting via strong coupling in hybrid Au/WS 2 /GaP nanoparticle-on-mirror cavities
abstract: By integrating dielectric and metallic components, hybrid nanophotonic devices present promising opportunities for manipulating nanoscale light–matter interactions and achieving strong light-matter coupling regime with atomically thin semiconductors.

title: An MgO passivation layer and hydrotalcite derived spinel Co 2 AlO 4 synergically promote photoelectrochemical water oxidation conducted using BiVO 4 -based photoanodes
abstract: The synergistic effect of MgO passivation layer and spinel Co 2 AlO 4 inhibits the rapid recombination of photogenerated carriers in BiVO 4 , accelerates the water oxidation reaction, and improves the PEC performance.

title: Evidence of oxygen vacancy-mediated ultrahigh SERS sensitivity of Niobium pentoxide nanoparticles through defect engineering: theoretical and experimental studies
abstract: Oxygen vacancy engineering in Nb 2 O 5 nanoparticles enables high SERS sensitivity through defect mediated charge transfer and electromagnetic enhancement.

title: Dual metal centers within a water-stable Co/Ni bimetallic metal-triazolate framework contribute to durable photocatalysis for water treatment
abstract: A bimetallic CoNi-MET with fixed metal occupancy combines the strong water resistance of Co-MET with the high porosity of a Ni-MET.

title: Catalytic NH 3 oxidation affected by the nanometric roughness of the platinum overlayer
abstract: The nanometric smoothness of the Pt overlayer achieved more than 10-fold greater TOF compared to the rough Pt surface.

title: Efficient CO 2 electroreduction to ethanol enabled by tip-curvature-induced local electric fields
abstract: A CuO nanoflower catalyst with tip curvature exhibited remarkable performance toward CO 2 to ethanol conversion. The exceptional ethanol selectivity was attributed to the enhanced *OH adsorption due to the tip-curvature-induced local electric fields.

title: Nanorod structure tuning and defect engineering of MoO x for high-performance SERS substrates
abstract: In recent years, surface-enhanced Raman scattering (SERS) based on metal oxide semiconductors has been an active area of research and development, attracting significant scientific interest.

title: Understanding and tuning magnetism in van der Waals-type metal thiophosphates
abstract: This article provides a timely review on engineering the magnetic properties of vdW-type metal thiophosphates material MPX 3 through X substitutions and inter-layer intercalation.

title: Tuning the performance of Fe–porphyrin aerogel-based PGM-free oxygen reduction reaction catalysts in proton exchange membrane fuel cells
abstract: The distance between active sites in Fe–N–C ORR catalysts can be fine-controlled in aerogel-based frameworks, and greatly affect their performance in proton exchange membrane fuel cells.

title: Substrate-induced phase transition within liquid condensates reverses the catalytic activity of nanoparticles
abstract: Substrate concentration induced modulation of reaction rates inside catalytic condensates.

title: Interfacial engineering of Ni–Co–Mn@Ni nanosheet–nanocone arrays as high performance non-noble metal electrocatalysts for hydrogen generation
abstract: The electrochemical hydrogen production from water splitting is a promising strategy for obtaining renewable energy sources and replacing fossil fuels.

title: Correction: High-performance p–i–n perovskite photodetectors and image sensors with long-term operational stability enabled by a corrosion-resistant titanium nitride back electrode
abstract: Correction for ‘High-performance p–i–n perovskite photodetectors and image sensors with long-term operational stability enabled by a corrosion-resistant titanium nitride back electrode’ by Tian Sun et al. , Nanoscale , 2023, 15 , 7803–7811, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3NR00410D.

title: Rapidly synthesized porous alloy heterostructure catalysts for ultra-low energy water splitting under industrial conditions
abstract: We created porous NiFe@RN bifunctional catalysts via one-step electrodeposition on RANEY® nickel. These catalysts show excellent HER and OER activity, enabling significant energy savings in large-scale hydrogen production.

title: A high-performance broadband phototransistor array of a PdSe 2 /SOI Schottky junction
abstract: We demonstrate a phototransistor based on a PdSe 2 /Si heterojunction on a SOI substrate, which exhibits high photoelectric performance. The photoelectric performance of the device can be further improved under gate voltage regulation.

title: Migration of zeolite-encapsulated subnanometre platinum clusters via reactive neural network potentials
abstract: Pt atoms and small clusters move through the zeolite framework via distinct mechanisms, leading to complex size-dependent diffusivity.

title: White light and near-infrared emissions of Pr 3+ ions in SiO 2 -LaF 3 sol–gel nano-glass-ceramics
abstract: White light and NIR luminescence were obtained for oxyfluoride nano-glass-ceramics doped with Pr 3+ ions prepared by the sol–gel method as an interesting alternative for well-known Pr 3+ -doped materials fabricated by the melt-quenching technique.

title: Smart building block with colored radiative cooling devices and quantum dot light emitting diodes
abstract: The smart light-emitting building block integrated with colored radiative cooling devices effectively dissipates heat generated during the operation of CdZnSeS/ZnS quantum dot light-emitting diodes.

title: Lithium intercalation in two-dimensional penta-NiN 2 : insights from NiN 2 /NiN 2 homostructure and G/NiN 2 heterostructure
abstract: First-principles calculations are employed to investigate the intercalation of lithium atoms in the interlayer gap of nitrogen-rich 2D bilayer NiN 2 for anode applications.

title: Pt and black phosphorus co-modified flower-like WS 2 composites for fast NO 2 gas detection at low temperature
abstract: Pt and BP co-modified WS 2 sensors were used for the first time to recognize ppb-level NO 2 at a low temperature of 50 °C. The sensor achieves satisfactory repeatability, superior selectivity, and fast response with negligible baseline drift.

title: Electron repulsion tuned electronic structure of TiO 2 by fluorination for efficient and selective photocatalytic ammonia generation
abstract: Through the regulation of electronic structure and optimization of hydrophobicity, two types of fluorine modification (fluorine doping and surface fluorination) have respectively enhanced nitrogen activation and selectivity during nitrogen fixation.

title: Laser-synthesized plasmonic HfN-based nanoparticles as a novel multifunctional agent for photothermal therapy
abstract: HfN nanoparticles exhibiting a tunable plasmonic feature in the near-IR were synthesized by laser ablation in liquids. A strong photothermal therapeutic effect yielding 100% cells death under 808 nm irradiation of nanoparticles was reported.

title: Metal-assisted chemical etching beyond Si: applications to III–V compounds and wide-bandgap semiconductors
abstract: Metal-assisted chemical etching of next-generation materials is catalogued in this exciting review showcasing device fabrication and successful process recipes.

title: Biotin-functionalized nanoparticles: an overview of recent trends in cancer detection
abstract: Biotin-functionalized nanoparticles enhance cancer detection by targeting biotin receptors, which are overexpressed on cancer cells. This targeted approach improves imaging accuracy and efficacy in identifying cancerous tissues.

title: Cell membrane coated nanoparticles as a biomimetic drug delivery platform for enhancing cancer immunotherapy
abstract: Graphical abstract of CMNPs delivering different therapeutic agents for enhancing cancer immunotherapy.
