title: In situ topotactic formation of an inorganic intergrowth bulk NiS/FeS@MgFe-LDH heterojunction to simulate CODH for the photocatalytic reduction of CO 2
abstract: By using of the structural memory effect of LDHs, an inorganic intergrowth bulk heterojunction NiS/FeS@MgFe-LDHs mimic active C-clusters [Ni–4Fe–4S] in Carbon monoxide dehydrogenase, in which unique photogenerated electron transfer channel encouraged carrier separation.

title: Rhombohedrally stacked layered transition metal dichalcogenides and their electrocatalytic applications
abstract: Layered transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDCs) are extensively investigated as catalyst materials for a wide range of electrochemical applications due to their high surface area and versatile electronic and chemical properties.

title: Polyetherimide (PEI) nanocomposite with WS 2 nanotubes
abstract: Nanocomposite fibers of polyetherimide with axially aligned WS 2 nanotubes were synthesized and studied. Increased nanotubes content notably enhanced strain hardening. Post-strain analysis unveiled nanocrystalline film surrounding the WS 2 nanotubes.

title: Ultrafast switching to zero field topological spin textures in ferrimagnetic TbFeCo films
abstract: Zero-field multiple topological spin textures are generated upon fs laser excitation on the saturated state of amorphous ferrimagnetic TbFeCo films due to an ultrafast nucleation mechanism.

title: Correction: Ultrafast switching to zero field topological spin textures in ferrimagnetic TbFeCo films
abstract: Correction for ‘Ultrafast switching to zero field topological spin textures in ferrimagnetic TbFeCo films’ by Kaixin Zhu et al. , Nanoscale , 2024, 16 , 3133–3143, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3NR04529C.

title: Nanoplasmonic sensing to study CO and oxygen adsorption and CO oxidation on size-selected Pt 10 clusters
abstract: The adsorption of CO and oxygen and CO oxidation on size-selected Pt 10 clusters were studied by indirect nanoplasmonic sensing (INPS) in the pressure range of 1–100 Pa at T = 418 K.

title: Enhanced catalytic performance through a single-atom preparation approach: a review on ruthenium-based catalysts
abstract: The outstanding catalytic properties of single-atom catalysts (SACs) stem from the maximum atom utilization and unique quantum size effects, leading to ever-increasing research interest in SACs in recent years.

title: An insight, at the atomic level, into the structure and catalytic properties of the isomers of the Cu 22 cluster
abstract: The structural isomers of Cu 22 exhibit significant differences in the copper-catalyzed [3 + 2] azide-alkyne cycloaddition reaction (CuAAC), which offers a valuable opportunity to investigate the structure-function relationship of copper nanoclusters.

title: Insight into the intrinsic activity of various transition metal sulfides for efficient hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: The electrocatalytic hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) is an efficient approach to convert sustainable energy sources into clean energy carriers, H 2 .

title: Two-dimensional ice-like water adlayers on a mica surface with and without a graphene coating under ambient conditions
abstract: Ab initio molecular dynamics simulations show that a previously unreported two-dimensional ice-like water adlayer structure, termed Ice-AL-II, can form on mica surfaces under ambient conditions.

title: Microwave-assisted ZnO-decorated carbon urchin resembling ‘shish-kebab’ morphology with self-healing and enhanced electromagnetic shielding properties
abstract: Herein, inspired by Acacia auriculiformis fruit, the shish-kebab-like growth of ZnO on carbon urchin (ZnO@CU) was designed using microwave radiation, thus leading to a hierarchal 3D structure that can promote multiple internal reflections through polarization centers.

title: Solution-phase synthesis of alloyed Ba(Zr 1− x Ti x )S 3 perovskite and non-perovskite nanomaterials
abstract: Alloyed Ba(Zr 1− x Ti x )S 3 nanoparticles are prepared via a solution-phase route. The phase evolution from a chalcogenide perovskite phase at low x to a hexagonal non-perovskite phase at high x is tracked along with changes in the optical properties.

title: Fabrication and assembly of supercapacitors based on Ni-based MOFs and their derivative materials for enhancing their electrochemical performances
abstract: A series of nickel-based materials were derived from two different Ni-MOFs by calcination, and the researchers investigated the structural and electrochemical properties of these materials in detail.

title: Synthesis and properties of phosphorus nanostructures on Au(110)
abstract: Phosphorus nanostructures are synthesized on an Au(110) substrate via molecular beam epitaxy. We obtain self-assembled phosphorus dimers at low coverage and long-range phosphorus nanoribbons at higher coverage. Both present metallic character.

title: In situ heating characterization of structural evolution and size-dependent melting point depression in gold nanoclusters: a comprehensive thermodynamic investigation
abstract: The investigation of nanocluster behaviors at elevated temperatures is important because it encompasses temperature-dependent structural evolution and size-dependent melting points.

title: Theoretical and experimental investigations of enhanced carbon nanotube-gold interface conductivity through nitrogen doping
abstract: With the introduction of nitrogen, the Schottky barrier and contact resistance between the carbon nanotubes and the gold electrode are reduced, and the device performance is improved.

title: Variable gain DNA nanostructure charge amplifiers for biosensing
abstract: DNA origami interfaced with electrical readout allowed a drastic charge amplification of ≈2 × 10 4 that can be tuned with an applied DC bias. The modularity and reusability of this approach will allow flexible and multiplexed biosensing.

title: Nanoparticle-enabled integration of air capture and conversion of CO 2
abstract: This work demonstrates a nanoparticle-enabled integration of air capture and conversion of CO 2 . Ambient CO 2 is captured in a KOH–ethylene glycol solution and then selectively reduced to formate under 50 °C and ambient pressure using Pd NPs.

title: Stable “snow lantern-like” aggregates of silicon nanoparticles suitable as a drug delivery platform
abstract: Porous silicon nanoparticles produced by controlled aggregation of smaller primary particles in the centrifugal Chemical Vapor Deposition (cCVD) process were found to have several beneficial properties for use as a versatile drug delivery system.

title: Research progress of dual-atom site catalysts for photocatalysis
abstract: Herein, the recent progress of DASCs in the field of photocatalytic conversion of small molecules is reviewed, including controllable preparation and characterization, reaction mechanisms and the relationship between their structure and activity.

title: On the origin of the improved hydrogen evolution reaction in Mn- and Co-doped MoS 2
abstract: MoS 2 films doped with Mn and Co metal cations show better HER properties. Using in situ resonant photoemission spectroscopy, we obtained direct evidence of the Mn and Co effects on the electronic properties in improving MoS 2 functionality.

title: Temperature dependence of the electron and hole Landé g -factors in CsPbI 3 nanocrystals embedded in a glass matrix
abstract: Coherent spin dynamics of carriers in CsPbI 3 perovskite nanocrystals embedded in a glass matrix are studied by the time-resolved Faraday ellipticity. Nanocrystal size and temperature dependencies of the electron and hole g -factors are measured.

title: Polyoxometalate nanocluster-infused triple IPN hydrogels for excellent microplastic removal from contaminated water: detection, photodegradation, and upcycling
abstract: Microplastic (MP) pollution threatens ecosystems globally, necessitating urgent environmental intervention. This article proposes a solution for combating MP pollution in water by an innovative hydrogel synthesis and circular economy-based upcycling.

title: Atomistic understanding of enhanced selectivity in photocatalytic oxidation of benzyl alcohol to benzaldehyde using graphitic carbon nitride loaded with single copper atoms
abstract: The enhanced selective oxidation of benzyl alcohol to benzaldehyde under visible light is achieved through atomically precise control of the coordination structure of single copper atoms on graphitic carbon nitride.

title: Energy-efficient resistive switching synaptic devices based on patterned Ag nanotriangles with tunable gaps fabricated using plasma-assisted nanosphere lithography
abstract: Devices composed of planar silver nanotriangles with tunable sharp features are fabricated through nanosphere lithography resulting in localized electric field thereby minimizing the threshold switching voltage for synaptic functionalities.

title: Perspectives on sustainable and efficient routes of nanoparticle synthesis: an exhaustive review on conventional and microplasma-assisted techniques
abstract: A comparative summary of conventional and microplasma-based nanoparticle synthesis.

title: NiCo-MOFs in situ anchored on graphdiyne with metal-like properties form a strongly coupled electron transport interface and construct an ohmic contact to achieve efficient charge–hole spatial separation
abstract: Metals exhibit unique characteristics in photocatalysis, and their incorporation into semiconductors can result in remarkable features.

title: Recent advances on applications of single-walled carbon nanotubes as cutting-edge optical nanosensors for biosensing technologies
abstract: Single-walled carbon nanotubes (SWCNTs) possess outstanding photophysical properties which has garnered interest towards utilizing these materials for biosensing and imaging applications.

title: Ferroelectric modulation of CuCo 2 O 4 nanorods for controllable alkaline water electrolysis
abstract: Polarized CuCo 2 O 4 nanorods exhibit excellent electrocatalytic water-splitting performance due to their large surface areas and fast electron transfer.

title: Structural and optical control through anion and cation exchange processes for Sn-halide perovskite nanostructures
abstract: Metal halide perovskite nanostructures, characterized by their ionic nature, present a compelling avenue for the tunability of dimensions and band gaps through compositional transformations involving both cationic and anionic exchange reactions.

title: Photothermally driven decoupling of gas evolution at the solid–liquid interface for boosted photocatalytic hydrogen production
abstract: Schematic diagram of a gas–solid biphase system for photocatalytic water splitting.
